statement_to_voters:
I live in Aberdeenshire with my family but spent most of my life in vibrant cities. I am deeply passionate about the region’s culture, history, and natural beauty and the deep links between Aberdeen and the rest of the North East. I am committed to build a fairer Scotland.

I will prioritise education, strong ties to Europe, and a sustainable future for the North-east.

My constituents know me as their “hands-on Councillor” - just as ready to help unblock a drain as to campaign for a fairer deal from the Scottish Government for our Councils.

Working with communities has been at the heart of my decades of volunteering work and inspired my political engagement. For the last decade, I have been volunteering in Aberdeen Central and created strong links with the third sector and business community.

I was born in Taiwan and raised in Brussels, now I am working and volunteering in the City and Shire. This gives me a unique perspective blending local insight with knowledge of how things can be done differently to make the North East a more compassionate and more prosperous place to live.

For media enquiries: media@scotlibdems.org.uk

James Wyllie is Emeritus Professor in International Relations and Strategic Studies at the University of Aberdeen.

He is from a farming family in Dumfriesshire, and he is a graduate of the universities of Stirling and Lancaster.

Between 1979 and 2021 he was Director of postgraduate Strategic Studies at the University of Aberdeen.

Previously, he was a Research Officer in the Defence Intelligence Staff in the UK Ministry of Defence, Teaching Fellow in Politics at Durham University, and Lecturer at the University of East Anglia before joining the Department of Politics and International Relations at Aberdeen.

In 1988 he was a Commonwealth Research Fellow in Strategic Studies at the University of Calgary and from 2012–2015 he was a Visiting Scholar at the German Council on Foreign Relations, Berlin.

He has published widely on international security, and for many years he was a regular contributor to television and radio news and current affairs programmes in the UK and abroad.

He was also a frequent consultant to the UK Ministry of Defence and the Foreign and Commonwealth Office, and some overseas governments.

I was elected as an Aberdeenshire Councillor in May 2022, representing North Kincardine. I've lived in Portlethen since the last millennium – moving here just before graduating from the University of Aberdeen with a degree in Economic Science. My husband is a Scout leader here and our four children attend/ed local schools.

I believe we all share responsibility for making our communities the best place they can be. I’ve contributed my time and energy whenever I’ve been able - from pre-parenthood days in sport, through to school committees, community organisations, and now as a volunteer with the charity Home-Start Aberdeen.

I am standing to be an MSP because I am tired and frustrated with the way the SNP government prioritises 'nice to have' projects over getting the essentials right. There is only so much I, as a councillor, can do, when so much of the council's budget and activities are dictated from Holyrood. Scotland needs fresh leadership and change with fairness at its heart, from people who genuinely care about their communities.

For media enquiries: media@scotlibdems.org.uk

Scotland deserves change, give me an opportunity to be that change.
I want the people of Scotland to be wealthier and healthier, this can be achieved through Independence.
Let's keep the globalists out of Scottish politics.
I never intended to get involved in politics but it's time for real people to step up.
#Independencenothingless

I care deeply about Aberdeen. It’s where I was born, where I grew up and where I live today. I have spent my whole career in the hospitality industry. My husband Harry and I opened Pappagallos Café on Holburn Street in 1990, and we have gone on to run restaurants and holiday accommodation in Ballater and Pennan.
I have been a member of Reform UK since 2024, and I am the Area Organiser for Aberdeen City and Kincardine.
I have always been passionate about serving my community and for 26 years I was a member and Chair of the Childrens’ Panels in Aberdeen City and Aberdeenshire. I have also been a longstanding volunteer in my local churches.
I’m not a career politician. I am someone who has experience of life, knows the challenges of running a small business and has spent many years serving their community as a volunteer.
Aberdeen Donside needs and deserves stronger representation, and I can deliver that.

Michael Turvey is a father of three, an oil and gas worker with 19 years experience, a volunteer and president of a local charity. He is passionate about the energy industry, empowering young people and improving lives in the North East.

His priorities for the Scottish Parliament are:
- delivering more effective NHS and care services,
- reducing the cost of living,
- supporting our oil and gas workers,
- bringing Scottish education back to its best; and
- fairer funding for the North East.

Michael has recently helped re-write the Liberal Democrats policy on Oil and Gas Taxation to support a long term, predictable regime that supports investment.

In Spring 2026, he helped pass policy motions supporting redeployment of health resources to preventative medicine and to care services to release hospital beds.  The motion also opposed the SNP’s GP “walk-in centres” which will draw finite resource away from communities across the North East.

Michael has also lead calls and a policy change to support funding for local bridge maintenance.

Michael is the Liberal Democrat candidate for Aberdeen Donside and residents across the North East can also vote for him on their peach regional ballot.

For media enquiries: media@scotlibdems.org.uk

David grew up in Newburgh and loves the communities that make up Aberdeenshire East.

The whole region has been let down, by both Labour and Conservative Governments in Westminster and the SNP Government in Holyrood. Jobs in our energy sector are being lost, and our public services are creaking under the pressure of chronic under funding.

With a decade of experience in campaigns, David has worked to protect energy sector jobs in the North East, fought for funding for our struggling NHS, and stood to protect foundational British industries.

Speaking to people right across the constituency, David understands that you feel let down and left behind.

For media enquiries: media@scotlibdems.org.uk

It is an honour to be the candidate for West Aberdeenshire and Kincardine. The North East has been my home since 2005, my roots are here and it is where I have chosen to bring up my family.

I was elected as a local councillor in 2022 and since then have led on education for the Labour group and campaigned for children to be able to walk and cycle safely to school. I was motivated to stand as a councillor because of my concern for the world that my children and other children were growing up in. The impact of climate change is apparent in the north east as elsewhere and I want my children to grow up in a greener, fairer Britain than we have today.

I have worked in further and higher education for over twenty years. Our young people are being failed by broken promises from both governments at Westminster and Holyrood. Broken promises on class sizes, teacher numbers, attainment, nursery places, I could go on. Our FE colleges are in crisis and our universities are suffering from a completely broken funding model.

I hope to play a part in helping a Labour Government to make a difference to our communities here in West Aberdeenshire and Kincardine. If you live in the constituency and have any questions or thoughts please do get in touch.

I live in Aberdeenshire, am a former nurse and midwife and have dedicated much of my life to caring about others and helping with the well-being of my community. As a health and wellness coach, I continue to help individuals’ lead healthier lives.

I am a proud mother of two and my journey into politics is motivated by a deep-seated belief that the current political leaders no longer serve the true interests of the people. I had no previous inclination towards politics but decided that we can all moan about it but nothing changes if we don’t take action.  So my candidacy is a commitment to honesty, transparency, and genuine representation.

Why Am I Standing for Reform

I grew up on a farm and am passionate about UK farming and fishing, recognizing these industries are vital to the nation’s heritage and economy.  A country cannot be self-sufficient if it cannot feed itself and our farmers are struggling as the Net Zero regulations clamp down on them.  The price of red diesel has been increased, farmers are being paid to re-wild their land thus reducing the number of crop producing fields and now with the closure of Britain’s only ammonia plant, there will be a huge increase in fertiliser costs.  It is Reform UK policy to increase the farming budget to £3 Billion with a focus on smaller farms, to keep farmland in use to produce food and to bring young people into farming. WE want to help our farmers to carry on farming. It is also another Reform UK policy to renegotiate our fishing arrangements with EU and help our fishing communities build back the communities and heritage.

I want to help empower local communities and be a voice on their behalf and bring about real change, restoring trust in political leadership and fostering a brighter future for all.

Ed Thornley is the Liberal Democrat candidate for Airdrie. A former Scout Leader and Duke of Edinburgh's award co-ordinator, Ed is passionate about volunteering, and making sure young people get the opportunities in life which allow them to flourish.

Ed has worked in campaigns and communications, leading teams of volunteers, and freelance work providing artworking services for online and print media.

For media enquiries: media@scotlibdems.org.uk

Caron Lindsay has lived in Livingston for 24 years. She was the Liberal Democrat candidate for Almond Valley in the Scottish Parliamentary elections in 2021 and for Livingston in the UK General Election in 2024.

She is currently the President of the Scottish Liberal Democrats and spokesperson for Social Security.

In her day job, she helps people with all sorts of problems, from healthcare to social security to housing.

Caron wants to see a society where everyone is free to live the best life that they can. She believes that too many are held back by poverty, lack of decent housing and ill health and that needs to change. She wants to see better support for unpaid carers. She is particularly concerned that so many young people are waiting for years to be diagnosed with ADHD and Autism. If elected, these will be her priorities.

For media enquiries: media@scotlibdems.org.uk

Lloyd Melville currently serves as an SNP councillor for Monifieth and Sidlaw on Angus Council, having been elected in May 2022. Born in Perth, and raised near Duntrune, he was until recently a council tenant in Monifieth; giving him first hand insight into local housing challenges.

On the Council, Lloyd has served as Vice-Convener of the Family, Education and Justice Committee, and Vice-Convener of the Angus Licensing Board. He led the creation of Angus Council’s new housing committee, drawing on personal and casework experience to drive improvement for tenants. He has been a prominent advocate for residents affected by RAAC, working constructively across party lines to secure solutions.

His priorities as a councillor include securing more investment in local schools, improving housing standards, repairing roads and protecting essential services from budget cuts. Lloyd has also championed action on climate change, backing investment in local initiatives, and supported community-led projects across Angus.

Before entering elected office, Lloyd worked across a range of sectors including theatre, food service and retail, where he served as a Customer Services Manager with Tesco. He holds a degree in History and Politics from the University of Stirling, having benefited from the SNP Government’s policy of free university tuition.

After being elected to Angus Council, Lloyd joined Dave Doogan MP’s team, working in both the constituency and at Westminster, and now supports Graeme Dey MSP, leading on community outreach.

I am your Reform UK Candidate for Angus South, great farming country. I was born and brought
up on a farm and went to the Agricultural College.

My first job was up Glenisla. Even as a young man I could see the effects of Government interference and soon I wanted something else.

I joined the Army and trained as a surveyor, travelling the World extensively. This gave me a much broader perspective on life and experienced how other parts of the World manage their
affairs.

When I retired from the Military as a Regimental Sergeant Major I retrained to enter the London Finance Sector. After a period I set up my own business in Manchester, which I later sold. On taking early retirement I returned to Scotland and built my own home.

I have always been interested in politics and when I look at the Arbroath High St, it made me search for a political party that had the courage to do something about it rather than wringing their hands as they watch miserable decline, in health, wealth and education, all of which are
devolved to the Scottish Parliament.

That is why I chose to stand for Reform UK, they have the
courage and vision to Reform and I get things done.

It's time for a change. Scotland desperately needs a change of direction. We’ve now seen years of managed decline, dead-ends and unfulfilled promises from the SNP, perhaps unsurprising given that they originally existed as a single-issue fringe pressure group and were never intended or set up to govern. Even longstanding SNP voters and supporters are abandoning the party in droves, as demonstrated by Labour’s landslide win in the 2024 UK general election, and numerous polls and by-elections since. they need and want something else. They deserve a new, fresh, dynamic Scottish Government centred on meeting their needs and delivering their desires for a future Scotland – the vision of a strong, safe, prosperous nation within a family of nations working together for universal gain. This is so essential, in an increasingly fractured world where it is clear that more than ever before, together we will stand and divided we will fall. Only Scottish Labour can and will deliver that new direction in government.

Callum is a resident doctor, currently based in the Oban and Lorn area, with experience working both in primary care and in hospitals, especially in remote and rural areas like much of our constituency. He's seen the day-to-day reality for people dealing with the NHS whilst working on the frontline of healthcare, as well as the human end result of social and economic decline and mismanagement. He's a proven leader, campaigner and shaper of policy, having worked at a Scotland-wide and UK-wide level with the British Medical Association and the Royal College of General Practitioners, as an elected representative, committee chair and conference chair on various occasions. Before his career in medicine, he worked in transport and complex operations management for a large logistics and security provider, with a knack for continuous improvement. You may have also heard him on local radio! (he's one of the trustees of Argyll FM).
Callum is committed to the ideals of democratic socialism, co-operation, progressive reform and evidence-based policy, whilst grounded in local community and common sense.

Alan Reid was the Lib Dem MP for Argyll and Bute between 2001 and 2015,  while in Parliament he served on the Scottish Affairs Committee.

He served as councillor for the Cowal Ward on Argyll & Bute Council for 5 years. Before being elected to Parliament he worked as a computer systems analyst.

Scottish Liberal Democrat candidate for Argyll & Bute Alan Reid said:

“Over the past nineteen years, the SNP have squandered every chance to deliver for Argyll & Bute.

“I want to get our communities moving again: fixing the Rest and Be Thankful, ending the ferries scandal, restoring NHS dentistry and getting people GP and hospital appointments when they need them.

“I can’t stand by and watch the SNP unleash more failures for Argyll & Bute.

“This election needs to be about change with fairness at its heart. Only the Scottish Liberal Democrats have a realistic plan to deliver it.”

My parents taught me the importance of paying attention to detail, to question everything, and to learn from mistakes and experiences. They instilled in me values of respect, fairness, politeness, tolerance and honesty.

I attended Mainholm Academy for 6 years which I enjoyed immensely.  I spent the first part of my career in the Navy serving on frigates and later submarines. I then went on to spend 20 years in the offshore oil and gas sector developing and maintaining subsea infrastructure aboard various vessels. This is one of the reasons I’m keen on promoting Reform UK policies for more technical jobs and to help businesses because they all provide valued jobs and are good for the economy, benefitting us all.

I joined Reform UK as I thought mainstream parties had gone off the rails and lost sight of what most of us are feeling and are no longer representing our views.  Reform UK will deal with mad net zero policies which are too stringent, stifling growth, making us less competitive and causing prices to rocket upwards. Immigration is out of control in an already full country with housing shortages. There are NHS concerns with over-the-top management and waste and an inability to retain staff including carers. We need more affordable housing to ease rents and waiting lists, more Police to police without political interference and fair justice, not this two-tier system which we have all seen emerging.

Help me to help you. Vote Reform UK on Thursday 16 October 2025 and let’s bring common sense back into politics.

A message from Denise Sommerville

In this election, you have a choice—not just between parties, but about the kind of representation you want.

You have two votes. One for a constituency candidate, and one for the regional list. And with both of those votes, you can choose to back someone who is not tied to party politics.

I am standing as an independent candidate for Ayr, and on the South Scotland regional list, because I believe our communities deserve a voice that answers to people—not parties.

For too long, decisions have been shaped by party lines, internal agendas, and systems that feel distant from everyday life. I’m standing to change that.

I come from this community. I understand the pressures families are under—the housing crisis, the impact of poor decisions and wasted public money, and the reality of a public health system struggling to respond to addiction.

My commitment is simple:
	•	Put Ayr, Prestwick and Troon first
	•	Challenge waste and demand accountability
	•	Push for real, joined-up solutions to the addiction crisis

If you choose to vote for me, you are choosing independent representation—someone who will speak up, question decisions, and stay accountable to you.

This election, you don’t have to follow party lines.

You can choose a representative who stands with you.

Denise Sommerville
Independent Candidate – Ayr Constituency & South Scotland Region

John (Gus) is a mature businessman, and serves as the party's Nominating Officer.

Raised on a family farm in Ayrshire and educated in Glasgow, he has had an extensive and varied career. After working in the industrial supply sector, his career took him overseas to live and work for many years, providing international export and insurance services before returning to Scotland to be nearer family.

He is a veteran political activist and seasoned election candidate, having previously stood and campaigned for UKIP at Council, Holyrood and United Kingdom general elections.

Hi, I’m David McLennan, your Reform UK candidate for the Bathgate Constituency and I live in the constituency area, near Seafield.

I won the Council election in Whitburn and East Blackburn in December last year, and am now working full-time as Reform’s first-ever elected Councillor in Scotland.

I see the problems that we, the normal folk, are facing on a daily basis because of failed local and national government policy and I’m determined to help as many constituents as I can with these personal challenges.

My colleagues and I are not career politicians – we’re normal,working folk the same as most of you – but we’ve had enough of the incompetence and mismanagement of the last twenty years or so.

The philosophy of Reform UK is to make Great Britain great again, through promoting the values of family, community and country.

I'm honoured to stand as the Scottish Labour candidate for Caithness, Sutherland, and Easter Ross. My roots in the region and extensive experience in politics and charity operations equip me to address diverse challenges, from economic diversification to healthcare access. Committed to transparency and community engagement, I'm driven by a passion for social justice. Let's work together to create a future where every voice is heard and every individual thrives.



My political journey traces its roots back to the heart of this region, where I began my career as a parliamentary researcher working alongside Highland and island MSPs. Having moved to Scotland from Brussels in my late teens, I immersed myself in the vibrant tapestry of Scottish life, pursuing my passion for public communication through degrees at QMU in Edinburgh and Stirling University. Armed with a strong foundation in critical thinking and problem-solving, I ventured into the corridors of power, working at both the European Parliament and Holyrood.



In 2016, personal circumstances led me to London, where I embarked on a new chapter in charity operations and finance, even retraining as an accountant. Guided by a commitment to social justice, I assumed leadership roles within the Violence against Women sector, advocating tirelessly for those in need. Today, as Head of Operations and Finance at the Political Studies Association, I continue to champion transparency, pragmatism, and community engagement.



Driven by a profound belief in the transformative power of politics, I am unwavering in my dedication to championing the voices of our communities. As we navigate the complexities of our time, I stand firm in my commitment to socialist principles, working tirelessly to build a future where every individual has the opportunity to thrive and prosper. Together, let us forge a path toward a more just and equitable society for all.

party_name: Alliance to Liberate Scotland and Sovereignty
statement_to_voters:
I’m a native Highlander born in Inverness and have always had a base here. I’ve been a supporter of Sovereign Independence since age 7. When I discovered, back then, that Scotland had 72 seats in Westminster out of 630 I realised that our entire nation is, enslaved and subordinate to England, even more so since the deficit is now greater, only 59 MPs in Westminster

I held office in the SNP at Branch, Constituency and National Level until leaving the Party in 1988 over their folly in the advocacy of so called “Independence” in the EU effectively turning the party in one of Devolution.

I am persuaded that the road to Independence means radical action by the people and in not seeking the permission of Westminster but by campaigning for a democratic majority of Scots who would support a Unilateral Declaration of Independence a route proved to be successful by many other Sovereign peoples.

A vote for Sovereignty expresses your support for that course of action.

Dr Steven William Welsh is a retired NHS Consultant Psychiatrist with decades of frontline service across some of Scotland’s most remote and challenging communities. He brings deep professional experience, strong local understanding, and a lifelong commitment to public service to his candidacy for Reform UK in his first choice constituency of Caithness, Sutherland, and Ross.

Steven began his consultant career in 1996 with the Caithness and Sutherland NHS Trust while living in Dunnet. For many years he travelled weekly to Inverness to support Highland-wide psychiatric services and take part in the regional on-call rota. He later served as a Consultant Psychiatrist with NHS Western Isles from 2007 until his retirement in 2015, providing care across the entire island chain from the Butt of Lewis to Barra.

Few candidates can claim such extensive firsthand experience across the communities of the Highlands and Islands.

Steven’s clinical training spans General Practice, Hospital based practice, General Psychiatry, and Old Age Psychiatry, giving him a broad understanding of Scotland’s healthcare challenges — particularly the pressures facing rural services, elderly care provision, recruitment shortages, and long travel distances for treatment. He believes strongly that the NHS must remain free at the point of need while being reformed to deliver faster treatment and better outcomes for patients, consistent with Reform UK’s commitment to cutting bureaucracy and eliminating waiting lists so resources reach the frontline.

Steven is proud to represent Reform UK and is passionate about the principles of family, community, and country. After decades serving patients and communities across the Highlands and Islands, he believes these values are the foundation of a strong society and should guide public policy in Scotland. He supports Reform UK’s programme to restore affordable energy, strengthen border integrity, protect freedom of speech, and ensure schools focus on education rather than ideology.

Married for over forty years with four children and three grandchildren, Steven understands firsthand the importance of stable families and resilient local communities. He believes Scotland needs a prosperous economy, reliable infrastructure, and public services that work for the people they serve — particularly in rural and island communities that are too often overlooked by central government.

With decades of experience working directly with patients and communities across the Highlands and Islands, Steven William Welsh offers informed leadership on healthcare reform and a strong local voice in Holyrood for Caithness, Sutherland, and Ross.

My name is Andrew Scott and I’m your candidate for the Scottish Parliament 2026 election for the Reform UK party.

I want to tackle issues such as high taxes, reforming the NHS and scrapping net zero.

I currently work as a postperson and I see the challenges and struggles daily going around my post duty and I would like to change and improve that.

I am standing for Reform UK party as they offer real hope and vision to transform our society through things like lower income taxes, reforming our health care, bringing our education to world class again and scrapping net zero saving you money on your energy bills.

I have decided to stand in this election because I have become increasingly disappointed in the calibre of our politicians and feel that after 19 years the SNP needs to be challenged. I believe that for too long the people elected to represent us have represented their party first and put the electorate second.

Eva has served as a family and child lawyer in the community for 40 years, her home, her work and her heart are in the constituency and she understands local needs and values because like you she lives there.

She self-describes as the ‘bionic candidate’ because the Scottish NHS saved her life; giving her a couple of dozen prosthetic legs over the years and custom-building her a titanium spine.

Eva will always put her constituents first.  A strong fighter for women and children's rights, she strives for true equality and is determined to see the eradication of poverty, financial and of aspiration.

Eva said.  “Since Scotland’s independent autonomous education and health services created and rebuilt me, I intend to return the favour by helping to rebuild Scotland as an independent, peace loving egalitarian country where everyone can afford to eat and heat free from the threats of poverty and homelessness. Scotland's greatest resource is our people and we all deserve the chance to realise our full potential.”

At Scottish Labour we know that we must deliver economic growth to make Scotland a success for all our citizens, and that the rewards of growth need to be tipped more in favour of our communities and workers.  Scotland's people deserve a future with opportunities for all, where kindness, hard work, and ambition lead to great outcomes. With a Labour government now in Westminster, we have a unique opportunity to energise the ambition for Scotland too.

I am a qualified accountant with over 25 years’ experience as a business leader and board director across manufacturing, oil & gas, livestock auctioning, property investment, commercial law, and financial services. These enterprises were substantial, but notably, they supported small and medium sized businesses, which account for nearly 60% of all jobs in Scotland. My experience extends to working with, and influencing, regulators, policy makers, and sector bodies. For over 24 years, my husband and I have lived in the constituency, where we raised our four children.

Thank you for your time. I am excited to be running for the Clydebank & Milngavie constituency for the Scottish Liberal Democrats. For 14 years, I have called Bearsden my home; I would not live anywhere else. I have raised my family here and I am lucky enough to represent Bearsden South on the council.

Unfortunately, I have seen this area move backwards under the SNP.  For too many people, it feels like things aren't working.

Household bills are soaring; taxes are going up, but services are reduced. People are struggling to see their GP and they're worried about the state of our schools. The fine people of Clydebank and Milngavie deserve better than this.

That's why I will use my platform to fight for a new health centre that will deliver first-rate healthcare for everyone. I will fight for support with the cost of living and for the funding needed to improve our schools and fix our roads.

I have only been involved in politics for a few years and I am standing as your candidate because I believe there is a better way. We, as elected representatives, should be working together to improve the lives of the people who have elected us. We should be setting the highest bar in behaviour and not squabbling over petty things that fail to benefit you, the constituents that elected us.

Clydebank and Milngavie deserves change with fairness at its heart. I hope you can place your trust in me to deliver that change by backing the Scottish Liberal Democrats on both ballot papers on May 7th.

Key Priorities for Milngavie:  •Improving Transport links across EDC and reinstating the trains to Glasgow City every 15 min  • Enough school bus provision for all in the catchments  •Green Belt Protection  •Helping the local economy grow by supporting small businesses  •Sorting out the potholes throughout Milngavie ward •Improved communication between councillors and constituents

After benefiting from an excellent Scottish state education, I went on to build a career of over 35 years in the software industry. I’ve worked across the full spectrum—from small startups to large multinationals—and for the past decade, I’ve been self-employed as a freelancer.
In recent years, I’ve become increasingly concerned about the direction our country is heading in. As a mother of two grown children, I worry deeply about the kind of future they—and the next generation—will inherit. That is why I’ve made the decision to stand for election.
I believe Scotland deserves better. Too many people feel let down, and meaningful change is urgently needed.
I’m a plain-speaking, hard-working professional—not a career politician. As an MSP, my priority will always be to put my constituents first, in every decision I make.

I have dedicated my entire working life to public service having served for 25 years in the British Army, completing operational tours around the world.

After leaving the Army, I continued my service to my country by working for 15 years as a civil servant with the Ministry of Defence.

For the past seven years, I have chaired a community group in Cowdenbeath. This experience gave me a hunger to make a real difference to local people’s lives. With my background in leadership, problem-solving, and community engagement, I believe the time is right for me to stand as your candidate for the Scottish Parliamentary elections.

party_name: Alliance to Liberate Scotland and Sovereignty
statement_to_voters:
Laurie Moffat is a proud resident of Cardenden, with deep roots in the historic coalfields of the Lochgelly, Cardenden, and Benarty area. Born in Sydney, Australia, in 1981 to Scottish parents who emigrated under the £10 Pom scheme, he returned with his family to Fife at the age of three and was raised in Bowhill. His family connections extend to Ballingry (his father’s birthplace), Lumphinnans (his Di’s birthplace, known as Fife’s “Little Moscow”), Bowhill (his mother’s birthplace), and relatives still living in Cowdenbeath.

A former pupil of Lochgelly High School and a graduate of Robert Gordon University, Laurie is a former rugby player and the youngest-ever club captain of Glenrothes RFC, demonstrating proven leadership and community spirit.

With over 15 years of experience in the subsea oil and gas sector before transitioning to the biogas industry—where he managed the Anaerobic Digestion plant at Inchdairnie near Kinglassie—Laurie advocates for an all-energy future for Scotland. This includes renewables, biogas, nuclear, and sustainable hydrocarbons through a pragmatic, worker-focused transition that prioritises job protection.

As a dedicated Georgist, sovereign money advocate, and land reformer, Laurie draws inspiration from Henry George, Positive Money’s work on sovereign money systems, the Scottish Land Revenue Group’s promotion of Annual Ground Rent as a form of land value taxation, and Scotland’s historical land reform movement from the Highland Clearances to contemporary efforts for community ownership. He supports public control of money creation, the end of fractional reserve lending, and a shift in the tax burden from labour and productive capital onto unearned land values—ensuring that community-created land value benefits the public while reducing taxes on wages, earnings, investment, and business.

Inspired by Salvo and the Claim of Right—including his signing of the Edinburgh Proclamation—Laurie is committed to restoring full Scottish sovereignty, independent from both the United Kingdom and the European Union. In line with Sovereignty party policy, he supports the legitimacy of a Unilateral Declaration of Independence (UDI), conditional on a democratic majority of the Scottish electorate voting for pro-independence parties. This approach emphasises radical action by the Scottish people themselves, without seeking permission from Westminster—a path successfully pursued by other nations to achieve sovereignty.

He will stand for Sovereignty in the Cowdenbeath constituency and for Liberate Scotland on the Mid Scotland and Fife regional list in the 2026 Scottish Parliament election.

If elected, Laurie will combat de-industrialisation, oppose freeports that risk privatising public resources, and advance Georgist, sovereign money, and land reform policies to foster regeneration, create sustainable jobs, revive derelict sites for community benefit, and secure long-term prosperity and robust public services across the Cowdenbeath constituency and beyond.

Darren is your Scottish Conservative and Unionist candidate for the Cowdenbeath ward and has represented the ward as a Councillor since 2017.

Darren lives in Cowdenbeath with his wife and three children and experiences the same issues and concerns that affect local residents.

As a Conservative, Darren firmly believes in localism and that local people know what is best for the area in which they live, and that decisions should be made locally and not by anonymous individuals based elsewhere.

If re-elected, Darren will continue to work tirelessly within your community and be the strong, dedicated representative you deserve.

I have lived and worked all my life in the Central Belt of Scotland. Educated at Edinburgh and Edinburgh Napier Universities, I have a BSc in Physics, as well as a Master’s degree in Business. After graduating, I enjoyed an extensive career in IT. Starting off as a Cobol Programmer in the Insurance sector, I had a number of roles, working my way up into IT Management. My final role was as a Development Manager for a public sector organisation.

Since retiring, I have used my Physics background to fully understand the ins and outs of Climate Change and Net Zero.

I joined Reform UK in July 2025, to campaign for an end to the Net Zero madness, which is the real cause of the cost-of-living crisis

party_name: Alliance to Liberate Scotland and Sovereignty
favourite_biscuit: Either Jaffa Cakes or Tunnock's Teacakes - but vegan!
statement_to_voters:
Author, academic, carer, teacher and law student, Alan McManus is the Sovereignty candidate for Cumbernauld & Kilsyth, standing for Liberate Scotland on the Central Scotland & Lothians West regional list.

Growing up in a 1960’s Glasgow overspill housing scheme in Kirkintilloch, ‘Dr McManus’ uses his title on social media to avoid his celebrated namesake being blamed for Alan’s forthright views on the UK and devolved government overreach of executive power, especially since 2020, resulting in increasing limitation of individual rights: especially of freedom of expression and association.

A lifelong advocate for animal welfare and a keen organic gardener, Alan opposes the cruelty of factory farming and the poisoning of the land, water, plants, fish, animals and people, by the indiscriminate use of pesticides and plastics. Canvassing for Freedom Alliance in rural Kintyre in 2023, farmers warned Alan of the coming food crisis, so even as a vegan he understands the slogan: “no farmers, no food!”

Alan has written several books on topics of ethical controversy and tries to see both sides and explore common ground: like pro-life and pro-choice women working together to support mothers under severe socio-economic pressure. As an amateur actor, cyclist and sailor, Alan wants to see Scots of all ages have the opportunity to thrive – in an independent Scotland!

party_description_text: Scottish Alliance for Democracy and Freedom
statement_to_voters: For too long the establishment parties of the LibLabCon, SNP and Greens and dictated to the people of Scotland in a top down manner the priorities of this parties and they do not serve the best interests of the people of Scotland. We believe in freedom of expression, to have a job or a business, to work hard and earn a good wage and keep the majority of your earnings rather than handing it to government who waste it on vanity projects and globalist net zero green agendas or similar. That is why I am standing in the Scottish Parliamentary elections to fight for public services par excellence to offer people choice to gain the best education, the best health care and the best location for living and working.

I am the Reform UK candidate for North Ayrshire and Arran constituency. I was born in Glasgow to a paediatric surgeon and a mathematics teacher. I studied at the High School of Glasgow and became an accountant and a working economist, with three master’s degrees in: International Commercial Law (LLM – University of Salford)); European Business (Euro MBA – University of Maastricht)); and Digital Transformation (MBA – University of Barcelona).  I am married with 3 children.

For the last 30 years, I have worked as an international economic development consultant and policy adviser for the European Union, World Bank, IMF, British Government Foreign and Commonwealth Office and others with assignments in more than 50 countries throughout the Middle East (Egypt, Jordan, Palestine, Syria, Lebanon and Yemen), Russia, Africa and Eastern Europe.

Notable assignments include:

Shipbuilding: Project leader for the privatisation of the Western Shipbuilding and Repair Yard JSC, Klaipeda, Lithuania; Project leader for restructuring of: Egyptian Ship Building and Repair Company, Alexandria; Bijela Shipyard, Montenegro and Karachi Shipyard, Pakistan.

Trade and Investment: Lead Adviser to the Ministry of Trade and Industry Kosovo (4 years), negotiating 4 free trade agreements and advising on trade and investment strategy.

Establishment of the Investment Promotion Agency and well as SME Development. Chief Accountant to the British Government Kosovo Rehabilitation Programme, with responsibility for the allocation of £180 million in 9 months to restore power supplies, hospitals, sanitary services and some 500 small projects.

On returning to my home in Scotland, I feel the need to be politically active as a force for change. I am known within the North Ayrshire area and live in Largs.

My professional experience spans 20 years in social care, street fundraising and horticulture. I currently work as office manager for Irene Campbell MP.

Each role has given me insights into the challenges faced by families, workers, and small businesses.

To win this campaign, we need to engage with people directly, on the doorsteps and in our communities. We need to connect with people, listen to their concerns, and fight for them every step of the way.

It’s time to have those difficult conversations to challenge the misinformation and division spread by those who do not have our community’s best interests at heart.

After many long years of SNP incompetence, we need change. We need a new direction.

Our community faces challenges that affect us all: health and economic inequality and a lack of opportunities for young people. This is our chance to make things happen.

Together, we can send a clear message - Cunninghame North deserves better than the SNP. With Anas Sarwar as First Minister and myself in Cunninghame North we can build a fairer, stronger Scotland.

As the MP for North Ayrshire and Arran since 2015, I have championed a range of important causes on behalf of constituents.From the commencement of my first term I have been assiduous in assisting constituents, community groups and local businesses as an active and accessible MP.At Westminster, I have vocally supported the right of Scotland to choose its own independent future while successfully working cross party to secure two weeks paid bereavement leave for all parents who lose a child under the age of 18 years old and those who experience a stillbirth.I continue to campaign for pension justice for WASPI women who had their state pension age raised with little or no notice and I have been extremely vocal on a wide variety of animal welfare and consumer rights issues.Locally, I work in partnership with SNP led North Ayrshire Council and promote the SNP Government as it delivers better housing, schools, and the best NHS in the UK.I have held portfolios in the SNP Westminster group such as spokesperson for Levelling Up, Department for the Environment, Farming and Rural Affairs and most recently the Department of the Attorney General.My constituency focus means that I have campaigned locally on matters such as support with dementia, loneliness, debt support, energy bills and the cost-of- living crisis.Importantly, I jointly organised with the local SNP MSP a dozen employment fayres, five funding fayres and actively encouraged a 900 job, £1.4 billion green investment into my constituency.

party_description_text: Scottish Trade Unionist and Socialist Coalition
statement_to_voters: Lynda lives in Dumbarton and was brought up in West Dunbartonshire.

Welcome! Thank you for your interest. As your local councillor, I would put local issues first. I would dive into West Dunbartonshire Council affairs, bringing a fresh voice of integrity, decency, originality and common sense.

A vote for the Scottish Family Party is a vote for protecting children and helping families. Strong academic teaching is the best way out of poverty and a great equaliser. We can't all quote Homer in Greek but I can quote Pink Floyd. We must make school a safe place to learn without questions that endorse pornography, under-age sex and controversial sexual practices for children. Adults can choose but - leave our kids alone!

Gary Mulvaney was elected for his fourth term in 2017 and leads the eight member group of Conservatives on Argyll & Bute Council.

In May 2013, Conservative councillors co-founded The Argyll, Lomond, and Islands Group (TALIG) and following the 2017 elections, they entered into Administration as part of this group.

Gary Mulvaney was elected as Depute Leader of the council and is the Policy Lead for Strategic Finance and Capital Regeneration. He sits on Policy & Resources and Development & Infrastructures committees.

He is principal political lead for the Helensburgh Waterfront Development.

He is a qualified accountant, and a fellow of the Association of Chartered Certified Accountants.  His career has covered local government, marketing, and extensive experience in the motor trade. He currently is Director, and Centre Principal, of Helensburgh Toyota, having previously been the dealership's Financial Director.

Gary was born in 1968.  His home town is Helensburgh, where he lives with his wife Janet and her daughter Sammy-Jo.

Gary is a former Westminster parliamentary candidate, having contested the seat in 2017 and 2010. In 2017, the Conservatives came within 1300 votes of taking the seat from the SNP. When he stood in the 2010 General he came second, with Liberal Democrat MP Alan Reid’s majority squeezed to just over three thousand votes.

During the 2010 campaign, the local news website ForArgyll.com said that, if elected to Westminster, Gary would be "a formidable operator".  Describing him as "A natural strategist and a clear thinker...", the website said "...he has the added advantage of experience".  The writer continued "If it is possible to be open-minded and interested in change while not being troubled by uncertainties or indecision, this is who he is”.

Outside business and politics, Gary was Business Manager of the Glasgow-based Apollo Players theatre group and made his stage debut in 2010 at the Kings Theatre, Glasgow, and in 2012 he played in Annie at the Glasgow Pavilion theatre.

Our party of ordinary people is totally fed up with the professional politicians ignoring our needs and bending to the wishes of their own parties.

We have decided to take matters into our own hands and retrieve our own power from people who no longer represent our values.

I am a lifelong resident who has worked with local businesses and residents all my career. I am here to challenge the chaos and current instability we have in Scotland and Westminster with disconnected and divided governments and uncertainty of Labour policies.  We need to be addressing the serious questions about whether our governments are presently undermining our country including our farmers, our countryside, our climate and our health. Are we to work together or in confrontation? I came in to politics for the sake of my children and we need to look long term, not just seek short term 'fixes'.

I am delighted to have been selected as the candidate for Dundee City East.

Too many families in Dundee are living in poverty, and child hunger is a daily reality. Young people have seen services cut and opportunities dry up, while households are struggling with rising bills and insecure jobs. That has to change.

Scottish Liberal Democrats believe in fairness for everyone, no matter who you are or where you come from. We've got a realistic plan to get things done.

We will fight child poverty and give families the support they deserve. We will create opportunities for young people through apprenticeships, skills, and safe spaces. We'll back local businesses to grow and bring investment into Dundee, creating secure, well-paid jobs. And we'll fix our NHS — cutting waiting times, supporting carers, and making sure everyone can see a GP, dentist or mental health professional when they need to.

Dundee City East deserves better. And with the Scottish Liberal Democrats, you can vote for it.

I am standing on a manifesto of change. Change that will allow people to thrive not just survive. Change that will deliver a brighter future for everyone. I'm not going to ask you to vote for me to stop someone else winning, but instead to help return a Labour Government. Only Labour can make the changes we so desperately need from Westminster. A vote for me is a vote for a strong local voice in Westminster as your Labour MP.

party_description_text: Scottish Trade Unionist and Socialist Coalition
statement_to_voters:
Donald lives in Dundee East and works in the NHS. He is a member of the Unison trade union.

TUSC says loudly and clearly that working class communities across Dundee can’t take another penny of cuts, and will fight cuts to all services. I stand for no cuts/needs budgets that utilise all financial mechanisms to stop cuts and mobilise a mass campaign for the funding we need.

I’m standing in this election to offer an alternative. One that demands the rich and big business pay, not working-class people.

The money is there, but it is in the wrong hands. It’s long passed time we had a political party prepared to fight for the working class. Scottish TUSC calls on the trade unions to build a new workers’ party. Until then Scottish TUSC will stand to offer a fighting socialist alternative.

I am looking forward to taking part in the democratic process once again. I am standing to give the residents of Dundee City West a new voice instead of the same old one trick ponies we have had in the city for too long. If you are looking for an end to the populist rhetoric we are currently seeing, voting Scottish Liberal Democrat is the way to go about it.

Scottish Liberal Democrats believe in fairness for everyone, no matter who you are or where you come from. We’ve got a realistic plan to get things done.

We will deliver first class heath care so you can see your GP, dentist or mental health specialist when you need them. We’ll help you with the cost of living by insulating cold homes and using Scottish renewable energy to cut bills.  We’ll fix our roads, ferries and public transport. We’ll get Scottish education back to its best by expanding pupil support in every school.

Liberal Democrats are winning again, with more councillors, a record number of MPs and more to come.

Dundee City West deserves better.    And with the Scottish Liberal Democrats, you can vote for it.

party_description_text: Scottish Trade Unionist and Socialist Coalition
statement_to_voters: Jim McFarlane is the branch secretary of Dundee City UNISON and is one of the city’s most well-known trade unionists.  During that time Jim has been to the forefront of opposing all cuts imposed by the administration and has been an active socialist since the Timex strike of 1993.

As someone born and raised right here in East Kilbride, my roots in this community run deep, and my commitment to that community is unwavering.

I am standing as an independent candidate to offer a genuine, people-focused alternative for our town. Through my daily work in libraries and my ongoing efforts as an active trade unionist, I have seen first-hand how vital robust, accessible public services are to our collective well-being.

I firmly believe in a society that puts people before profit - one where the resources generated by our community are reinvested directly into uplifting every resident, ensuring no one is left behind.

Beyond our local economy, my vision for East Kilbride and Scotland is fundamentally rooted in sustainability and peace. As a dedicated environmentalist, I will fight for policies that protect our future, and I will always advocate for compassion, dialogue, and cooperation over division.

As an Independent MSP, I would:
- Promote investment in public services. Robust public services are the foundation of a fair society, ensuring community well-being and collective support, and must always come before private profit.
- Protect workers' rights. Championing workers' rights and trade unions ensures fair pay and safe conditions, empowering working people and keeping wealth within our local East Kilbride community.
- Support independence. Independence gives Scotland the democratic power to protect public services, control our own resources, and build a peaceful, sustainable society on our own terms.

“I am standing on the Regional List for Edinburgh and Lothians East because
Scotland’s decisions should be made in Scotland,” said Morgwn C Davies.
“Independence is about democratic control—having the power to shape our
economy so that Scotland’s wealth can benefit Scotland’s people, set a fair and
workable approach to immigration that works for Scotland, and choose a foreign
policy that reflects our commitment to peace, neutrality, and constructive
engagement with the world.”

I am standing because I believe government in Westminster and Holyrood has lost touch with real life. Too many career politicians have never run a business or faced the impact of rising taxes and energy costs.

After 26 years of building businesses and supporting entrepreneurs, I decided I had to step forward and bring common-sense and honesty to politics.

My background is in IT and AI, with experience in environmental and energy projects. I have worked in agriculture and still live on a smallholding, so I empathise with the pressures and crazy bureaucracy and carbon audits our farmers face.

My priorities are to cut taxes, scrap net-zero policies that harm our economy, and make devolution work for Scotland.
My aim is simple. Put local people first and make Scotland the most prosperous place in the UK to live and work.

I am a retired research scientist. I live in Clydebank with my wife and young adult daughter. I served for ten years as a Labour Councillor in West Dunbartonshire. I aim to deliver accountable, fair and efficient government.

Vote Reform - get reform. The Scottish Parliament desperately needs Reform.

I've worked in Eastwood for over a decade and live here with my young family. Like many families in East Ren, I moved here because of the opportunities it offers for my two girls.

Eastwood is an amazing place to raise children because of our beautiful green spaces, thriving communities and excellent schools, but our area cannot take another five years of cuts to local budgets. That's why I'm standing as Labour's candidate in the Scottish Parliament election next year.

The first campaign I ran in Eastwood was a decade ago, to save the local 394 bus service, which you can still see serving our communities today. I have a proven record of delivering for these communities already, and want to do more as your MSP.

Charles Dundas grew up in West Lothian, attended Whitburn Academy and graduated with an MA in History from the University of Glasgow.

He has since worked in accountancy, policy research, campaigning, and as the Chief Executive of a Scottish conservation charity.

Between 2007 and 2012 he was elected to the City of Edinburgh Council, representing the City Centre. He chaired the Lib Dem Group and sat on the Planning, Education, Culture, and Transport & Environment Committees, as well as the Lothian and Borders Police Board and the board of the Royal Lyceum Theatre.

Charles is the Convenor of the Scottish Green Liberal Democrats group and has performed at each Edinburgh Festival Fringe since 2015.

I Don't know what's best for other people and I don't believe other people know what's best for other people either. Especially those people in Government. I will fight to defend individual rights and liberties from the predations of government and campaign to restore those rights already eroded, such as free speech, free association and property rights. NO to net zero. NO to CBDC NO to censorship. End the war on Drugs.

Taxation Is Theft

With a career as an international sales professional with over 30 years of experience and with a proven track record of building relationships, negotiating complex deals, and leading cross-cultural teams, I have a deep understanding of the dynamics that shape successful business transactions.

I am leveraging this expertise to stand as a Reform candidate for the Holyrood Parliament constituency of Edinburgh Central on Thursday May 7th, 2026.

With a results-driven approach to public service, I am committed to providing both accountability and value for money for the hard-working people and business owners of Central Edinburgh.

I’m standing as an MSP candidate to force the Guga hunt out of the shadows and into the political spotlight,

Gannet chicks are being snatched from their nests and bludgeoned to death for nothing more than a tradition. That clearly shouldn’t be happening, especially not in the middle of a biodiversity crisis.

Polling shows the vast majority of the Scottish people want the Guga hunt to end. It’s high time this outdated exemption was removed from the law, and these magnificent Gannets given the protections they deserve.

Lorna Slater was elected Co-leader of the Scottish Greens in 2019 and hopes to use her post to get more women elected to Holyrood in 2021 and councils across Scotland in 2020. As Engineering Project Manager at Orbital Marine power, Lorna has first-hand experience of how green industries can benefit Scotland and how we can lead the way in a just transition away from oil and gas.

Together we can elect Lorna to Holyrood to fight for climate action, and for our future as an independent country at the heart of the European Union.

I’m standing as a candidate because our communities deserve better than rising costs, stretched services and a lack of real opportunity.

My family have called this area home since the beginning of the last century and have deep roots into the community.

Raised by a single‑parent mum who became a social worker, she worked hard to build a better life. I went to school in East Lothian, studied at Edinburgh University, became a teacher and taught in some of the toughest schools, and now run a digital learning business supporting organisations with compliance policy.

I’m standing to bring practical, community‑focused solutions: easing the cost of living, improving local services, supporting schools and ensuring housing works for local people.

Born in Leith, raised in Niddrie, living in hope.
An I.T consultant with FT100 companies for 35 years.
Have acquired a mindset and a set if core values essential to negotiations and delivery of customer expectations. With this experience I intend to use these skills to deliver independence , nothing else, nothing less to the sovereign people of Scotland.

I’m standing to be an MSP to bring an unapologetic feminist and left-wing voice to the Scottish Parliament to fight for the radical, transformative change our country needs.

As a seasoned activist and campaigner, I have worked across a broad range of issues, fighting for abortion rights, trans rights, migrant justice and Palestine, and against the two-child benefit cap and the arms trade.

In my professional life, I have worked as a facilitator and researcher with young people, women and activist groups in Scotland and across the world. This work has had a huge influence on how I see the world and the need for radical, revolutionary action at both small and large scales.

If elected as an MSP, I would bring my passion for radical change right to the heart of Scottish politics. I would stand up against the unequal, oppressive systems that are so harmful to marginalised communities in Scotland, helping to address the cost of living crisis and ensure that everyone has access to safe and secure housing and healthcare. I would work to build an economy based on equality, peace and wellbeing rather than growth, and I would fight against the climate emergency – the defining issue of my generation and beyond.

I am immensely proud of what we’ve achieved as Greens in Scotland, from securing the right of refugees to vote, to pushing through free bus travel for young people, to standing up so strongly for trans rights, migrant rights and Palestine when others have run scared. If elected to Holyrood, I would work hard every day on behalf of my community to transform Scotland for the better, continuing this Green legacy of fighting for progressive action.

I’m really excited to be a lead candidate for the Lothian list for the Greens, alongside the amazing Lorna Slater, Q Manivannan and Kayleigh Kinross-O’Neill, and I look forward to bringing our collective vision to Holyrood in May.

Edinburgh City Council has a reputation for wasting money, for not listening to residents, focusing on vanity projects and not getting the basics of city management right.

Initiatives such as the Tram, Bin hubs, Spaces for People bollards, Controlled Parking Zone extensions to Gorgie and Hutchison and the proposed Low Emissions Zone are good examples while roads and pavements deteriorate, drain gullies are unmaintained leading to flooding, and the Dell bridges and Craiglockhart Hill paths are closed.

If elected I will focus on getting the basic services right by listening to those who know best, local residents.

Euan currently serves as a local elected Councillor for Drum Brae/Gyle Ward. Since being elected he has been an energetic local advocate for residents and has led campaigns on a range of issues, developing a reputation as someone who shows up and does the work”.Euan is the SNP Council Group Spokesperson for Education, Children and Families. He worked as a Support Worker with vulnerable young people in local schools and a charity here in west Edinburgh before starting his business.As a small business owner he has brought debates to Edinburgh Council asking for measures to revive our local high streets. He opened his cafe as a ‘warm space’ before bringing a motion to the Council asking them to open public spaces under a similar initiative.Euan is passionate about workers’ rights, human rights and climate justice. He regularly attends demonstrations, for example, on climate action and Palestine solidarity protests calling for a ceasefire in Gaza.

Originally from Motherwell, Kayleigh moved to Edinburgh for university and later worked for Euan’s Guide and Inclusion Scotland. Since being elected she has been the Green Councillor Group’s Transport spokesperson, while also working part-time for Lorna Slater MSP in the Scottish Parliament.

Kayleigh is a strong advocate for better accessibility, stronger engagement, and greener values. She is vocal on issues of pavement parking, dropped curb access, and other barriers that disabled and elderly people face in their daily life.

As an MSP, Kayleigh will bring her experience of representing people who are struggling with housing issues, health problems, financial difficulties, access needs, and barriers to multiple areas of society to the Scottish Parliament. She is standing in this election in order to push further against the rise of the far right and the divisive politics they represent.

My parents immigrated from Bolton, England and moved to Ontario, Canada where I was born and raised.

I attended York University in Toronto for Film Production and Screenwriting and moved to Edinburgh seven years ago.

I work in a local Primary School as a Pupil Support Assistant and I’m an elected shop steward for Unison.

I was a Support Worker at a charity in Restalrig for blind, autistic and epileptic adults.

I have also previously worked as a visual artist and album cover artist

Community Councillor for Liberton and district a local resident Abu Meron has been officially indorsed as ALBA Party candidate for Liberton Gilmorton ward for the City of Edinburgh Council Election in 5th May 2022.

He was an elected community councillor for Leith links Community council before he moved to Morton Hall in 2017. Currently he is a community councillor for Liberton & District community council.

Abu born in Bangladesh in 1974. After finishing of his Graduation from National University of Bangladesh he went to India to further study in Computer Concepts. He also completed LLB Preliminary. Abu obtained MBA degree from International University LONDON Centre, UK and moved to Edinburgh 21 years ago.

His wife Dr.Tabassum is working with the NHS Scotland, son studying engineering at Heriot Watt University and daughter is in Gracemount High School.

Abu has been working for local and international charitable organization, since his student life he is always distinguishable, approachable and available. He is a blood donor of National services Scotland. he is always passionate about helping others. Abu has experience of public life and voluntary sectors. He also run his own businesses. He fully supports ALBA Party’s five-point plan moreover he is a fundamental believer of equality for all.

He works hard for the people and the communities to preserve green space, road safety, community safety, place of residence whilst demonstrating strong work ethics.

ABU MERON  FOR LIBERTON GILMERTON t : 07883590159 f : Abu Meron Alba e : abumeronalba@outlook.com

Promoted by Chris McEleny on behalf of Abu Meron, both of Alba party,17 Forth street, Glasgow G41 2SP

Lyn was born in Glasgow in 1970 and grew up in Morayshire. Having studied at Robert Gordon’s University in Aberdeen with honours in Public Administration, where she also was first active in student politics with the Federation of Student Nationalists, Lyn began her housing career in Aberdeen City Council where she continued her activism within the trade union movement and served as both Equalities Officer and Convenor of the city branch of Unison.With a postgraduate degree and over 15 years of a housing career which included working for the Chartered Institute of Housing, local authorities, housing associations and being self employed as a consultant, Lyn moved, with her husband, to East Lothian around 15 years ago and has undertaken a wider range of public service roles in health and social care as well as equalities, both paid and voluntary.Now raising a family in the area, Lyn was persuaded back into a more politically active life, was elected as Councillor for Dunbar & East Linton ward in 2022 and is Leader of the Opposition at East Lothian Council.

David is the Reform candidate for Edinburgh Northwestern, bringing nearly 30 years of policing experience – much of which as a detective dealing with serious and organised crime.

Since retiring from the police in 2016, he has remained active in his community, serving as a community councillor and now working as an independent driving instructor.

Motivated by concerns about declining public services, David is standing to deliver practical, common-sense solutions that put people first.

Deirdre was a front-bench member of the SNP group in Westminster covering relations with the devolved administrations, and liaison with the Scottish Government and Scottish Parliament. She sits on the Public Accounts Committee which is responsible for examining the value for money of public spending, holding the UK Government and Civil Servants to account for the way our money gets spent.

Before winning her seat in the 2015 election, Deidre was a councillor for the Leith Walk ward on Edinburgh Council and Depute Lord Provost of Edinburgh. She was born in Western Australia, grew up in Perth and studied for her BA in English from John Curtin University before studying acting at the WA Academy of Performing Arts. She lives in Edinburgh with Dougie and her two daughters.

I am often asked why I got involved in politics. Quite simply, I like to fix things. I’m the sort of person who likes to get out their tools and put matters to rights there and then. My politics is the same – if I see things not working or people not having the opportunities they need, I will do all I can to put that right.

I feel honoured to represent Edinburgh Southern in the Scottish Parliament. It’s where I grew up and where I live with my wife and children. I started my career working in consulting, specialising in the financial services sector. I then ran several local businesses in Edinburgh – the shops Paper Tiger and Studio One. Since my election in 2016, I have striven to represent the diverse views and needs of my community in parliament and to make a difference in our part of Edinburgh.

My interests in Parliament have developed from these core principles – I am interested in what we need to do to give people opportunities, and improve our economy and our society. With the changes that automation and digitisation are bringing, I believe innovation and technology are hugely important. I also take a close interest in neuro-developmental disorders including ADHD, autism, dyslexia, and dyscalculia, along with the associated Education and Health Policy, as these are major barriers to people succeeding in education and life.

It’s not everyday you pass a law. But helping run my family’s small businesses gave me direct experience of being a front-line retail worker. This lead me to campaign for and pass through parliament a members bill to protect workers from violence and abuse at work. It is a tragedy that up until now, employees have had no defence from harassment and intimidation, for example while enforcing the law on age-restricted items.

I am currently a member of the Economy and Fair Work committee at the Scottish Parliament and  I am also the Scottish Labour Spokesperson for Economy, Business and Fair Work, helping build Scottish Labour’s vision for a growing economy and a fairer society.

Jane first moved to Scotland to study Law and Politics at Edinburgh University. She began her working life in financial services, investing funds in the Asia Pacific region. After starting a family and spending some time overseas, she returned to live in Scotland in 2010. Jane  has over the last decade retrained and worked as a mental health counsellor in Edinburgh working with adults and young people with experience in the voluntary sector, high schools and private practice. Through personal experience Jane is an advocate of the living donor project.

Jane believes in the positive impact all individuals can make to contribute to a diverse and flourishing society, and that it is the state’s role to support and facilitate citizens reaching their potential. Her experience in mental health services informs the importance of this. She seeks to help build a society where understanding and empathy outweighs blame and intolerance and a system which works to reduce entrenched inequalities. She has a special interest in getting counselling services fit for purpose, accessible and sustainable for all whilst ensuring fair pay for those working in the sector. As a parent of young adults, Jane wants to work to ensure every young person in Scotland has access to work, and quality training and those in work are paid a fair wage.

Through the experience of living overseas, Jane has a strong belief that there is more that binds us than divides us, and that problems need to be solved by cross border collaboration not separation. Jane also has a renewed appreciation for many aspects of Scotland including the humour, the food, the landscape and yes, the lovely climate too

My parents migrated to the UK from the West Indies in the 1960’s as part of the Windrush Generation.

Having been brought up in social housing and watching my parents struggle to pay bills and buy food throughout my childhood, I was determined to not be in the same situation.

I completed an apprenticeship in Lift Engineering in the 90’s and worked my way up through the grades to become a service manager. Thereafter, I became a lift consultant for an international real estate solution company and went on to start up my own lift consultancy company which is now in its third year of trading.

Scottish Parliament Elections 7th May 2026
Use your 1st Constituency Vote to vote for one of the main parties.
Use your 2nd vote on the Regional List to vote for your
NEW LOCAL REGISTERED POLITICAL PARTY

Together let’s start a revolution to remove the
mechanism which allows billionaires the ability to influence politics.
This IS achievable as to the nearest percentage point 100% of the electorate agrees with our objective. We only need around 25K List Votes out of a total electorate of 554K for us to secure one MSP and 50K votes to secure two MSPs.
WE ONLY REPRESENT THE PEOPLE LOCALLY
Your party only represents the people who live in Edinburgh, East Lothian and Midlothian North with zero external influence. Especially from billionaires.
OUR POLICY IS THE WILL OF THE PEOPLE
Your regional MSP will be bound by an UNCORRUPTIBLE system as they will vote as instructed directly by the party members from within the region.
ELECT PEOPLE DON’T ELECT POLITICIANS
Career politicians often start their careers in Freshers’ Week at Uni and then don’t acquire enough real-life experience to be telling us how to live our lives.
COMMON SENSE - NOT LEFT NOT RIGHT
All people want are common sense policies at which point people on the Left and the Right will often agree. The Left/Right divide is often artificial.
OUR INDEPENDENCE POLICY IS NEUTRAL
We are bringing unionists and nationalists together to fix politics. Once there is an Independence Referendum on the horizon members may vote to no longer be neutral. Three months later we will vote to decide to become unionist or nationalist. At that point the party will split in two and I will commit to helping the losing team to all resign and form a new local party with bottom-up control.
TWO LOCAL PARTIES WITH THEIR RESPECTIVE MEMBERS
AND BOTTOM-UP CONTROL WILL SOUND THE DEATH KNELL FOR BILLIONAIRE INFLUENCE OVER POLITICS.
REFORM received a £9M donation on 1/8/25 from a Thai Defence/Crypto billionaire. In Nov 2022 he donated £1M to Boris Johnson. In Jan 2023 his defence business Qinetiq secured an £80M contract.

Having lived in Edinburgh South constituency for over 30 years, I know personally many residents as well as the local issues which affect us. My children were brought up and educated here.

Following service as a policeman I was elected as a councillor in this constituency where I served for 15 years. This election has reactivated me in politics – I have been attracted by the common sense policies of Reform UK. I was formerly a Conservative but our economy has been burdened by the highest taxation in a generation, over regulation, high energy prices and crippling restrictions on personal freedoms. Labour offers more of the same. And the Greens seem to have become anti-science denying the biological reality of male and female (and they are not the only ones to do so!)

The ‘cancel culture’ of recent years has had a chilling effect; sadly it has often been government and official bodies which have played fast and loose with truth in silencing views other than those they want to impose. I have experienced that in our local council in the attempts to silence whistle-blowers. I want to value and guarantee freedom of expression and individual liberty.

I believe there are many ways to reduce energy costs and so benefit families, the cost of living and the economy. The Government, supported by all other parties, has banned or discouraged the use of energy resources available to us. I have long argued against net zero which is ruinously expensive and lacks justifying evidence. It needs scrapped.

I believe we need lower taxation. Reform UK will raise the tax-free threshold for Income Tax to £20,000 to help the lower paid and motivate those on benefits to move into work. The taxation burden on us ordinary people has never been higher – and especially so in Scotland.

I believe in valuing each person whatever their circumstances. I find that much of the current emphasis on racism and equality actually divides rather than unites us in all our diversity. I want to support families – and whilst not everyone has the opportunity, a stable family provides the best environment for children, and indeed us all.

Oh, one more thing. In this uncertain world we have unwisely allowed our Armed Forces to fall to a dangerously low level. I passionately believe the Government’s first duty is to defend its people from foreign and domestic threats.

And so, on these and many other issues, I want to offer residents of Edinburgh South the pragmatic, common sense alternative which Reform UK provides. I have experience and local knowledge. Join me. Vote for sensible policies. Vote Reform UK.

Andy is a passionate supporter of liberal principles. At the core of all policymaking should be the pillars of fairness, equality, dignity, and freedom. Our current political discourse has ventured far from these core ideals, and instead, partisan and parochial interests now dominate the way we are governed.

Andy wants to bring real solutions to the real problems faced by the people of Edinburgh South:

Bring down the cost of living     Save our schools     Build affordable housing     Preventing flood and end sewage dumping

In the time since the last General Election, we have seen the COVID pandemic, the cost of living crisis, and, recently, a housing emergency. All of these have been exacerbated by poor leadership and instability in both Holyrood and Westminster.

Edinburgh South deserves better - and Andy is the person to best represent us at Westminster!

I’m someone who has spent their life working hard to support family, neighbours, and community.

For years, I’ve seen how decisions made in party-political bubbles often ignore the realities of life here in the Borders.

I’m standing because I believe we deserve better, not just promises at election time, but honest, independent representation that puts people before parties.

I managed a McDonald’s in my younger career, like many young people around Berwickshire who started their first jobs there with me, and then I built up my own small gardening business from scratch.

Life threw some big challenges my way: serious health problems and major operations that changed everything. But out of that came one of the most rewarding parts of my life, becoming a foster carer.

For nearly 14 years now, I’ve opened my life to children who needed a home, giving them stability, love, and a chance to feel safe.

A few years ago I joined Eyemouth Community Council, thinking I’d just help out locally. What I saw shocked me.

Ordinary people, hard-working mums, dads, grandparents were being dismissed, talked down to, or ignored by those in positions of power. The disdain was real, and it made me angry.

I’m not a career politician. I went into this for one reason only: to stand up for the things that affect our lives, decent schools, proper care for our elderly because we will all get old if we are lucky, fair opportunities, safe streets, and a local economy that gives our young people a reason to stay here instead of leaving.
