My focus is straightforward and heartfelt - cut out real waste in the Scottish Government so we can protect and properly invest in our schools, education, and the NHS, the things that matter most to families every single day.

favourite_biscuit: Chocolate covered ginger from Border Biscuits
statement_to_voters:
INDEPENDENCE
Nothing Else! Nothing Less!
I believe in
Scotland
In the Energy and Power of its
PEOPLE
ITS
OIL, GAS, Wind & Wave Power
and the Vast Wealth of its LAND!
...BUT...
For too long
Scotland has been trapped
in a toxic and unequal union
...IT IS TIME...
To set her
Free from Westminster Rule
Free to take her rightful place on the
WORLD STAGE.
...FREE...
To be an Independent Country
And to rule Herself.
The Time is Now!

This is my first time standing for election for any party. I have decided to do this to put more money in the pockets of hard-working Scots, to bring real change to Holyrood and to protect the Union from the SNP’s plans for another divisive referendum.

Amanda born in Scotland, resides in the Falkirk area and has dedicated 35 years to front line public service, building a career focused on supporting people and strengthening communities.

Her professional background spans roles in law, child protection, safeguarding and education; she is a qualified and experienced teacher. Amanda’s life has been dedicated to serving the public. Throughout her career, she has held senior leadership positions, where she has been responsible for delivering meaningful, practical outcomes for individuals and families. She brings this same results driven approach to her candidacy, with a clear focus on making a tangible difference for local people. Amanda is determined to put the people of Falkirk East and Linlithgow first. She will listen to your concerns, she will be your voice, she will work hard to make change happen, she will be your champion, Amanda will be there for you.

Vote Reform. Together, let’s see what we can do to make that change happen.

Since 2015, Martyn Day has helped thousands of local people, businesses, and groups. Almost everyone in these communities will know someone he has assisted.His tireless dedication to public service has been evident in his representation of our communities at Westminster, and throughout his time as a councillor before that. Now he is asking for your support tocontinue being the experienced voice our communities need and deserve.Martyn has always put the communities he serves first. Martyn sees every enquiry emailed to him, and has always been available in person to constituents by way of appointments, surgeries and home visits.In Parliament, Martyn has been a passionate advocate for human rights issues, has advocated for animal free research and has fought for pension justice.Throughout his life, Martyn has campaigned resolutely for independence. Now, as 14 years of austerity, Brexit and the Cost-of-Living crisis have all been inflicted on Scotland by Westminster, it is time for Scotland to take full control which is only available with independence. On 4th July, vote for Independence DAY.

I got into politics to make a difference. Becoming homeless at 16 showed me how fragile the system can be, how easily people can fall through the cracks and how vital it is to have someone fighting your corner.

Living in central Scotland, I see how much people are struggling. They feel like they’re stuck on a permanent waiting list, from NHS treatment to affordable housing.

As a Liberal Democrat, I am committed to giving people the representation they deserve. That means fixing our broken health service, tackling the housing crisis, lifting up local economies and delivering the best education for our children.

I don’t consider myself a typical politician. I’ve seen life’s challenges up close and personal, and that’s what motivates me as a parliamentary candidate. I want to change things for the better and get results for the people of Central Scotland and Lothians West.

I was born in 1993.  My father a farmer and my mother a civil servant.  I went to Larbert Village primary school and Larbert High School then on to Oatridge Agricultural College.  I met my wife Cheryl in 2015 and we wed in May 2023.

Since I was old enough to be useful I have farmed in Larbert, Denny and other parts of the Falkirk area.  When I left college I worked full time on the family farm, becoming a partner in the farm business with my Dad.  Since Dad sadly passed away in 2018 I have run the farm business on my own account, expanding it with the addition of more livestock and rented and contract-farmed ground.

We are a mixed animal/arable operation but have diversified into other enterprises such as selling pre-fabricated buildings and internal stabling.  Our contract winter maintenance business lasted until high fuel costs made it uneconomic.

I may only be 30 but having been brought up on the farm and having been lucky enough to have had a father who gave me responsibility for running the farm has given me an excellent grasp of the business and personal struggles of the real world. This is something our current batch of MP’s seem to be severely lacking!

Why I’m standing for Reform UK Scotland.

I first became political in the run up to the independence referendum in 2014. I hated how the Yes campaign split our country in two.

I was pro Brexit in the 2016 EU referendum as I believed, and still do, that Britain is at its best when fully self-governing.

After the Conservatives’ mismanagement of both Brexit and the Covid scare, abandoning their core values, and the SNP dividing and running Scotland into the ground, I now believe it’s time for ordinary people like you and me to take our country back from the career politicians and the “Indy Ref or bust” crew.  Please join me and help to Take Our Country Back.

John Beare has lived in Markinch since 1998 and has represented the area as a local councillor since 2006. He was elected to Fife Council in a by-election with a 30% swing from the incumbent Labour Party. He has had several leadership roles at the Council since 2007, including terms as Convener of the Standards and Audit Committee and the Planning Committee. He is currently an opposition member of the Cabinet regularly challenging the Labour/Tory/Liberal coalition that runs the Council, while also providing practical solutions to issues facing communities across Fife. He is also the Fife SNP Group Business Manager.Since 2015 John has been Office Manager for Peter Grant, the outgoing MP for Glenrothes, having previously worked for an MSP. Prior to this he had a long career in commercial construction, aiding the construction of schools, hospitals, television studios, factories and clean rooms across these islands.John has held many roles in the SNP including as Organisation Convener of the Party, a member of the National Executive Committee, Convener of the SNP Candidate Assessment Committee, Agent for the SNP for several elections and most proudly as the Yes Scotland Agent for Fife in 2014.John has a long political CV, strong local knowledge, experience of Holyrood and Westminster, and a history of hard work and complete dedication to realising Independence for Scotland.

From growing up on a council estate in a working family needing benefits to smashing glass ceilings at The Scotsman, I am proof of the power of Labour governments. I'm a councillor and a campaigner who has been one of the leading voices in changing the UK landscape around menopause. I've worked with everyone from politicians and peers to prison officers and foodbank staff, been named a Big Issue Changemaker and appeared in national and international media, TV and radio. I have created real change in both attitudes and law. But I'm still a working woman and I know the challenges that face each and every one of us -- but I also know that a better way is possible. I've lived it. The SNP and the Tories have nothing left to give Angus & Perthshire Glens. Only Scottish Labour can deliver the change Scotland needs. It's time for a fresh start and a fresh voice.

My name is Senga Beresford, a businesswoman with over 25 years of experience in small business management.

Having lived in Dumfries and Galloway all my life, I care deeply about our communities and the future of working families across Scotland.

I entered politics because I’m tired of ordinary people being ignored and let down by those in power. I want to change that. I believe in lower taxes, putting more money in the pockets of working people, and creating a system that truly supports those who contribute. I want better education for all children, stronger support for young people, and the return of meaningful apprenticeships.

I strongly support our farmers, who are vital to our rural economy and food security, and I believe they must be backed to succeed.

I stand for fairness, free speech, and opportunity for all. As a married mother of four, everything I do is driven by a determination to build a better, more secure future for the next generation.

I’m not a career politician. I spent years as a diplomat, working across four continents in three languages. I always put in the hours behind the scenes to do the job properly. That’s the same commitment I’d bring to representing this area. And then, home called. We came back. And after working for our country overseas, I want to represent our home in Holyrood.

My politics is rooted in my faith and community. I helped set up our church’s food bank, campaigned for affordable housing as a community organiser (we secured 115 co-op homes), and volunteered in a night shelter. One guest asked to be woken at 5am to get to work. That stuck with me. A job—and still homeless. That crystallised a lot.

And with Labour, the clue’s in the name. Work should be fair and dignified. That’s why I’m proud the UK Labour Government is pushing the Employment Rights Bill.

But Labour’s also the party of social justice. I don’t want to just patch things up with food banks and shelters. I want to build a society where they’re not needed in the first place. Like every member, I want to make those things unnecessary.

As a Cooperative Party member too, I believe in real community power. From energy to land, we should be expanding community ownership—and making sure community benefit is felt widely and fairly.

Candidate Statement Tracey Warman
I live in Dumfries and Galloway with my partner and can think of nowhere I would rather
be than in this corner of the world we call home.
I volunteer in our local community shop once a week, which is hugely satisfying and is a
great way of getting to know the locals as well as visitors to our area. It is really the hub of
local life . This year I was also elected to serve on the Community Council and am very
much looking forward to see what avenues this may take me down.
I worked in finance for 33 years, and took early retirement in 2019. I now spend my time
as a relief dairy worker, concentrating mainly on calf rearing at farms across the area. I en-
joy going to the agricultural shows throughout the summer.
I also run an Airbnb house share. We have been very lucky to have met some lovely peo-
ple from across the world by doing this.
I enjoy swimming in the sea and in 2023 was one of the founding members of a women’s
swimming group. We meet regularly for swims throughout the year. Our group has been a
great way of forging friendships through a shared joy of being in the water. I have also al-
ways enjoyed long distance running and have completed marathons and half marathons
across the UK, Europe and Africa
I have voted Liberal since the age of 18 and have been a member of the Liberal Democ-
rats since 2011and joined the Scottish Liberal Democrats in 2017.
My aim is to give a voice to the local communities in West Dumfriesshire and Galloway.
As a resident of Galloway, I feel I have gained a good understanding of the community in
which I live and now I want to listen to your views, concerns and needs - to learn what is
important to you and to bring your views to the attention of those that can get your voice
heard. The Liberal Democrats, in their various guises and long history have been instru-
mental in empowering local communities through a community approach, which is so of-
ten ignored by the other parties.
As a Liberal Democrat, I believe in the liberal qualities of fairness and equality

"My heart didn't start in Scotland, but it found its home here—and my soul found its peace."
In 2011, I did more than just move; I voted with my feet. I brought my family here because I fell in love with a Scotland that dared to dream of a different, fairer course. I am a Muslim and a ‘New Scot’ by choice, and every day I am inspired by the rugged beauty of this land and the unbreakable spirit of its people.
I carry the radical fire of Jimmy Reid, John MacLean, and Mary Barbour. But I also carry the wisdom of my mentors: the late Alex Salmond, who showed us how to lead with a vision of a sovereign nation, and George Galloway, who reminds us that the working class deserves a voice that refuses to be silenced.
My roots are in the pits, steel plants, and shipyards of the North East of England, woven into the fabric of the trade union movement. As an award-winning journalist and war correspondent, I’ve spent my life uncovering the truths the powerful want buried. I’ve stared death in the face—locked up by the Taliban while pursuing a story, and later kidnapped by heavily armed Israeli forces on a humanitarian flotilla bringing food to Gaza. Held as a hostage in K'ziot prison, I went on a 7-day hunger strike for my dignity. I know what it means to fight when the world is looking the other way.
Today, I am standing in the heart of Glasgow Cathcart and Pollok, a constituency steeped in the grit and glory of working-class Glasgow. From the industrial history of the White Cart Water to the defiant spirit of the Pollok Free State, this is a place that knows how to stand its ground. I see a political establishment that has lost its way, and I see communities here being left behind by young, career politicians who have never known a day's struggle in their puff. That is why I am standing for the Workers Party Scotland.
We are the party of peace in a time of warmongering. No to NATO, no to conscription, no to nukes, and a definitive 'no' to the foreign wars that drain our treasury while our own people shiver.
We are the party of common sense and dignity. We will kill the GRR Bill to protect the hard-won rights of women and the safety of our daughters. We will stand as a shield for our pensioners, ensuring those who built this country are never forced to sell their homes just to survive their final years in care.
I am not a career politician; I am a worker, a mother, and a journalist who is tired of waiting for change. I am choosing to be that change. Scotland’s future shouldn't belong to the bureaucrats in Brussels or the bankers in London—it belongs to the workers, the builders, and the dreamers of Cathcart and Pollok who keep this country moving.
Support me and I will be your voice—I have a strong voice, and I ken how to use it. For a Scotland that is sovereign, socialist, and at peace: let's put the power back where it belongs ... with the people.

"I am delighted to be standing in the Glasgow North seat. I was born and bred in the West End and my children are at schools in the area. These communities mean a lot to me and I want them to fulfil their potential. I have a positive message for voters in Glasgow North. It is about focusing on what really matters to them like investing in local infrastructure, keeping our communities safe from crime, and standing up for struggling businesses, rather than a SNP MP who will only be focused on their independence obsession."

Statement- Paul Kennedy For 30 years, I served in a wide variety of roles with Strathclyde Police Force, covering areas in Glasgow and West Dunbartonshire, and I now work as a mathematics teacher at a secondary school.  The late Charles Kennedy, former leader of the Liberal Democrats, was instrumental in introducing me, his older cousin, to politics.  Those experiences have shown me that elected office should be about public service and making a real difference to peoples' lives.  That's exactly what Liberal Democrats believe too. We believe that everyone should have faster access to see their GP and have access to an NHS dentist. We believe that teachers and pupils should feel properly supported so that we can make our schools world-class again. We believe in protecting our beautiful natural environments by ending the scandal of sewage dumping. Above all, I want the people of West Dunbartonshire to feel represented again. A vote for a Liberal Democrat is a vote to make that happen.

Alison Thewliss is standing for the Scottish Parliament Constituency of Glasgow Central, and tops the SNP Glasgow Regional List.

She was elected as an MP in May 2015 and was re-elected in 2017 and 2019. Alison has served as both Shadow Chancellor and as the SNP Spokesperson for Home Affairs. She stood for the redrawn Glasgow North constituency in the 2024 General Election, but was unsuccessful.

Alison is interested in issues around immigration and economic justice, particularly for women. She has also been a vocal advocate for changes to drug policy in the UK and is well known for her high profile campaign on the scrapping the two-child limit on Universal Credit and Working Tax Credits. She established and Chaired the All Party Parliamentary Group (APPG) on Infant Feeding and Inequalities, Chaired the APPG on Immigration Detention and the APPG for Working at Height. Prior to her election as an MP, she served as Glasgow City Councillor.  Alison lives in Glasgow with her husband and 2 children.

If elected MSP for Glasgow Kelvin and Maryhill, I pledge to work diligently for all my constituents. As I have done in the previous parliamentary session I will seek to advance causes which will help deliver a more socially just and environmentally sustainable Scotland.

In the last parliament I directly campaigned and helped deliver various achievements such as extending new born baby screening for Spinal Muscular Dystrophy  (SMA) and securing additional funds for our hospices and palliative care.

In the next parliament, tackling child poverty, delivering more affordable homes, and supporting families with increased childcare will all continue to be key priorities. I also aim to ensure **record levels of investment in the NHS** to continue to bring down waiting lists and to extend a new **network of GP Hubs** to make it easier to see a GP.

I want to protect the gains made by our SNP Government such as **abolishing prescription charges, offering free university tuition, reducing child poverty** (including our Scottish Child Payment), extending free school meals and maintaining extensive support for Scotland's small businesses.

I will continue to champion net zero initiatives which will also help tackle fuel poverty and improve the quality of our housing stock. I will also play my part to help ensure Scotland is a modern, vibrant and diverse society at home and speaks up on the international stage for the rule of law and against the breaking of international law which amongst other things has shamefully led to genocide in Palestine.

Ultimately though only through the full powers of an **independent Scotland** will we fully see the transformation of Scotland we all wan

I’m not a career politician — I’m someone who understands real life and real struggles in Glasgow.

I’ve seen how hard people work just to keep up, while the cost of living rises and services don’t improve the way they should. Families deserve better. Communities deserve to be heard.

This isn’t about politics as usual — it’s about honesty, fairness, and real accountability.
I’m here to listen, to stand with you, and to push for real change that actually makes a difference in people’s lives.

Because leadership should serve people — not ignore them.

Holly is a keen cyclist and feminist activist who is determined to make Langside work for people and planet.

Holly has campaigned for more bike storage facilities and segregated infrastructure. She has also produced research and advocated for Glasgow's streets, parks and buses to be safer, more accessible, affordable and convenient for everyone.

Holly is of the founders of the Reclaim Our Lanes group, which shares knowledge and has built a network to help residents clean up their lanes and backcourts.

Holly is a culture policy researcher fighting for more funding and stability for artists, musicians, makers and freelancers. As the culture spokesperson for the Glasgow Greens' campaign, she also wants to ensure better opportunities for women and non-binary people in sport, and seeks to protect grassroots culture venues.

Holly's priorities for Langside are based on hundreds of chats with local residents throughout her campaign. She is determined to:

- Fight for better funding for street cleaning, more recycling opportunities, waste collections and tackling fly tipping.

- Invest in pavement accessibility, road maintenance and improved lighting to ensure safer streets for all.

- Champion walking, wheeling and cycling by supporting school bike buses and free cycle storage.

- Amplify residents' voices in planning applications like Shawlands Arcade and Mount Florida Bowling Club.

- Work to bring rent controls to Langside and advocate for more support for tenement repairs.
Phone number: +447771127918

I am standing as the candidate for real change in Glasgow Southside.

If elected, I aim to bring meaningful improvements to our local public services.

I have lived in Glasgow for most of my adult life and in the heart of the city for the last 15 years, where I have been involved in my local Community council.

I have seen, first hand, the impact of poor management by both Labour and the SNP.

I am a retired Chartered Accountant and successful businessman, I have the experience and the tenacity to deliver for our Community.

Now is the time to take the action, which will make Glasgow and Scotland once again the great place that I grew up in. Together lets create a safer and better environment for future generations.

For too long the establishment parties of the LibLabCon SNP and Greens and delivered their top down dictates to the people of Scotland which bear no resemblance to serving the interests of the Scottish people.  We believe that people have the right to freedom of expression, to work hard and keep the majority of their own money rather than politicians wasting it on vanity projects and globalist net zero green agendas which do nothing to help the local community. That is why I am standing for the Hamilton, Larkhall and Stonehouse constituency and the South of Scotland List with the Alliance for Democracy and Freedom. If elected I will work hard to hold elected representatives to account and deliver for the people of Scotland.

I’m Davy Russell, and I’m proud to be standing as Scottish Labour’s candidate in the upcoming by-election for Hamilton, Stonehouse and Larkhall.
I’ve lived here my whole life. I’m a dad – and a granddad – and like many of you, I care deeply about the future of our community.

I’m not a politician. I’ve never stood for election before. But I’m standing in this by-election because this is where I was born, where I live, and where I’ve raised my family. For me, it’s the best place in the world. I want to be a champion in parliament for this place and the people I’ve grown up with and lived alongside my entire life.

I was born in Quarter, and went to school in Hamilton. I started my career as a roads trainee in 1980 and ended up as a director at a local council. I’ve worked my entire life to improve the services that we all rely on. Today, I support people with disabilities, help run a local hospice, and I’m honoured to represent His Majesty as Deputy Lord Lieutenant in Lanarkshire.

Like you, I’ve had enough of the way things are going with the SNP. I’m standing for one reason: to get things done for our community. We need to fix our NHS – too many people have had to wait too long for the care they deserve. We need to tackle the SNP’s housing crisis – so families here aren’t stuck in damp flats or waiting years for a home they can barely afford. And we need to breathe life back into our town centres – investment in the places we all use and used to be proud of. After 18 years, the SNP are out of ideas and out of touch. If they had a plan to fix things here we’d have seen it by now. This by-election is a chance for all of us to send the SNP a message: enough is enough.

I’ll always fight for our communities and put Hamilton, Larkhall and Stonehouse first.

I’m ready to work for you – and fight for you – every single day.

Malcolm Offord is the Reform UK candidate for Inverclyde, bringing experience from both business and government to his campaign. He is standing to offer a clear alternative to the policies of the established parties, with a focus on practical, common-sense solutions that put local people first.

He is campaigning for lower taxes, economic growth, and an end to policies that drive up the cost of living. Malcolm is committed to representing the people of Inverclyde, supporting local businesses, and ensuring that the area is no longer overlooked by those in power.

- Lower taxes to help working people keep more of what they earn
- End costly Net Zero measures that push up bills
- Stand up for free speech and common-sense policies
- Back local businesses and create real economic growth

I am absolutely honoured to have been selected as the Scottish Liberal Democrat candidate for Inverness and Nairn. This is a community I care deeply about and I am determined to give local people the strong voice they deserve.

Sport, culture and community have always been at the heart of my life. Whether on the rugby pitch, representing Scotland in Australian Rules Football, or championing grassroots clubs and local causes, I have seen first-hand the power of people coming together to make a difference. My background in leadership and campaigning has shown me how much can be achieved when local voices are heard — from standing up for investment in our roads to pushing for the services our area needs.

Too often, decisions made in Edinburgh have left communities across Inverness and Nairn at the back of the queue, with healthcare under strain, roads not fit for purpose, and families and businesses struggling to get the fair deal they deserve. I want to change that.

With every ounce of energy I have, I will fight to put Inverness and Nairn first: pressing for investment in healthcare and education, improving infrastructure like the A9 and A96, and ensuring our young people have the opportunities to stay, work and thrive here at home. Politics should be about listening as much as leading, and in the months ahead I will be out in our towns and villages, hearing from people about their hopes and concerns and working to turn them into real action.

At the next election, we have the chance to do things differently. With your support, we can build a fairer, stronger and more connected Inverness and Nairn, and I am ready to work tirelessly to make that happen.

Scotland has an historic chance for change on May 7th. We have a plan to turn the tide on decline, local to national.

Government policy touches all issues from homes to schools to health. My particular concerns are on the economy, education and housing.

We don’t want a sprawl of new wee boxes with no community. Tornagrain is setting the benchmark for well designed, soundly constructed homes with community. Public buildings like the new Inverness prison must be value for money. We need to reduce the waste of taxpayers’ money.

My work experience is in architecture, teaching and writing novels.

I am a son of the soil, my ancestors were the thanes of Cawdor, they served kings and queens, I hope to serve you.

Statement to voters:
"I'm Shaun Fraser, I'm Inverness born & bred, and I'm your Scottish Labour candidate at the Scottish parliament election in May.

I don't come to politics through a conventional route - my background is as a self-employed artist, and before that I worked in homelessness prevention for local charity New Start Highland.

It was because of the experiences that I have through working for New Start that I first got involved in politics. The corrosive austerity agenda of the Tory-Lib Dem UK coalition of the time left a profound impression on me.

I could see that whether we like it or not, politics touches on all of our lives, the good and the bad, and I want to do whatever I can to steer things in the direction of compassion.

That's why I'm standing at this election. I'll be on your ballot paper in May as a principled voice for change for Inverness & Nairn".

Passionately British! I have spent most of my working life in the United Kingdom, mainly in the South of England, but with a love for Scotland. I say that because for 25 years my family and I spent every summer in the Highlands.

My business life started after retiring from a 30-year service in law enforcement, and continued after my wife and I moved abroad in 2006.

We returned to the UK in May 2020 and settled in the Highlands. This was an easy decision despite the threat of the SNP’s obsessive call for Independence.

Returning during the early days of the Covid epidemic and lockdown begged the question, what was the logic in locking people in their own homes, closing schools, and locking the doors of business? We, our children, and grandchildren suffer the consequences of those political decisions today and for the foreseeable future.

Why I am standing as the Reform Prospective Parliamentary Candidate for Moray West, Nairn and Strathspey.

I have always been interested in politics but believe now is the time to address the enormous problems this United Kingdom face, the country is BROKEN!

The SNP have shown that they are not worthy of continuing in government.

The Conservative Party in its current form is over. A Labour government will only make the current situation worse.

Reform UK is the only party that offers true change to the current impasse, we need urgent reform across the breadth of government policy. Direct, concise action to quickly bring Scotland and the rest of the United Kingdom back on course.

Reform’s policies on key issues are needed to revive our economy and restore healthy communities.

Immigration is a serious issue; we cannot sustain the constant stream of immigrants into our nation without serious consequence to those already here.

Economy is under strain, growth is vital, higher tax revenues can be invested back into public services such as the NHS.

Net Zero Targets will make us poorer; it adds extra costs to consumers and businesses. We need to get our key utility companies under control, consider renationalizing key industries such as water.

Tax Reform to free up those people on lower wages from paying income tax and corporation tax reform for businesses.

Housing needs urgent attention; we need to review the planning system and prioritise local people who have paid into the system above and beyond those foreign nationals who jump the housing queues in most parts of the UK.

The Scottish Conservative Party has announced the selection of Councillor Ruraidh Stewart as the party’s candidate for Inverness and Nairn in the 2026 Scottish Parliament election. The announcement was made at a well-attended meeting of local members in Inverness.

Born and raised in the Highlands, Ruraidh Stewart has spent his life living and working in the region. A respected figure in Highland politics, he made history in 2022 by becoming the first Conservative elected to represent Skye on Highland Council. As Leader of the Conservative Group on the Council, Stewart has earned a reputation for principled, energetic leadership and his ability to connect across communities with a practical, people-first approach.

"I am honoured to have been selected as the Scottish Conservative candidate for Inverness and Nairn," said Mr Stewart. "Other than my time studying in St Andrews, the Highlands have always been home. I've lived here and worked here and  I understand the challenges we face but I also see the resilience, pride, and potential in our communities and I’m committed to standing up for them in Holyrood."

Mr Stewart’s campaign will focus on restoring trust in government, reversing centralisation, and delivering for Highland communities. He has consistently opposed SNP policies that he argues ignore the distinct needs of the Highlands.

"Our communities deserve support that’s flexible, local, and respectful of their unique needs. Sadly, we’ve seen too much centralisation under the SNP—decisions being made in Edinburgh that simply don’t fit the realities of life in the Highlands. It’s time to bring power and trust back to our local areas, where we know what works best for our families, our services, and our future."

"Let’s be honest about where things stand," said Mr Stewart. "The A9 was supposed to be fully dualled by 2025—now we’re being told it might not be finished until 2035, or even later. At Raigmore Hospital, one in five patients are waiting more than 12 weeks just to be seen as outpatients. NHS Highland is under real strain, with staffing shortages reaching 25% in some of our most remote communities. Meanwhile, roads continue to deteriorate and ferries sit idle in shipyards, all while SNP ministers seem more focused on spinning headlines than actually fixing the problems that matter to people here in the Highlands."

"People in Inverness, Nairn, and across the Highlands aren’t asking for the moon. They just want roads that are safe, healthcare that’s accessible, and local services that actually work. They deserve a government that listens, acts, and delivers."

"While the SNP obsesses over yet another divisive referendum, they’ve taken their eye off the ball. It’s Highland families who are left picking up the pieces."

With deep roots in the Highlands, a proven record of principled leadership, and a commitment to restoring common-sense government, Ruraidh Stewart offers a dynamic and credible alternative to the status quo at Holyrood.

Hello my name is Michael Gregori and I’m standing as your Scottish Liberal Democrat candidate because I believe our community deserves better than the tired politics we’ve been left with. For too long, the SNP and the Conservatives have let people down — neglecting local services, centralising decision-making, and failing to deliver the change Scotland needs.
Labour themselves has shown through their Westminster Party that they can’t be trusted by scandals and their flip flop antics on important policy.
The Liberal Democrats offer something different: a positive, practical vision for Kilmarnock and Irvine Valley. We will put fairness, opportunity, and community at the heart of everything we do. That means fighting for better healthcare close to home, supporting our schools and young people, tackling the climate crisis with real action, and ensuring local voices are heard in every decision that affects us.
I’m committed to being a strong, accessible representative who listens and acts. I will always put people before party politics, and I will work tirelessly to improve the lives of everyone in our constituency.
It’s time for fresh leadership. It’s time to move beyond SNP excuses and Tory neglect. It’s time for a fairer, greener, and more hopeful future with the Scottish Liberal Democrats.

party_description_text: Alliance to Liberate Scotland For Independence
statement_to_voters:
My name is Garry Campbell McClay, and I’m from Kilmarnock, Scotland.

I’ve spent my working life in the skilled trades and small business sector and currently work as a self-employed tradesman. Through my work I’ve seen first-hand the challenges faced by working people, families, and small businesses across Scotland
.
Outside of work I’ve always enjoyed being active in sport and community life, taking part in football, boxing, golf, wrestling, and the Highland Games to name a few. I’m also a father, which is one of the main reasons I care deeply about the future of our country and the opportunities the next generation will have.

My priorities are to recognise Scotland’s popular sovereignty so the people in the communities have a real voice in shaping the future of an independent Scotland. I want to protect Scotland’s identity and culture, strengthen our economy and infrastructure, and tackle the serious issues affecting healthcare, education, and the justice system. I also believe in supporting the farming and energy sector so Scotland can become more self-sufficient and reduce everyday costs without being dependent on other countries.

I’m now standing for Alliance to Liberate Scotland in the Kilmarnock and Irvine Valley constituency on May 7th 2026 to be a voice for local people and help bring the change our constituency needs to lead by example.

The party allows me to stay self funded and have my independent views with no political party whip apart from that we all unite for Scottish independence - nothing else, nothing less. This is why I decided to join ATLS as we can't make the changes we need without having full control of our own country 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿

I am standing as a candidate to give something back to the community which shaped my early life.

If elected my aim would be to improve local public services as an absolute priority.

Born and educated in Kilmarnock, I have always maintained strong ties to the area, where much of my family and many friends still live. Since retiring, I have spent more time in the constituency.

I joined Reform UK in December 2024 and bring a professional background in law, having graduated with honours from the University of Glasgow, qualified as a solicitor, and spent over 20 years as a principal in private practice.

Alongside my career I have volunteered internationally teaching English as a foreign language and also been a volunteer for my local community council.

Fraser Graham, a Chartered Architect, is the Scottish Liberal Democrat candidate for the Cowdenbeath and Kirkcaldy Constituency. Growing up in Fife, Fraser has deep roots in the area and a strong commitment to serving his community. His family continues to reside within the constituency, further strengthening his personal connection to the region.

Fraser's professional career as an architect is marked by a dedication to sustainable and community-focused design, reflecting his belief in the power of thoughtful, innovative solutions to improve lives.

With a passion for social justice, environmental sustainability, and economic development, Fraser brings a fresh and dynamic perspective to the political landscape. His campaign is driven by a desire to create inclusive, thriving communities where everyone can succeed.

Fraser's candidacy for the Cowdenbeath and Kirkcaldy Constituency embodies the values of the Scottish Liberal Democrats: fairness, opportunity, and a commitment to building a better future for all. He is eager to leverage his professional skills and personal dedication to effect positive change and represent the interests of all residents.

I’m standing for Reform UK in Mid Fife and Glenrothes because I believe in putting the Scottish people first.

For too long, the SNP and Labour have treated the people of this constituency like an irrelevance. It is my goal to end that.

As a young person, I have the energy and commitment to represent your interests in the Scottish Parliament. I have put in hard work before, cleaning community centres and serving over shop counters, before becoming involved in academia and politics.

I am the only candidate that will stand up for your values of Family, Community and Country.

For 25 years, Scotland has endured high spending and declining outcomes. Despite a steady increase in taxes, hard-working families face rising costs, failing public services and stagnant economic growth.

With a background is in investment and financial management, including as an economic policy advisor at the Bank of England, I believe Scotland’s current challenges are the result of poor fiscal management and misguided policies.

I am standing to help Reform UK dislodge the SNP and bring about change – restoring economic growth, creating well-paid jobs, and ensuring Scotland has the resources to deliver high-quality public services once again.

It is an honour to be selected as the Scottish Labour candidate for Berwickshire, Roxburgh and Selkirk. After years of two failing, chaotic Governments, I will be a strong voice for Berwickshire, Roxburgh and Selkirk in a UK Labour Government. Only Labour can boot the Tories out of Westminster and every vote counts. I will be a positive voice for the Borders delivering Labour's plan to improve public services, save our NHS, improve wages, and lift kids out of poverty.

Why I’m standing

Having campaigned across Midlothian South, Tweeddale and Lauderdale, I have spoken to many  voters who tell me the same things: difficulty seeing a GP, roads full of potholes, inadequate public transport, too few opportunities for young people, and pensioners waiting too long for the care and services they rely on.

The SNP government has failed Scotland. Economic and social decline has become normalised and our communities deserve better.

It doesn’t have to be this way.

Scotland needs a government focused on delivery and on doing the right thing for the people we seek to represent. That is why I am standing as a candidate in this election.

My ambition is to be part of the next Scottish Labour Government that restores trust in politics, invests in the NHS, improves educational standards and creates real opportunities for young people across the country.

I know first-hand the difference a Labour government can make. Labour policies gave my family security and gave me the opportunity to go to university and build a career as an economist. I want to ensure everyone has the same life chances I had.

This election is about Scotland. It is about investment, jobs and housing. It is about getting Scotland moving in the right direction. After 18 years of SNP failure, I want to play my part in turning Scotland around. In May, I want to see a positive Scotland emerge. To do that, I need your support, and I ask you to consider giving me your vote for the constituency and a Labour vote on the list.

Please get in touch or fill out the survey at survey.labour.org.uk/mstl

My family has lived in East Lothian for generations. I was born on a hill sheep farm in the Highlands and have lived in Dunbar for nearly 6 years with my wife and our two kids, who go to local schools. I coach and referee at Dunbar mini rugby and volunteer in projects working for children in the care system. I want to be your MP because I deeply care about our community and believe in taking real action to make our lives better.

For most of the last fifteen years, I’ve been the CEO of Who Cares? Scotland. Where I fought for children in care who have been dealt the worst hand. Under my leadership we didn’t just talk about change—we made it happen. We showed that with care and action, we can transform lives. I’ve spent my life making a difference, from promoting democracy in Lithuania to tackling poverty in India and Ghana and building peace across former-Yugoslavia.

I’ve seen firsthand what happens when people fall through the cracks through no fault of their own—it’s unnecessary and unacceptable.

I want to be your MP, as I’ve seen too many politicians make empty promises, but they mostly get in the way instead of helping us thrive. The Liberal Democrats are different. We are fundamentally liberal. We respect people and will empower you to make a difference in your lives.

We have a local growing economy, but successive governments just interfere and make it harder for our businesses to thrive. We need policies that support growth without getting in the way. Small businesses are the backbone of our community, and they need fewer obstacles and more support.

Local green energy successes like the Berwick Bank wind farm should do more to benefit the local communities directly.

Schools should have the freedom to innovate and help our children succeed. Our healthcare system must be accessible and local, especially for end-of-life care. It’s unacceptable that community hospitals in Dunbar and North Berwick are closing, and this must be urgently addressed.

As your MP, I will deliver results. I’ve got a proven track record. I’ve delivered progressive solutions on budget and on time, showing that experience and passion can make a real difference.

It’s time we had quality people in the House of Commons, people who are competent and effective. I care about East Lothian and its people. I care about East Lothian’s stunning environment. I care about this constituency and will represent you faithfully. Let’s take action together.

I was born and raised in Glasgow and in 2005 came to work in the Borders on a 6 month contract in IT. I liked it so much I moved here permanently in 2010.

My background is in newspaper advertising and IT. I have worked in the Borders now for almost 20 years, latterly supporting schools IT in the area and concentrating on Galashiels primaries up till 2016. I am currently still working in IT for a local company.

I am living and working in the Borders as I love the people here. They are very friendly and community minded with good family values. I particularly enjoy local traditions, including the common ridings which are a great and unique feature of this beautiful area.

Why I am standing for Reform

I joined Reform as I felt none of the current parties were working in the interest of the public and were just coming up with the same old ideas and policies. Nor were they supporting traditional British and Scottish values. I was particularly annoyed at the neglect of the farming industry by successive governments. I feel it is so important that we produce our own food. Everyone needs to eat!!

What Reform can do for the Borders

Net Zero policies and too much red tape has damaged our farming industry. Reform wants farmers with farms under 500 Ha to be able to manage their own land the way they know best. We also want to encourage more young people into farming.

There is a severe lack of affordable housing for Borderers. Local housing associations are under increasing pressure from people looking for homes. I would like to help young people remain in the rural areas in which they grew up by encouraging them to take up a trade to help build and restore homes as well as build their own future and that of the Borders. Reform UK Scotland’s small business policies will encourage new entrepreneurs to help regenerate Border Businesses and generate more jobs.

The NHS is the biggest employer in the Borders but there is a severe shortage of medical staff. Reform will bring people back into NHS frontline medicine by putting them on zero basic rate tax for three years, and writing off their student loans after 10 years of NHS service or pro rata depending on length of service.

Calum Kerr is the SNP MP for Berwickshire, Roxburgh and Selkirk and the party's Lead Westminster Spokesperson on the Environment, Food and Rural Affairs. He has established a reputation as an effective parliamentarian and a powerful and passionate campaigner on constituency issues. These include improving transport and communications, boosting the Borders economy and fighting poverty, austerity and welfare cuts.

Calum is a strong advocate of a better deal for Scotland's farmers and the rural economy, including maximising support from the EU and improving the supply chain to obtain better prices for primary producers.

Calum's passion for politics found its voice when he became Chair of Yes Scottish Borders in the run-up to the 2014 referendum. A dynamic and tireless organiser, he drove the campaign right across the region, achieving an unprecedented 45 per cent increase in support.

A history graduate from St Andrews University, Calum was born and raised in the Scottish Borders and lives locally with his wife and three children. Family holidays are enjoyed in the beautiful North West Highlands and hobbies include spending time with friends, rugby and the odd good malt whisky.

I am a tree surgeon living in Moray.  I am standing for Labour because we are the party of working people.  I want the Scottish Government to use the full powers of devolution in all areas from taxation to land reform and public ownership of renewables.  I am standing for better public services, urgent action on climate change, fairer distribution of wealth, and against all forms of hatred and division.

Born and raised in Wishaw and married to Jessie (49 years) with one daughter, Duncan has had two separate successful business with a break between to gain a BA Accounting at Napier University (1994).

First business venture was owning and running a MOT testing garage for 12 years before University and then a complete change by doing Small Business accounts working mainly from home. Further studies allowed him to act as a Financial / Mortgage Advisor which continued within his new venture, namely estate agency, opening in late 2001.

After more than 40 years of self-employment, Duncan retired in 2021 during the pandemic. Always being interested in politics Duncan joined Reform UK in August 2024 and has been an active member since. He is sure that 2026 will be a defining moment in Scottish politics, with Reform UK making massive inroads and breaking up the cosy status quo.

party_description_text: Alliance to Liberate Scotland For Independence

party_name: Alliance to Liberate Scotland and Sovereignty
statement_to_voters:
Kenneth Mackenzie is the Sovereignty candidate for Na h-Eileanan an Iar (Western Isles) and in the Highlands & Islands electoral region. Kenneth is a 61-year-old father of two daughters in their twenties, ex-Army in his twenties, has worked as a labourer in Arnish, Harris tweed weaver, firefighter at Stornoway Airport, and is currently a self-employer taxi driver.

He joined the SNP in 2002 (SNP voter since the mid 80’s) after his second daughter was born with the belief, they were the party to guide us to Independence, but left in 2020 after Nicola’s capitulation speech, then joined and subsequently left the Alba Party because of their position on freeports. Having lost all faith in the SNP he is "determined not to reward these gravy train devolutionist's with my vote".

After joining Sovereignty, he accepted our invitation to stand as the party’s candidate in Na h-Eileanan Iar against Alasdair Allan MSP.

Having taken early retirement from Stornoway Airport, and making major lifestyle changes to regain his health and fitness, Kenneth is focus on fighting for his daughters’ future by fighting for Scotland’s future.

“No more GRR crap or the other stuff they put ahead of Independence…” the SNP are currently sitting on the GRR bill waiting to put it through again after the election but Kenneth promises, “Enough is enough". It’s time to send a clear and unambiguous message to the SNP and the Yes movement that Scotland’s Independence is the highest priority if we are to survive this corrupt, gender denying, colonising, thieving UK. We must put people in place who will fight for Scotland’s freedom and prepare the Scottish Parliament to assist Salvo with the decolonisation campaign in the UN, not those who will bend the knee to Westminster as the current devolutionist SNP party are doing.”

“Sovereignty are working with ISP and I4I candidates as part of Alliance to Liberate Scotland, demonstrating the unity Scotland requires unlike the SNP who oppose any other independence parties.”

Kenneth Mackenzie

SAOR ALBA

Born the eldest of eleven siblings, Malcolm and his wife of 37 years, have three children and five grand children.

Brought up predominantly on a small holding near Aberfeldy, in 1994 they moved to Lewis, where both sets of parents still reside. Malcolm studied and worked in the horticultural industry before the couple took on the care of his sister with additional needs, subsequently becoming foster carers for the local authority.

Through local committees, music, crofting and the local church he has got to know the community.

The decline in the way the country has been governed, and the disdain with which the main stream parties treat the voting public, has inspired him to get involved with ReformUK Scotland.

If elected he would prioritise ‘Family, Community and Country’ backed by the old Stornoway Town Council motto ‘Gods Providence is our Inheritance’.

I am married to a Highland lass from Easter Ross; we have two grown up sons and two young grandsons. In 1988 we moved to the Highlands and built a house in the Dingwall area where we still live. I have worked in the construction sector for most of my working life and as such have travelled extensively. My oldest son was born in Hong Kong and my youngest finished the last three years of his schooling in New York.
For the last twenty years I have been a self-employed consultant providing constructability and budgetary advice, estimating and planning for underground structures and deep foundations. During that time, I have been involved in projects such as Thames Tideway (London’s Super Sewer), Crossrail, HS2, Sizewell C and Lower Thames Crossing in the UK and The World Trade Centre, Fulton Street Transit Centre, and California High Speed Rail in the USA.
I invented a system for simplifying the construction and improving the performance of a specialised geotechnical process and together with an Italian foundation contractor and a worldwide engineering consultancy was awarded a €2.4 million grant by the EU to develop and trial the system.
Apart from voting at elections, I have never been actively involved in politics. But the appalling record of the SNP here in Scotland, the pathetic performance of the May, Johnson, Truss and Sunak governments and the election of a labour government who clearly are out of their depth made me so angry that I decided I should try and do something about it.
In October 2024 I joined Reform UK. In May 2025 I became the interim Chair of the Highlands and Islands Branch of Reform UK and am now standing in the upcoming Scottish elections for the Orkney Islands constituency.
I first visited Orkney 45 years ago when my, soon to be wife, took me to meet her grandmother who lived in Stromness. Over the last 15 years I have come to Orkney six to eight times both to visit my wife’s relations and for holidays, so I do have an affinity for both the place and its people.

party_description_text: Scottish Trade Unionist and Socialist Coalition
statement_to_voters: Sinead has worked in Paisley for the last seven years at UWS. She has an outstanding record as a socialist fighter for the interests of the working class and against the brutal cost of living crisis

For over 4 years we’ve watched our children’s dreams and ambitions crumble. We are their defenders and we must NOT allow the destruction of their futures. The future belongs to them and it’s our duty to ensure it’s a future where human rights and freedoms are not only permitted but immovably enshrined in law.

I want every child to grow up in a world with freedom, true democracy and absolute human rights where politicians and leaders operate with integrity for the future of our society and our economy. Our governments have not been acting in our best interest. The impact of lockdown and damaging or immoral policy making have been devastating to our individual lives; our health, society and economy have not been their priority.

Opposition parties have not been standing against damaging decisions and policies and we the people are now morally obliged to be the opposition. Without political intervention our country will continue to be led by individuals with conflicts of interest or ulterior motives.

I recognise that it’s impossible to go back to the way it used to be, this does not mean that we have to accept the ‘New Normal’ as it’s laid out to us by those who have taken absolute control. We have the ultimate opportunity for change if that’s what we want, to create a fair and just world. We can of course, choose to live in fear and hang on to the idea of the return to the ‘Old Normal’ or we can choose to create our own future, a new future that works for us, our children and our children’s children. One in which we reject censorship, propaganda and applied behavioural psychology in favour of freedom of speech, bodily autonomy and human rights.

Our children are depending on us...

Kenneth Morton is a qualified Rural Chartered Surveyor, Agricultural Valuer and Project Manager, who has worked in various positions for private estates, civil service, third sector, consultancies and energy companies for over 25 years having dealt with matters ranging from housing, forestry, agriculture, renewables, the environment, as well as energy transmission and generation.

Born and raised in rural Ayrshire and settled in rural Perthshire via Lancashire and Stirlingshire and now living in Perthshire on the edge of the Carse of Gowrie with his wife Karen and a Springer Spaniel, Kenneth has been at the forefront of witnessing the collapse of rural areas following the closure of coal mining, has witnessed the lack of joined up thinking with regards agriculture, forestry, energy policy, the damage done to the marine ecosystems due to over fishing, not to mention the degradation of the NHS, public infrastructure and transport, and our once leading education system as he has gone through life.

Kenneth was one of the first to stand for Reform UK in the 2021 Holyrood election and has been an active proponent of Reform UK since then having also stood in the 2024 Westminster election, delivering thousands of leaflets to the constituency, attending hustings and responding to government consultations both personally and on behalf of Reform UK.

As well as giving energy generation, grid planning, agricultural and environmental talks to local members branches, Kenneth wrote the 2021 energy policy paper for Reform UK and has input into Reform UK policy on a number of matters.

If elected to Holyrood, Kenneth intends to help reform Holyrood and better align its policies to support Scots in their everyday lives, reduce the burden of over regulation and put Scottish economic growth back in a positive direction.

Kenneth’s top priorities for North Perthshire are as follows: -

•	Reduce the cost of energy, and thereby lowering the cost of living for everyone and businesses
•	Reform the agricultural support system and reduce the bureaucracy on farmers
•	Reduce taxes to enable people to spend their hard earned money on what they want
•	Reform the NHS to reduce waiting lists

People often quote that the “nail that sticks up, is the first to be hammered down”, but the truth is if no one stands up, then everyone is hammered flat and nothing will change, Kenneth is standing up and ready to represent the people of North Perthshire and invites you to do so as well, and to join him in building a better Scotland by supporting and voting for Reform UK in the upcoming Holyrood election.”

LIB DEMS CHOOSE LOCAL CARER and TEACHER

The Scottish Liberal Democrats candidate for Perthshire South and Kinross-shire shire at this Scottish Election is Amanda Clark. She lives in Scotlandwell near Kinross, with her family and enjoys playing an active part in the local community.
After teaching for thirty years, Amanda took early retirement to become a full-time Carer for her son who has complex disabilities. She balances her caring role with volunteering for a local anti-poverty charity .This has given her a greater understanding of the challenges faced by many families in Perthshire South and Kinross-shire in the current cost of living crisis. She also regularly completes Mighty Hikes to fund-raise for the Macmillan Cancer Charity.
Amanda is a trustee of a charity that aims to improve the quality and provision of short breaks for Carers in Scotland. She also serves on her local Community Council and Gala Committee.
Amanda’s husband runs a small, manufacturing company that exports internationally. This means she has first-hand knowledge of the challenges faced by Scottish businesses in this post- Brexit era.
Her wealth of experience in teaching, charity work and fighting for her son’s needs means that, if elected as MSP, Amanda will be a strong champion for Carers and Families.

Jim has lived, worked and campaigned in this constituency his whole life. He was born in Perth, brought up in Tarvie Place, Letham and now lives just five minutes from Methven. He began his career as a shepherd before becoming a hill farmer and building a successful food and events-based catering business alongside it. He has extensive experience in urban and rural affairs and background in farming as the Founder of Farmers For Yes.

I am standing as the Reform Scottish Parliamentary Candidate for Perthshire South and Kinross-shire.  I am also the first Candidate on the Mid Scotland and Fife Regional List.  Please vote Reform on both ballot papers.

I grew up in the far north of Scotland and, apart from a year in Kent, I have lived, worked and brought up my family in different parts of Scotland – firstly as a veterinary surgeon and latterly working for environmental charities on public affairs and media.  These varied experiences have given me a broad view of the needs of Scottish people and has led me to want to work for improved lives and communities locally and across Scotland.

I have lived in beautiful rural Perthshire for twenty-three years and I’m well aware of the challenges in rural areas. In recent years, we’ve seen the loss of post offices and police stations, poor public transport, and a drastic need for housing and jobs to help young people remain in the area. Perth city centre needs regeneration and Reform’s small business policies would encourage new entrepreneurs.  Our policy for lowering taxes will help people in work and pensioners.

Standing up for local and national concerns

I have an active and successful campaigning record, including leading a campaign to protect our wild peatlands, leading to Wild Land Areas being recognised as a national asset by the Scottish Government in Scottish Planning Policy. I have campaigned  and given evidence in Public Local Inquiries against the deluge of industrial energy projects - such as the proposed 200m turbines in Perthshire and Kinross-shire.  I have been an unpaid carer and  know that both social care and the NHS need reform.

I am an experienced public speaker and I know how the Scottish Parliament works, not just in the debating chamber but in Committees and other events, so I will be able to engage and effect change immediately.  And how needed that change is!

Reform’s policies on key issues are needed to revive our economy and restore healthy communities.  Please vote Reform on both ballot papers, and I hope that you will come and visit me in the parliament after 8th May!

party_description_text: Scottish Trade Unionist and Socialist Coalition
statement_to_voters: Jim lives in Renfrewshire North and was a leader of the teachers’ trade union - the EIS - for many years.

It is a privilege to be the Labour candidate for Renfrewshire North &  Cardonald, standing for election to the Scottish Parliament. As a retired consultant surgeon at the Royal Alexandra Hospital in Paisley with over 27 years of experience, I know the NHS in Scotland inside out — and I know that only Labour, the party of the NHS, can fix it.

After 19 years of SNP government, we have widespread failure in public services across the NHS, education, local government, transport and infrastructure.  The people of Scotland, and our economy, cannot flourish in such an environment. The SNP are solely responsible. We must strongly state that and make the case for change.

Ross Stalker first joined the Liberal Democrats in 2006 while studying Biological Sciences at Edinburgh University. He made Renfrew his home in 2010 and went on to obtain a Master's Degree in Biotechnology at the University of the West of Scotland in 2014. With his background as a biologist, he understands the urgency of the climate emergency and he firmly believes in protecting and enhancing Scotland's natural heritage.

Ross has been employed for several years as a Campaign Organiser for the party, throughout three parliamentary elections, the EU referendum and the 2017 local elections, which has given him extensive experience in engaging with voters and winning political campaigns. He has also served for over a decade on the Scottish Liberal Democrat Conference Committee, which has given him experience in a broad range of policy areas.

He is particularly concerned with improving mental health services - especially outpatient services which he feels are being left behind in the new Health and Social Care Partnership model - and also wants to raise awareness and improve diagnosis of adult ADHD, having been diagnosed himself in his late 20s. He is worried about the impact of Brexit on the economy and public services, and passionate about achieving a more socially just Scotland.

Ross was the Liberal Democrat candidate in Paisley & Renfrewshire North in 2019, where he outperformed the party's national swing to more than double the local vote share.

For too long the establishment parties of the LibLabCon, SNP and Greens and dictated to the people of Scotland in a top down manner the priorities of this parties and they do not serve the best interests of the people of Scotland. We believe in freedom of expression, to have a job or a business, to work hard and earn a good wage and keep the majority of your earnings rather than handing it to government who waste it on vanity projects and globalist net zero green agendas or similar. That is why I am standing in the Scottish Parliamentary elections to fight for public services par excellence to offer people choice to gain the best education, the best health care and the best location for living and working.

Allan Lyons is the Reform UK candidate for Rutherglen, Cambuslang and Blantyre.

A working-class candidate from Glasgow, Allan has a background in construction and financial services, where he has worked directly with people facing financial pressure.

He is standing to offer a clear alternative to the parties that have been in power, focusing on lowering the cost of living, putting local people first, and restoring a sense of community.

party_description_text: Scottish Trade Unionist and Socialist Coalition
statement_to_voters:
Chris lives in Cambuslang and is the branch secretary of the Glasgow City UNISON trade union branch.
Chris has an outstanding record of fighting to defend the working class in the midst of the brutal cost of living crisis.

party_name: Alliance to Liberate Scotland and Sovereignty
statement_to_voters:
I have lived in Hamnavoe, Burra for over 30 years.
I am standing for Sovereignty (https://sovereignty.scot) in the Shetland constituency and the Highlands and Islands region in the Scottish Parliament elections.
Sovereignty want Scotland to be independent, and to stay out of the EU.
Policy passed at Annual Congress can be seen at https://sovereignty.scot/policy/

Brian Nugent

“We ALL have one VERY IMPORTANT thing in common - we want the best for our community.  If elected, I will do my utmost to work positively and collaboratively with all, regardless of political persuasion, in order to improve life in the Highlands” - Helen

Next year will see young people voting for the first time who have grown up entirely under the SNP government,” she said. “Together with the rest of the electorate, they have the chance to choose a new direction for our communities and for Scotland’s future by voting for Scottish Labour.

Falling educational standards, fewer opportunities for school leavers, housing shortages, and some of the longest waiting times for mental health diagnosis and treatment in the country, are all making it harder for children and young people to fulfil their potential.

This affects not only younger generations but also impacts our wider communities today and into the future. Many are left with no choice but to leave the area for opportunities elsewhere, with a knock-on effect on the workforces of local businesses and essential public services.

Since taking power in 2024 the UK Labour government has delivered a pay rise for 200,000 of the lowest paid Scottish workers, strengthened employment rights, seen the fastest growing economy in the G7 with five interest rate cuts in a year as the economy recovers, and provided the largest budget settlement for Scotland in the history of devolution.

Despite this, Scots continue to see our public services decline under an SNP government that is tired and out of ideas to fix the mess they have made since 2007, while England is benefiting from improved NHS waiting times and expanded provision of childcare.

I’m honoured to have been chosen as the SNP’s candidate for the constituency of Skye, Lochaber & Badenoch at May’s Scottish Parliament elections.

I’m a **fluent Gaelic speaker** and musician, so the culture and history of the Highlands are very important to me. **My husband works in the NHS** in mental health nursing, and we have two sons who are in primary school.

I want to help working families by **bringing bills down** and improving **access to affordable childcare**; I want to see **increased staffing investment** both in **education and social care**; and I want to support the specific **needs of rural businesses**.

My background is in Gaelic media and I’ve worked in politics since 2021, based at the Scottish Parliament managing the office of the MSP for the Western Isles. I split my time between Kingussie and Edinburgh due to work, so I am already well used to the demands of a Highlands MSP’s weekly commute! We have family ties all over the constituency, and I am really looking forward to spending time speaking to voters across all the different parts of our area in the next few months ahead of May’s vote.

If elected, I will work as hard as I can to **continue the progress made by Kate Forbes** in ensuring the Highlands’ needs are fully considered through policy-making and in investment decisions, including by pushing for **improved infrastructure, housing and public services.** What we really need to enable Scotland to thrive, however, is **independence** - being able to make our own decisions on energy and on the economy to put money back in people’s pockets.

I’d love to hear about what’s important to you personally, so please do get in touch.

My name is Jon Whitton and I am a 56 year old retired Nurse, who has spent the past 30 years caring for patients in both the NHS and the private care sector.

I moved to Scotland with my wife in 2013 and quickly immersed myself in the local area, engaging in many rural pursuits and building relationships with the local farming community.

I have not previously been involved in politics but in December 2024 felt compelled to act. This was due to poor health services, rising energy cost and declining infrastructure in Scotland. I joined Reform UK Scotland and hit the floor running, taking up the role of Treasurer and Constituency Organiser in my local area.

In the summer of 2025 I answered Reforms call for ordinary regular people to come forward and stand as a candidate. After successful vetting and interviews I was announced as the candidate for Skye, Lochaber and Badenoch at this years Scottish Conference.

Jill grew up in Aberdeenshire and studied politics at Glasgow University before moving to London.  She worked delivering projects and transformational change across public and private sectors  before returning to Scotland and settling in Fife.

Jill campaigns for active travel and has cycle toured extensively across Scotland mainland, out islands and mainland Europe. She recognises the need for public transport to connect people and towns and cities and will prioritise decarbonising public transport.

Jill has worked in a voluntary capacity for a number of charities including a local cycle outreach programme as well as with NGOs overseas. She also enjoys takes open water swimming, running, cycling and painting.

Born in Glasgow in 1973, Alyn grew up in Scotland and Saudi Arabia, returning to Scotland in 1986. Having studied Law and European Law, Alyn also gained a Masters degree in European Studies from the College of Europe in Warsaw. After a year teaching English in India and working with Scotland Europa in Brussels, Alyn qualified as a lawyer, practising commercial law in London and Edinburgh. In 2002, he started working for Richard Lochhead MSP, then the SNP Group at Holyrood as an adviser on European, Justice and Business policies. Alyn was elected to the European Parliament for the first time in 2004 and was re-elected three times in the SNP victories in the 2009, 2014 and 2019 elections - in 2019 as leading SNP candidate, winning the best ever result for the SNP in a European election. In the December 2019 Westminster election, Alyn won the Stirling seat with 51% of the vote. He pledged at the declaration to represent everyone in Stirling regardless of politics. In the Westminster Parliament, he served as the SNP Europe and EU Accession Spokesperson. Alyn maintains a keen interest in Agriculture, Food and Animal Welfare issues. He is the Honorary Vice President of the Scottish Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals, the SSPCA, Scotland’s animal welfare charity, and is an Honorary Associate of the British Veterinary Association. Both posts are voluntary and unpaid.

I’m an experienced Operations Director in manufacturing, have worked in the rural sector and am a wife and mother. Our politicians have lost your trust. That changes now.

I’ll protect women and young girls, fight for safer communities, better local services, real economic opportunity and employment. No excuses, no empty promises – just action to deliver real change. My ambition is to rebuild prosperity for everyone in Stirling, the surrounding towns and rural areas.

Adam grew up in Bishopbriggs, where he now lives with his wife and young son. This is his community – and it’s where he’s raising his family today.

He started out working in theatre and the arts before moving into the charity sector, where he campaigned for people with cystic fibrosis to access life-saving medicines. Adam has also volunteered with organisations supporting children from disadvantaged backgrounds and worked with community groups helping young people at risk of becoming involved in the criminal justice system to turn their lives around.

Adam says, _“This area is my home. It’s where I grew up, and where I’m raising my family. Too many people here feel really let down - by the state of local roads and pavements, by having to wait months for treatment on the NHS and by how much of a struggle it is to afford energy bills and food in the supermarket._

_Many of them have also totally lost faith in anyone involved in politics to make a difference or do the things they promised to do. That’s why I’m working hard to improve things locally and speak to listen to as many people on the doorsteps as possible.”_

Adam’s experience in the charity and voluntary sectors has shown him that real change takes hard work and persistence. That’s why he has already knocked on thousands of doors across Strathkelvin & Bearsden – listening to people, hearing what matters most to them, and standing up for the changes they need.

_“More than anything, being dad to a wee boy motivates me to get elected and change things for the better. For too long, the country has been going in the wrong direction – we desperately need to turn things around.”_

Steven was born in Bellshill on 27 August 1981 and raised in Viewpark and still lives in the village today. He was educated at St Gabriel’s Primary School and Cardinal Newman High School. He was first elected for the SNP in 2015 as Councillor for Thorniewood and reelected in 2017. Steven was then elected to represent his hometown constituency of Coatbridge Chryston and Bellshlll in 2019.At Westminster,  Steven held the role as the SNP spokesperson for Environment, Food and rural affairs and has been a strong voices for Scotlands Produce and those who produce it for us.Steven entered politics from a successful career in retail and security management for two main reasons . Independence being the main driver but also to support in protecting local green spaces for local use and to protect the local environment for futures generations.

I live in Lanarkshire and have done so most of my life and have largely been shaped by it. I am standing to give back to the community and to Scotland as a whole.
I joined the RAF as a teenager and after an apprenticeship was an Aircraft Technician.
In civil life I was an Electrical Engineer working on many large and small projects in a wide range of industries including Oil, Gas, Manufacturing, Hospitals, Data Centres and Electrical Power Industry culminating in a management role.
I am my own man and believe firmly in the Local Connection, Locals First, Rejuvenation of our Manufacturing Capabilities, Incentivising our young people, Exploiting the Oil & Gas Resource for our use, lowering taxes and cost of living, An Effective Care System and fixing the NHS. I strongly believe in a Majority Opinion and not the Minority.
If elected, I will work tirelessly for the Community of Uddingston and Bellshill. The sense of duty that comes with that runs deep and is grounded in something personal and sincere.

Ben Munnoch is the Scottish Liberal Democrat candidate for Uddingston and Bellshill, campaigning for change with fairness at its heart.

Ben currently works in the telecommunications sector, after many years working in the public sector, and most recently as a Communications and Parliamentary Affairs Advisor. Ben was key in forming the Cross-Party Group on Ice Sports, working across the political spectrum to champion ice sports.

Ben has worked on, and championed a variety of issues, including; health, energy, net zero, foreign policy, defence, social issues, health, LGBT rights, local government, and sports, especially ice sports.

Ben is an alumni of the University of Stirling with a Bachelor of Arts, with Honours, in Journalism Studies and Politics.

Ben has also fundraised for a cancer charity, based in Central Scotland, which offers support to those living with cancer, and those affected by cancer.

For media enquiries: media@scotlibdems.org.uk

Amanda born in Scotland, resides in the Falkirk area and has dedicated 35 years to front line public service, building a career focused on supporting people and strengthening communities.

Her professional background spans roles in law, child protection, safeguarding and education; she is a qualified and experienced teacher. Amanda’s life has been dedicated to serving the public. Throughout her career, she has held senior leadership positions, where she has been responsible for delivering meaningful, practical outcomes for individuals and families. She brings this same results driven approach to her candidacy, with a clear focus on making a tangible difference for local people.
