Amanda is determined to put the people of Falkirk East and Linlithgow first. She will listen to your concerns, she will be your voice, she will work hard to make change happen, she will be your champion, Amanda will be there for you.

Vote Reform. Together, let’s see what we can do to make that change happen.

Steven was born in Bellshill on 27 August 1981 and raised in Viewpark and still lives in the village today. He was educated at St Gabriel’s Primary School and Cardinal Newman High School. He was first elected for the SNP in 2015 as Councillor for Thorniewood and reelected in 2017. Steven was then elected to represent his hometown constituency of Coatbridge Chryston and Bellshlll in 2019.At Westminster,  Steven held the role as the SNP spokesperson for Environment, Food and rural affairs and has been a strong voices for Scotlands Produce and those who produce it for us.Steven entered politics from a successful career in retail and security management for two main reasons . Independence being the main driver but also to support in protecting local green spaces for local use and to protect the local environment for futures generations.

I was born in 1993.  My father a farmer and my mother a civil servant.  I went to Larbert Village primary school and Larbert High School then on to Oatridge Agricultural College.  I met my wife Cheryl in 2015 and we wed in May 2023.

Since I was old enough to be useful I have farmed in Larbert, Denny and other parts of the Falkirk area.  When I left college I worked full time on the family farm, becoming a partner in the farm business with my Dad.  Since Dad sadly passed away in 2018 I have run the farm business on my own account, expanding it with the addition of more livestock and rented and contract-farmed ground.

We are a mixed animal/arable operation but have diversified into other enterprises such as selling pre-fabricated buildings and internal stabling.  Our contract winter maintenance business lasted until high fuel costs made it uneconomic.

I may only be 30 but having been brought up on the farm and having been lucky enough to have had a father who gave me responsibility for running the farm has given me an excellent grasp of the business and personal struggles of the real world. This is something our current batch of MP’s seem to be severely lacking!

Why I’m standing for Reform UK Scotland.

I first became political in the run up to the independence referendum in 2014. I hated how the Yes campaign split our country in two.

I was pro Brexit in the 2016 EU referendum as I believed, and still do, that Britain is at its best when fully self-governing.

After the Conservatives’ mismanagement of both Brexit and the Covid scare, abandoning their core values, and the SNP dividing and running Scotland into the ground, I now believe it’s time for ordinary people like you and me to take our country back from the career politicians and the “Indy Ref or bust” crew.  Please join me and help to Take Our Country Back.

Prior to entering the third sector Toni worked in Brussels and in the Scottish Parliament. During the independence referendum he worked for Yes Scotland, supporting grassroots groups across the country.Toni graduated from the University of Edinburgh in European politics and modern languages. Toni is proud of his Scottish-Italian heritage. He’s married to Scott, enjoys long walks with Harris the cockapoo and has recently taken up bouldering.

On November 14th, we can send the Labour/Tory council a message. In recent years, councillors have closed our swimming pools, sat by as our bus services were destroyed, and now they are threatening our community centres.

I was born and brought up in West Lothian and believe that we deserve so much better than this, that is why I am standing to put people and planet first at this election. We need change, but unfortunately, those who have promised that in the past have failed time and again.

If you vote for the Scottish Greens on November 14th, I pledge to fight for: - Improved public transport in Whitburn and Blackburn. - Local action to tackle the climate emergency. - Reform of council tax, ensuring the richest pay their fair share. - A ban on pavement parking on our high streets, making roads safer for all.

Whilst Labour have cut the Winter Fuel Payment, I have campaigned to keep it in place. If elected I will directly challenge the Labour administration to call on their Westminster and Holyrood colleagues to reintroduce the payment.

I have long campaigned for better public transport in West Lothian, from cheaper rail fares to more reliable bus services and if elected, I will make this one of my main aims.

I am proud of the work of Scottish Green MSPs who have more than doubled the Scottish Child Payment, introduced free bus travel for people under 22, and held the SNP to account over their cuts to nature and climate spending.

On November 14th, vote like our future depends on it. Vote Scottish Greens.

I have lived and worked all my life in the Central Belt of Scotland. Educated at Edinburgh and Edinburgh Napier Universities, I have a BSc in Physics, as well as a Master’s degree in Business. After graduating, I enjoyed an extensive career in IT. Starting off as a Cobol Programmer in the Insurance sector, I had a number of roles, working my way up into IT Management. My final role was as a Development Manager for a public sector organisation.

Since retiring, I have used my Physics background to fully understand the ins and outs of Climate Change and Net Zero.

I joined Reform UK in July 2025, to campaign for an end to the Net Zero madness, which is the real cause of the cost-of-living crisis

I live in Lanarkshire and have done so most of my life and have largely been shaped by it. I am standing to give back to the community and to Scotland as a whole.
I joined the RAF as a teenager and after an apprenticeship was an Aircraft Technician.
In civil life I was an Electrical Engineer working on many large and small projects in a wide range of industries including Oil, Gas, Manufacturing, Hospitals, Data Centres and Electrical Power Industry culminating in a management role.
I am my own man and believe firmly in the Local Connection, Locals First, Rejuvenation of our Manufacturing Capabilities, Incentivising our young people, Exploiting the Oil & Gas Resource for our use, lowering taxes and cost of living, An Effective Care System and fixing the NHS. I strongly believe in a Majority Opinion and not the Minority.
If elected, I will work tirelessly for the Community of Uddingston and Bellshill. The sense of duty that comes with that runs deep and is grounded in something personal and sincere.

The fundamental values that underpin our society are being eroded and so we are offering a fresh perspective that focuses on the family, marriage and a pro life message.. The family is a source of joy that underpins society and the state should not take the place of the family but provide support to enable the family to raise children according to their beliefs and values. Strong families and parenthood should be honoured as a high calling through which many of the seemingly intractable problems in society could be better addressed. Society needs a healthy democratic enviornement with openness and improved accountability especially at a local level. Public debate and information should not be dominated by central Government funded agencies or charities who simply promote the ruling parties agenda. Introducing referenda  when the government is ignoring the will of a majority would enhance the participation of people in political decision making. The Scottish Family Party does not support Hate Crime legislation and believes that men and women naturally have different interests and priorities. We wish to see society unified rather than divided by identity based politics that divides people into oppressors and oppressed causing people to feel afraid to express their beliefs even in their own home. We support freedom of religion and freedom of speech.  In schools policies such as the 'named person approach' treat parents as secondary to professionals or experts and the focus should move instead to educational rigour rather than the subjective approach of the curriculumn for excellence. The taxation system should make allowances transferrable so that insread of taxing individuals separetly the family is taxed as a whole with allowances for dependent children so that one parent can remain at home to dedicate his or her time to home life. Council Tax should be discounted when the family has children.  The law should reflect the fact that life begins at conception with protection for the child in the womb and the Scottish Family Party wishes to see better support with independent counselling and the involvement of fathers in decision-making. The denial of prolife coinselling to women and exploration of adoption for the child is very damaging to women and the voice of prolife politicians need to be heard in Holyrood. As a prolife party the Scottish Family Party will oppose euthansia.

Caron Lindsay has lived in Livingston for 24 years. She was the Liberal Democrat candidate for Almond Valley in the Scottish Parliamentary elections in 2021 and for Livingston in the UK General Election in 2024.

She is currently the President of the Scottish Liberal Democrats and spokesperson for Social Security.

In her day job, she helps people with all sorts of problems, from healthcare to social security to housing.

Caron wants to see a society where everyone is free to live the best life that they can. She believes that too many are held back by poverty, lack of decent housing and ill health and that needs to change. She wants to see better support for unpaid carers. She is particularly concerned that so many young people are waiting for years to be diagnosed with ADHD and Autism. If elected, these will be her priorities.

For media enquiries: media@scotlibdems.org.uk

After benefiting from an excellent Scottish state education, I went on to build a career of over 35 years in the software industry. I’ve worked across the full spectrum—from small startups to large multinationals—and for the past decade, I’ve been self-employed as a freelancer.
In recent years, I’ve become increasingly concerned about the direction our country is heading in. As a mother of two grown children, I worry deeply about the kind of future they—and the next generation—will inherit. That is why I’ve made the decision to stand for election.
I believe Scotland deserves better. Too many people feel let down, and meaningful change is urgently needed.
I’m a plain-speaking, hard-working professional—not a career politician. As an MSP, my priority will always be to put my constituents first, in every decision I make.

Born and raised in Wishaw and married to Jessie (49 years) with one daughter, Duncan has had two separate successful business with a break between to gain a BA Accounting at Napier University (1994).

First business venture was owning and running a MOT testing garage for 12 years before University and then a complete change by doing Small Business accounts working mainly from home. Further studies allowed him to act as a Financial / Mortgage Advisor which continued within his new venture, namely estate agency, opening in late 2001.

After more than 40 years of self-employment, Duncan retired in 2021 during the pandemic. Always being interested in politics Duncan joined Reform UK in August 2024 and has been an active member since. He is sure that 2026 will be a defining moment in Scottish politics, with Reform UK making massive inroads and breaking up the cosy status quo.

party_description_text: Independence for Scotland Party, Direct Democracy.
statement_to_voters:
Julie originally trained to be a professional musician, but her life changed when her mother became ill and she became her full time carer.

She was a prominent campaigner for Monklands Hospital when its A&E was under threat of closure. The hospital was saved and she got involved in health politics as part of the Scottish Health Campaigns Network, later joining the SNP in 2010.

She became a councillor for North Lanarkshire in 2012 and was noted for bringing attention to the situation surrounding the Mears housing property contract in 2015. The leader of the council subsequently resigned and a full investigation of the council’s procurement practice was launched.

She left the SNP in 2017 and joined ISP in January 2020. She splits her time between teaching, playing and fighting for Scottish independence.

I got into politics to make a difference. Becoming homeless at 16 showed me how fragile the system can be, how easily people can fall through the cracks and how vital it is to have someone fighting your corner.

Living in central Scotland, I see how much people are struggling. They feel like they’re stuck on a permanent waiting list, from NHS treatment to affordable housing.

As a Liberal Democrat, I am committed to giving people the representation they deserve. That means fixing our broken health service, tackling the housing crisis, lifting up local economies and delivering the best education for our children.

I don’t consider myself a typical politician. I’ve seen life’s challenges up close and personal, and that’s what motivates me as a parliamentary candidate. I want to change things for the better and get results for the people of Central Scotland and Lothians West.

Hi, I’m David McLennan, your Reform UK candidate for the Bathgate Constituency and I live in the constituency area, near Seafield.

I won the Council election in Whitburn and East Blackburn in December last year, and am now working full-time as Reform’s first-ever elected Councillor in Scotland.

I see the problems that we, the normal folk, are facing on a daily basis because of failed local and national government policy and I’m determined to help as many constituents as I can with these personal challenges.

My colleagues and I are not career politicians – we’re normal,working folk the same as most of you – but we’ve had enough of the incompetence and mismanagement of the last twenty years or so.

The philosophy of Reform UK is to make Great Britain great again, through promoting the values of family, community and country.

favourite_biscuit: Either Jaffa Cakes or Tunnock's Teacakes - but vegan!
statement_to_voters:
Author, academic, carer, teacher and law student, Alan McManus is the Sovereignty candidate for Cumbernauld & Kilsyth, standing for Liberate Scotland on the Central Scotland & Lothians West regional list.

Growing up in a 1960’s Glasgow overspill housing scheme in Kirkintilloch, ‘Dr McManus’ uses his title on social media to avoid his celebrated namesake being blamed for Alan’s forthright views on the UK and devolved government overreach of executive power, especially since 2020, resulting in increasing limitation of individual rights: especially of freedom of expression and association.

A lifelong advocate for animal welfare and a keen organic gardener, Alan opposes the cruelty of factory farming and the poisoning of the land, water, plants, fish, animals and people, by the indiscriminate use of pesticides and plastics. Canvassing for Freedom Alliance in rural Kintyre in 2023, farmers warned Alan of the coming food crisis, so even as a vegan he understands the slogan: “no farmers, no food!”

Alan has written several books on topics of ethical controversy and tries to see both sides and explore common ground: like pro-life and pro-choice women working together to support mothers under severe socio-economic pressure. As an amateur actor, cyclist and sailor, Alan wants to see Scots of all ages have the opportunity to thrive – in an independent Scotland!

party_description_text: Independent Green Voice - Organic Green Scotland

statement_to_voters:
I want to promote unity and common sense from our side of politics. I am standing as a starting point for Advance UK and have been the Scotland Regional Director for almost 5 months. During that time we have established a large set up in Scotland with Area Managers and a working team.
We are a new political party, and not currently well known.
We have a few weeks until polling, so lets see what we can do.
My five primary aims are:
Removal and savings against economic/illegal migrants.
Removal and savings made against the woke green agenda.
To open up universities to more of our young people in Scotland.
To remove bad decision making against woke priorates for police. (Such as DEI)
To improve public sector procurement (my expertise) so we get better value for money.

party_description_text: Vote Scottish Greens - Let's demand better

statement_to_voters:
Those who live in Edinburgh will know that over the past number of years our city has declined. Housing costs have risen while access to GPs has fallen. Traffic has worsened as our roads crumble and securing care packages for loved ones has become harder than ever.

All of this has been happening right on the Scottish Parliament’s doorstep and yet very little if anything is being done about it.

The needs of our capital city are being ignored by the major parties in Holyrood while they play political games and chase cheap headlines.

Edinburgh deserves better. Our city deserves a representative free from party shackles, someone who will unapologetically give voice to local concerns at Holyrood.

That is why I am seeking re-election as an independent candidate for the Edinburgh and Lothians East region.

Edinburgh has always been my home and I am passionate about ensuring that both the city and its surrounding areas thrive.

As Scotland’s modern, metropolitan hub, Edinburgh is in many ways the country’s powerhouse. We should not be watching our infrastructure crumble and our citizens’ quality of life decline.

It should not be too much to ask to see a GP when you need one or to secure a care package that allows our loved ones to live comfortable and dignified lives.

I will stand on a platform that puts the needs of Edinburgh and Lothians East at the forefront, while continuing to fight tirelessly for the rights of disabled people.

I am committed to remaining fiercely independent, working with any party that is willing to deliver for our region.

I will balance compassion with financial responsibility, offering practical solutions on housing, healthcare and social security.

And I will advocate loudly and persistently that the needs of disabled people must no longer be ignored by those in power.

I look forward to engaging with residents throughout the campaign, listening to your priorities and setting out a positive plan that champions our capital and protects the vulnerable.

party_description_text: Vote Scottish Greens - Let's demand better
statement_to_voters:
Chas Booth was elected as the Green Councillor for Leith on Edinburgh Council in May 2012, and was re-elected in May 2017. Chas lives in Leith with his wife and two young children.

Prior to his election in May 2012, Chas was a fuel poverty campaigner with a small trade association in Leith. He was a member of the Scottish Government’s fuel poverty forum advising on policy and implementation.

He has also campaigned for Friends of the Earth Scotland, managed the Edinburgh office of sustainable transport charity Sustrans, worked for the Green Party in the Scottish and European Parliaments, and taught English in Bavaria and Bosnia.

Deirdre was a front-bench member of the SNP group in Westminster covering relations with the devolved administrations, and liaison with the Scottish Government and Scottish Parliament. She sits on the Public Accounts Committee which is responsible for examining the value for money of public spending, holding the UK Government and Civil Servants to account for the way our money gets spent.

Before winning her seat in the 2015 election, Deidre was a councillor for the Leith Walk ward on Edinburgh Council and Depute Lord Provost of Edinburgh. She was born in Western Australia, grew up in Perth and studied for her BA in English from John Curtin University before studying acting at the WA Academy of Performing Arts. She lives in Edinburgh with Dougie and her two daughters.

party_description_text: Independent Green Voice - Organic Green Scotland
Alliance to Liberate Scotland For Independence
Vote Scottish Greens - Let's demand better

statement_to_voters:
For 25 years, Scotland has endured high spending and declining outcomes. Despite a steady increase in taxes, hard-working families face rising costs, failing public services and stagnant economic growth.

With a background is in investment and financial management, including as an economic policy advisor at the Bank of England, I believe Scotland’s current challenges are the result of poor fiscal management and misguided policies.

I am standing to help Reform UK dislodge the SNP and bring about change – restoring economic growth, creating well-paid jobs, and ensuring Scotland has the resources to deliver high-quality public services once again.

Edinburgh City Council has a reputation for wasting money, for not listening to residents, focusing on vanity projects and not getting the basics of city management right.

Initiatives such as the Tram, Bin hubs, Spaces for People bollards, Controlled Parking Zone extensions to Gorgie and Hutchison and the proposed Low Emissions Zone are good examples while roads and pavements deteriorate, drain gullies are unmaintained leading to flooding, and the Dell bridges and Craiglockhart Hill paths are closed.

If elected I will focus on getting the basic services right by listening to those who know best, local residents.

party_description_text: Scottish Family Party - Standing for Life

statement_to_voters:
**A WORKERS’ VOICE AT HOLYROOD - NOT A CAREER POLITICIAN**

**My name is Chris.** I’m a trade union and tenancy union activist, a community councillor, and living in the working-class scheme of Muirhouse in social housing.

I’ve worked in hospitality, heritage, homelessness services and I’ve been self-employed. **I know what it’s like to face insecurity, unaffordable rents, and the feeling that Holyrood doesn’t listen to people like us.**

The SNP have run the Scottish Government for nearly two decades.  The result?
**Homelessness has risen** (a record 10,480 children trapped in temporary housing);
**An NHS pushed to the brink** (with exploding waiting lists);
**The creeping privatisation of our care system.**

They don’t need more time. The other Parties have squandered every chance they have had across Edinburgh, Scotland, and Britain. **They didn’t fail by accident - they failed because they serve the system.**  _We need a change of system._

**I am standing with the Communist Party to give Edinburgh and Lothians East a true socialist voice - one that refuses to manage capitalism and fights to end it.**

**VOTE COMMUNIST. VOTE CHRIS CULLEN**
_Edinburgh and Lothians East_
_Communist Party of Britain_

party_description_text: Alliance to Liberate Scotland For Independence

statement_to_voters:
“I am standing on the Regional List for Edinburgh and Lothians East because
Scotland’s decisions should be made in Scotland,” said Morgwn C Davies.
“Independence is about democratic control—having the power to shape our
economy so that Scotland’s wealth can benefit Scotland’s people, set a fair and
workable approach to immigration that works for Scotland, and choose a foreign
policy that reflects our commitment to peace, neutrality, and constructive
engagement with the world.”

party_description_text: Scottish Family Party - Standing for Life
statement_to_voters:
It's an honour to stand for Edinburgh North and Leith in these General Elections, i want to represent your views. I will work hard to earn the trust of people, creating positive relationships. I will act on what i say. Together we can forge a better community and make Edinburgh North and Leith an even more wonderful place to live.

Issues i would fight for - more active policing for safer streets. Good education for all. Look at ways to deal with the local housing issues. Ways to help with the cost of living crisis.  The Council needs to step in and help with the alcohol and drug abuse i will work hard on this - will work along side community centres. We need to attract more investment into businesses. Let's resource youth centres properly to help with well-being and life skills, get new ones started. Let's get creative in converting abandoned buildings and railways into great facilities that meet local needs.  The roads are a mess in Edinburgh and it's time to get this sorted and make it safe for all. We need a crackdown on dog fouling. We need an end to overflowing street bins and let's make a difference for those unable to work and who are out of work.

There is so much to get done and i know i can make that difference for us. We need each other and we need to stand together for the good of our city and nation. I don't just talk i will work and make that difference.

I find it a privilege and honour to be part of the Scottish Family Party as we are standing for truth, we keep to our promises and really want to see a change for people and the good of our Scotland.

We will always stand for the core principles of promoting our policies as the best foundation for a stable nation.

I am standing because I believe government in Westminster and Holyrood has lost touch with real life. Too many career politicians have never run a business or faced the impact of rising taxes and energy costs.

After 26 years of building businesses and supporting entrepreneurs, I decided I had to step forward and bring common-sense and honesty to politics.

My background is in IT and AI, with experience in environmental and energy projects. I have worked in agriculture and still live on a smallholding, so I empathise with the pressures and crazy bureaucracy and carbon audits our farmers face.

My priorities are to cut taxes, scrap net-zero policies that harm our economy, and make devolution work for Scotland.
My aim is simple. Put local people first and make Scotland the most prosperous place in the UK to live and work.

Charles Dundas grew up in West Lothian, attended Whitburn Academy and graduated with an MA in History from the University of Glasgow.

He has since worked in accountancy, policy research, campaigning, and as the Chief Executive of a Scottish conservation charity.

Between 2007 and 2012 he was elected to the City of Edinburgh Council, representing the City Centre. He chaired the Lib Dem Group and sat on the Planning, Education, Culture, and Transport & Environment Committees, as well as the Lothian and Borders Police Board and the board of the Royal Lyceum Theatre.

Charles is the Convenor of the Scottish Green Liberal Democrats group and has performed at each Edinburgh Festival Fringe since 2015.

party_description_text: Scottish Socialist Party - Independent Socialist Scotland
Independence for Scotland Party, Direct Democracy.
statement_to_voters:
Hi, I’m John Hannah. I'm standing in the 2026 Scottish Parliament elections to put power where it belongs: with the people of Scotland.

I've been a Business Analyst for 25 years, working in business change in Banking, Insurance and Pensions across the UK and Ireland, Europe, the Middle East, Asia, India and the US.

I believe independence isn’t a slogan — it’s the practical route to making decisions in Scotland’s interest. With the powers of a normal country, we can take control of our own energy future, keep more of the value created here, and build an economy that delivers security for ordinary households. Independence also means the ability to design a fairer system of support — including a minimum income approach that no longer leaves people choosing between heating and eating. And it means shaping our own foreign policy: acting with principle, prioritising peace, human rights, and Scotland’s voice on the world stage.

My politics are rooted in popular sovereignty — the idea that the people are the highest authority, not distant institutions. That’s why I back direct democracy: citizen assemblies, transparent government, and national referendums on the decisions that matter most. Too much of politics happens behind closed doors; I want Scotland to be run out in the open, with the public actively involved, not just consulted after the fact.

As your MSP, I will be a consistent, uncompromising advocate for self-determination — and for the everyday policies that independence makes possible: secure homes, affordable energy, fair pay, strong public services, and democratic control over our future.

A Parliament that serves Scotland should answer to Scotland. My commitment is simple: independence, democracy, and a Scotland where power is held by its people.

Our Manifesto is here: https://www.isp.scot/manifesto/

party_description_text: Scottish Family Party - Standing for Life
statement_to_voters:
I believe families are the foundation of our society. They are the fundamental unit of welfare and wellbeing. The more families flourish the less the state needs to do to pick up the pieces. Strong families lead to a strong nation.

I think that mums and dads that decided to look after their own children should not be discriminated against by the tax system. Couples should have a shared tax allowance. Any money that is made available by the state for childcare should also be made available to a parent that takes time off work to care for their own child.

A women is an adult human female. Men should be kept out of women's spaces and women's sport. I would seek to repeal the 2004 gender recognition act.

I am for free speech. I would oppose a "Conversion Therapy" ban. These bans criminalises parents that do not accept that a child can change gender. I oppose abortion clinic buffers zones that turn silent prayer into a thought crime.

I oppose assisted dying and believe all life needs to be protected and cherished.

Lyn was born in Glasgow in 1970 and grew up in Morayshire. Having studied at Robert Gordon’s University in Aberdeen with honours in Public Administration, where she also was first active in student politics with the Federation of Student Nationalists, Lyn began her housing career in Aberdeen City Council where she continued her activism within the trade union movement and served as both Equalities Officer and Convenor of the city branch of Unison.With a postgraduate degree and over 15 years of a housing career which included working for the Chartered Institute of Housing, local authorities, housing associations and being self employed as a consultant, Lyn moved, with her husband, to East Lothian around 15 years ago and has undertaken a wider range of public service roles in health and social care as well as equalities, both paid and voluntary.Now raising a family in the area, Lyn was persuaded back into a more politically active life, was elected as Councillor for Dunbar & East Linton ward in 2022 and is Leader of the Opposition at East Lothian Council.

I am often asked why I got involved in politics. Quite simply, I like to fix things. I’m the sort of person who likes to get out their tools and put matters to rights there and then. My politics is the same – if I see things not working or people not having the opportunities they need, I will do all I can to put that right.

I feel honoured to represent Edinburgh Southern in the Scottish Parliament. It’s where I grew up and where I live with my wife and children. I started my career working in consulting, specialising in the financial services sector. I then ran several local businesses in Edinburgh – the shops Paper Tiger and Studio One. Since my election in 2016, I have striven to represent the diverse views and needs of my community in parliament and to make a difference in our part of Edinburgh.

My interests in Parliament have developed from these core principles – I am interested in what we need to do to give people opportunities, and improve our economy and our society. With the changes that automation and digitisation are bringing, I believe innovation and technology are hugely important. I also take a close interest in neuro-developmental disorders including ADHD, autism, dyslexia, and dyscalculia, along with the associated Education and Health Policy, as these are major barriers to people succeeding in education and life.

It’s not everyday you pass a law. But helping run my family’s small businesses gave me direct experience of being a front-line retail worker. This lead me to campaign for and pass through parliament a members bill to protect workers from violence and abuse at work. It is a tragedy that up until now, employees have had no defence from harassment and intimidation, for example while enforcing the law on age-restricted items.

I am currently a member of the Economy and Fair Work committee at the Scottish Parliament and  I am also the Scottish Labour Spokesperson for Economy, Business and Fair Work, helping build Scottish Labour’s vision for a growing economy and a fairer society.

My Name is Sean Moffat
Born and raised in Edinburgh from 1991.
I am a lifelong patriotic resident of this amazing city and was educated at Forrester High School.
I have lived and worked in Edinburgh my entire life and know the communities of Edinburgh and Lothians East better than most.
Like many of you, I have voted in every Scottish Parliament and UK General Election, as well as both the 2014 Scottish Independence referendum and the 2016 Brexit referendum.
Before 2014, I voted SNP.
In the first independence referendum, I voted Yes.
But after the result, I completely lost trust in the SNP when they repeatedly misrepresented the facts — and in doing so gave Scotland and its democracy a disrespectful slap in the face by not respecting the decision given in 2014.
For me, that was the red line: I did not want to swap one form of dependence on a union for dependence on another. That is why I voted to leave in the Brexit referendum.
In the elections that followed, I supported the Conservatives because they delivered Brexit and seemed to stand up for the Union. But during the Covid pandemic, I lost all remaining faith in them, too. By the 2024 General Election, I voted Reform UK, looking for a party that would finally put British citizens first.

I have now joined Advance UK because it is the only party offering a fresh, principled alternative that rejects the failed politics of the past.
I am standing as your candidate for Edinburgh and Lothians East in the Scottish Parliament election on 7 May 2026 because this region deserves better. For too long, voters here have been ignored while politicians in Holyrood obsess over another independence referendum. Meanwhile, the real issues that affect everyday life — the cost of living, housing shortages, pressures on our NHS, falling education standards, and community safety — have been pushed down the agenda.

I believe in a United Kingdom that works for the people who live here. That means defending our union, rejecting divisive nationalism, No not freindly internal tit for tat, controlling our borders, and delivering practical, common-sense solutions that improve lives right here in Edinburgh and Lothian East.

Advance UK is the only party offering a fresh, principled alternative that puts British citizens and our shared future first.

As your representative, I will be a strong, consistent voice for the working people of this region.
I will fight for policies that put money back in your pocket, protect our public services, restore our communities, and push to ensure Scotland remains securely part of the United Kingdom.
As we democratically decided to do.

I am not a career politician.
I am a local family man who has had enough of the same old failures and is now ready to put the interests of Edinburgh and Lothians East voters first.

party_description_text: Vote Scottish Greens - Let's demand better
statement_to_voters:
I’m standing to be an MSP to bring an unapologetic feminist and left-wing voice to the Scottish Parliament to fight for the radical, transformative change our country needs.

As a seasoned activist and campaigner, I have worked across a broad range of issues, fighting for abortion rights, trans rights, migrant justice and Palestine, and against the two-child benefit cap and the arms trade.

In my professional life, I have worked as a facilitator and researcher with young people, women and activist groups in Scotland and across the world. This work has had a huge influence on how I see the world and the need for radical, revolutionary action at both small and large scales.

If elected as an MSP, I would bring my passion for radical change right to the heart of Scottish politics. I would stand up against the unequal, oppressive systems that are so harmful to marginalised communities in Scotland, helping to address the cost of living crisis and ensure that everyone has access to safe and secure housing and healthcare. I would work to build an economy based on equality, peace and wellbeing rather than growth, and I would fight against the climate emergency – the defining issue of my generation and beyond.

I am immensely proud of what we’ve achieved as Greens in Scotland, from securing the right of refugees to vote, to pushing through free bus travel for young people, to standing up so strongly for trans rights, migrant rights and Palestine when others have run scared. If elected to Holyrood, I would work hard every day on behalf of my community to transform Scotland for the better, continuing this Green legacy of fighting for progressive action.

I’m really excited to be a lead candidate for the Lothian list for the Greens, alongside the amazing Lorna Slater, Q Manivannan and Kayleigh Kinross-O’Neill, and I look forward to bringing our collective vision to Holyrood in May.

Jane  has over the last decade retrained and worked as a mental health counsellor in Edinburgh working with adults and young people with experience in the voluntary sector, high schools and private practice. Jane believes in the positive impact all individuals can make to contribute to a diverse and flourishing society, and that it is the state’s role to support and facilitate citizens reaching their potential. She seeks to help build a society where understanding and empathy outweighs blame and intolerance and a system which works to reduce entrenched inequalities. She has a special interest in getting counselling services fit for purpose, accessible and sustainable for all whilst ensuring fair pay for those working in the sector. As a parent of young adults, Jane wants to work to ensure every young person in Scotland has access to work, and quality training and those in work are paid a fair wage.

Through the experience of living overseas, Jane has a strong belief that there is more that binds us than divides us, and that problems need to be solved by cross border collaboration not separation. statement_to_voters:
David Renton is standing in Leith Walk Ward in Edinburgh, where he has lived since 2000. David is a Charity Worker, a Community Councillor and a Disability Advocate. By background he is a Barrister. David is a founding life member of the Women’s Equality Party (WE) and has been a Feminist for 50 years.

David also stood as a WE candidate in the Scottish Parliament Elections in 2021, and has been involved in every election WE have ever stood for in Scotland. David is Treasurer of WE Edinburgh and Lothian; the first ever Women’s Equality Party branch in the UK.

Having lived in Edinburgh South constituency for over 30 years, I know personally many residents as well as the local issues which affect us. My children were brought up and educated here.

Following service as a policeman I was elected as a councillor in this constituency where I served for 15 years. This election has reactivated me in politics – I have been attracted by the common sense policies of Reform UK. I was formerly a Conservative but our economy has been burdened by the highest taxation in a generation, over regulation, high energy prices and crippling restrictions on personal freedoms. Labour offers more of the same. And the Greens seem to have become anti-science denying the biological reality of male and female (and they are not the only ones to do so!)

The ‘cancel culture’ of recent years has had a chilling effect; sadly it has often been government and official bodies which have played fast and loose with truth in silencing views other than those they want to impose. I have experienced that in our local council in the attempts to silence whistle-blowers. I want to value and guarantee freedom of expression and individual liberty.

I believe there are many ways to reduce energy costs and so benefit families, the cost of living and the economy. The Government, supported by all other parties, has banned or discouraged the use of energy resources available to us. I have long argued against net zero which is ruinously expensive and lacks justifying evidence. It needs scrapped.

I believe we need lower taxation. Reform UK will raise the tax-free threshold for Income Tax to £20,000 to help the lower paid and motivate those on benefits to move into work. The taxation burden on us ordinary people has never been higher – and especially so in Scotland.

I believe in valuing each person whatever their circumstances. I find that much of the current emphasis on racism and equality actually divides rather than unites us in all our diversity. I want to support families – and whilst not everyone has the opportunity, a stable family provides the best environment for children, and indeed us all.

Oh, one more thing. In this uncertain world we have unwisely allowed our Armed Forces to fall to a dangerously low level. I passionately believe the Government’s first duty is to defend its people from foreign and domestic threats.

And so, on these and many other issues, I want to offer residents of Edinburgh South the pragmatic, common sense alternative which Reform UK provides. I have experience and local knowledge. Join me. Vote for sensible policies. Vote Reform UK.

I’m standing as a candidate because our communities deserve better than rising costs, stretched services and a lack of real opportunity.

My family have called this area home since the beginning of the last century and have deep roots into the community.

Raised by a single‑parent mum who became a social worker, she worked hard to build a better life. I went to school in East Lothian, studied at Edinburgh University, became a teacher and taught in some of the toughest schools, and now run a digital learning business supporting organisations with compliance policy.

I’m standing to bring practical, community‑focused solutions: easing the cost of living, improving local services, supporting schools and ensuring housing works for local people.

Holly is a culture policy researcher fighting for more funding and stability for artists, musicians, makers and freelancers. As the culture spokesperson for the Glasgow Greens' campaign, she also wants to ensure better opportunities for women and non-binary people in sport, and seeks to protect grassroots culture venues.

Holly's priorities for Langside are based on hundreds of chats with local residents throughout her campaign. She is determined to:

- Amplify residents' voices in planning applications like Shawlands Arcade and Mount Florida Bowling Club.

statement_to_voters:
"My heart didn't start in Scotland, but it found its home here—and my soul found its peace."
In 2011, I did more than just move; I voted with my feet. I brought my family here because I fell in love with a Scotland that dared to dream of a different, fairer course. I am a Muslim and a ‘New Scot’ by choice, and every day I am inspired by the rugged beauty of this land and the unbreakable spirit of its people.
I carry the radical fire of Jimmy Reid, John MacLean, and Mary Barbour. But I also carry the wisdom of my mentors: the late Alex Salmond, who showed us how to lead with a vision of a sovereign nation, and George Galloway, who reminds us that the working class deserves a voice that refuses to be silenced.
My roots are in the pits, steel plants, and shipyards of the North East of England, woven into the fabric of the trade union movement. As an award-winning journalist and war correspondent, I’ve spent my life uncovering the truths the powerful want buried. I’ve stared death in the face—locked up by the Taliban while pursuing a story, and later kidnapped by heavily armed Israeli forces on a humanitarian flotilla bringing food to Gaza. Held as a hostage in K'ziot prison, I went on a 7-day hunger strike for my dignity. I know what it means to fight when the world is looking the other way.
Today, I am standing in the heart of Glasgow Cathcart and Pollok, a constituency steeped in the grit and glory of working-class Glasgow. From the industrial history of the White Cart Water to the defiant spirit of the Pollok Free State, this is a place that knows how to stand its ground. I see a political establishment that has lost its way, and I see communities here being left behind by young, career politicians who have never known a day's struggle in their puff. That is why I am standing for the Workers Party Scotland.
We are the party of peace in a time of warmongering. No to NATO, no to conscription, no to nukes, and a definitive 'no' to the foreign wars that drain our treasury while our own people shiver.
We are the party of common sense and dignity. We will kill the GRR Bill to protect the hard-won rights of women and the safety of our daughters. We will stand as a shield for our pensioners, ensuring those who built this country are never forced to sell their homes just to survive their final years in care.
I am not a career politician; I am a worker, a mother, and a journalist who is tired of waiting for change. I am choosing to be that change. Scotland’s future shouldn't belong to the bureaucrats in Brussels or the bankers in London—it belongs to the workers, the builders, and the dreamers of Cathcart and Pollok who keep this country moving.
Support me and I will be your voice—I have a strong voice, and I ken how to use it. For a Scotland that is sovereign, socialist, and at peace: let's put the power back where it belongs ... with the people.

Tax Reform to free up those people on lower wages from paying income tax and corporation tax reform for businesses.

Housing needs urgent attention; we need to review the planning system and prioritise local people who have paid into the system above and beyond those foreign nationals who jump the housing queues in most parts of the UK.

The Scottish Conservative Party has announced the selection of Councillor Ruraidh Stewart as the party’s candidate for Inverness and Nairn in the 2026 Scottish Parliament election. The announcement was made at a well-attended meeting of local members in Inverness.

Born and raised in the Highlands, Ruraidh Stewart has spent his life living and working in the region. A respected figure in Highland politics, he made history in 2022 by becoming the first Conservative elected to represent Skye on Highland Council. As Leader of the Conservative Group on the Council, Stewart has earned a reputation for principled, energetic leadership and his ability to connect across communities with a practical, people-first approach.

"I am honoured to have been selected as the Scottish Conservative candidate for Inverness and Nairn," said Mr Stewart. "Other than my time studying in St Andrews, the Highlands have always been home. I've lived here and worked here and  I understand the challenges we face but I also see the resilience, pride, and potential in our communities and I’m committed to standing up for them in Holyrood."

Mr Stewart’s campaign will focus on restoring trust in government, reversing centralisation, and delivering for Highland communities. He has consistently opposed SNP policies that he argues ignore the distinct needs of the Highlands.

"Our communities deserve support that’s flexible, local, and respectful of their unique needs. Sadly, we’ve seen too much centralisation under the SNP—decisions being made in Edinburgh that simply don’t fit the realities of life in the Highlands. It’s time to bring power and trust back to our local areas, where we know what works best for our families, our services, and our future."

"Let’s be honest about where things stand," said Mr Stewart. "The A9 was supposed to be fully dualled by 2025—now we’re being told it might not be finished until 2035, or even later. At Raigmore Hospital, one in five patients are waiting more than 12 weeks just to be seen as outpatients. NHS Highland is under real strain, with staffing shortages reaching 25% in some of our most remote communities. Meanwhile, roads continue to deteriorate and ferries sit idle in shipyards, all while SNP ministers seem more focused on spinning headlines than actually fixing the problems that matter to people here in the Highlands."

"People in Inverness, Nairn, and across the Highlands aren’t asking for the moon. They just want roads that are safe, healthcare that’s accessible, and local services that actually work. They deserve a government that listens, acts, and delivers."

"While the SNP obsesses over yet another divisive referendum, they’ve taken their eye off the ball. It’s Highland families who are left picking up the pieces."

With deep roots in the Highlands, a proven record of principled leadership, and a commitment to restoring common-sense government, Ruraidh Stewart offers a dynamic and credible alternative to the status quo at Holyrood.

party_description_text: Vote Scottish Greens - Let's demand better
statement_to_voters:
Anne Thomas is one of the prospective Scottish Green Party Candidates for Highlands and Islands Region. Anne says, “We are now seeing the effect of climate change in ever more extreme weather which has devastating effects on homes, livelihoods and travel.“ I want local climate action focusing on better buses and trains when people need them, insulation for warmer homes and a public energy company which keeps money in the area and reduces poverty. Anne was a children’s speech therapist working with children with additional needs.
She is Convenor of the charity Transition Black Isle which runs community markets encourages people to grow food, an energy saving project, encourages active travel and now runs the Black Isle Library of Things including a Bike Library and other active travel projects.
Anne helped lead successful campaigns to persuade the Highland Council to declare a climate emergency, and exclude climate damaging companies from pension investments.
She is a Trustee of Highland People’s Power which is running a project to provide solar on the roofs of community buildings, reducing their energy costs and climate impact and also seeks community ownership of renewables rather than all the profits going elsewhere.  She is Treasurer of Highland Cycle Campaign which advocates and lobbies for better cycling provision. She has delivered projects to cut the environmental impact of food, travel and energy e.g solar panels for my church.”
She is Vice Chair of North Kessock Residents Association which runs North Kessock Community Larder which cuts food waste and helps people make ends meet.
Anne says "I am already delivering for the community. As your MSP I could do even more. I want to build a world worth living in for my grand-children, where people and nature can thrive"

My name is Jon Whitton and I am a 56 year old retired Nurse, who has spent the past 30 years caring for patients in both the NHS and the private care sector.

I moved to Scotland with my wife in 2013 and quickly immersed myself in the local area, engaging in many rural pursuits and building relationships with the local farming community.

I have not previously been involved in politics but in December 2024 felt compelled to act. This was due to poor health services, rising energy cost and declining infrastructure in Scotland. I joined Reform UK Scotland and hit the floor running, taking up the role of Treasurer and Constituency Organiser in my local area.

In the summer of 2025 I answered Reforms call for ordinary regular people to come forward and stand as a candidate. After successful vetting and interviews I was announced as the candidate for Skye, Lochaber and Badenoch at this years Scottish Conference.

party_description_text: Vote Scottish Greens - Let's demand better
statement_to_voters: Dr Kate Willis is the first Scottish Green candidate to stand in council elections in Lochaber. She has lived in the Highlands for nearly 20 years, working as a marine ecologist, a wildlife tour guide and currently works for Lochaber Environmental Group developing projects to improve local active travel opportunities and cycling infrastructure. Many in Lochaber will know Kate from the work she is doing to deliver HIBIKE, the Fort William ebike share scheme. Kate has also campaigned for many years for government action on the climate and ecological crises and is passionate about rewilding and local food production. Kate says: "Looking to the future after COP26, we need to think longer-term and do everything we can to ensure the right decisions are made in the coming months and years to deliver a brighter future. As a Green councillor, I would work with residents and communities helping to improve democratic systems of local governance, building inclusive, resilient communities with affordable warm homes for all and improved active travel infrastructure and public transport. With a more local focus, we could concentrate on building a circular economy that supports and promotes local enterprises and jobs, while reducing waste and carbon emissions. To tackle the climate and ecological crises we need to work together to build a stronger, sustainable future and a greener fairer world for our children and grandchildren to grow up in."

John Beare has lived in Markinch since 1998 and has represented the area as a local councillor since 2006. He was elected to Fife Council in a by-election with a 30% swing from the incumbent Labour Party. He has had several leadership roles at the Council since 2007, including terms as Convener of the Standards and Audit Committee and the Planning Committee. He is currently an opposition member of the Cabinet regularly challenging the Labour/Tory/Liberal coalition that runs the Council, while also providing practical solutions to issues facing communities across Fife. He is also the Fife SNP Group Business Manager.Since 2015 John has been Office Manager for Peter Grant, the outgoing MP for Glenrothes, having previously worked for an MSP. Prior to this he had a long career in commercial construction, aiding the construction of schools, hospitals, television studios, factories and clean rooms across these islands.John has held many roles in the SNP including as Organisation Convener of the Party, a member of the National Executive Committee, Convener of the SNP Candidate Assessment Committee, Agent for the SNP for several elections and most proudly as the Yes Scotland Agent for Fife in 2014.John has a long political CV, strong local knowledge, experience of Holyrood and Westminster, and a history of hard work and complete dedication to realising Independence for Scotland.

From Dollar to Dunfermline it's evident people want to put climate action into their day-to-day choices but frankly they can’t afford to. Voting Green is no waste of a vote, it puts climate action and social justice into the conversations and future decision making of the next UK Government. Independence would be pointless without a liveable planet, that’s why people should vote to deliver independence for a Green Scotland.

From growing up on a council estate in a working family needing benefits to smashing glass ceilings at The Scotsman, I am proof of the power of Labour governments. I'm a councillor and a campaigner who has been one of the leading voices in changing the UK landscape around menopause. I've worked with everyone from politicians and peers to prison officers and foodbank staff, been named a Big Issue Changemaker and appeared in national and international media, TV and radio. I have created real change in both attitudes and law. But I'm still a working woman and I know the challenges that face each and every one of us -- but I also know that a better way is possible. I've lived it. The SNP and the Tories have nothing left to give Angus & Perthshire Glens. Only Scottish Labour can deliver the change Scotland needs. It's time for a fresh start and a fresh voice.

I have decided to stand in this election because I have become increasingly disappointed in the calibre of our politicians and feel that after 19 years the SNP needs to be challenged. I believe that for too long the people elected to represent us have represented their party first and put the electorate second.

Eva has served as a family and child lawyer in the community for 40 years, her home, her work and her heart are in the constituency and she understands local needs and values because like you she lives there.

She self-describes as the ‘bionic candidate’ because the Scottish NHS saved her life; giving her a couple of dozen prosthetic legs over the years and custom-building her a titanium spine.

Eva will always put her constituents first.  A strong fighter for women and children's rights, she strives for true equality and is determined to see the eradication of poverty, financial and of aspiration.

Eva said.  “Since Scotland’s independent autonomous education and health services created and rebuilt me, I intend to return the favour by helping to rebuild Scotland as an independent, peace loving egalitarian country where everyone can afford to eat and heat free from the threats of poverty and homelessness. Scotland's greatest resource is our people and we all deserve the chance to realise our full potential.”

I have dedicated my entire working life to public service having served for 25 years in the British Army, completing operational tours around the world.

After leaving the Army, I continued my service to my country by working for 15 years as a civil servant with the Ministry of Defence.

For the past seven years, I have chaired a community group in Cowdenbeath. This experience gave me a hunger to make a real difference to local people’s lives. With my background in leadership, problem-solving, and community engagement, I believe the time is right for me to stand as your candidate for the Scottish Parliamentary elections.

Jim has lived, worked and campaigned in this constituency his whole life. He was born in Perth, brought up in Tarvie Place, Letham and now lives just five minutes from Methven. He began his career as a shepherd before becoming a hill farmer and building a successful food and events-based catering business alongside it. He has extensive experience in urban and rural affairs and background in farming as the Founder of Farmers For Yes.

Hello, my name is John Forbes and I will be standing as a candidate in the upcoming Scottish elections in May in the Mid Scotland and Fife region representing Independence for Scotland party ISP.

We are a Scottish party in Scotland for the people of Scotland, a true grassroots Scottish Independence party who advocates for:
Direct Democracy.
Protecting Woman's rights.
Protecting Children's rights.
Minimum Income Guarantee.
No to Freeport’s & Investment zones.

About me and why am I standing.
I'm a bricklayer to trade and have worked in the construction industry since leaving high school.
I was born in Dunfermline when Dunfermline had its own maternity hospital, one of four hospitals that were in Dunfermline at that time. Unlike what Fife as a whole has now the third largest council are in Scotland with only one acute hospital and a handful of community hospitals.

There was a time when getting a GP and Dentist wasn't difficult but With the rapid expansion of Mid Scotland and Fife the new houses and new families who move into them are being built without a thought of a workable infrastructure in place to support the good folk from Dunfermline and every other town and village in the mid Scotland region the reality now being folk now find it very difficult to get either a Dentist or a GP appointment this putting even more pressure on A+E and in turn causing even longer waiting times.

My education came from a primary school and high school in Dunfermline when classroom sizes were acceptable unlike what things are like now with some classroom sizes bursting at the seams leaving teachers stressed out and many leaving the profession.

I volunteer with a Fife mens suicide prevention mental health charity and see and hear first hand just how much society is broken. The cost of living crisis is making life even harder for too many sovereign scots causing many to rapidly spiral into depression.

When I left high school there were far more opportunities for employment back then for school leavers compared to the sad state of affairs now school leavers face.

I was proud to be part of the Freeport’s and special economic zone campaign group for just under two years. As a group we held public meetings all over Scotland informing the members of public who attended the public meetings just exactly what was going on where they lived, needless to say many felt angry when finding out just exactly what these abominations, states within a state, land grabs and basically privatisation of Scotland through the back door were and why were the politicians silent.

I'm standing as a candidate for the Mid Scotland and Fife region as things need to change politically in Scotland removing the feeling of being scunnered with too many of voters thinking whats the point of voting as nothing will change, Removing the thought of Sleekit when thinking of politicians as trust has been all but obliterated.
With any sort of change it has to start somewhere and as your MSP I plan through hard work to gain your trust not through words but with actions with every fibre of my being. Granted I don't have a university degree in politics, I'm a bricklayer to trade but come with a masters degree in real lived life experience so understand only too well the struggles many face both financially and mentally on a daily basis as I've travelled that road.
I believe passionately that Scotland should be an Independent sovereign nation and have a transparent Scottish political system eliminating closed door policy making with Direct Democracy returning the power to the rightful owners, every single Sovereign Scot and returning the true meaning of a public servant from what many feel that the people working for the politicians instead of what it should be the politicians working for the people.
I will be as your MSP a politician who will listen, and fight for every single constitute living in the mid Scotland and Fife region as I'm certainly not shy to putting in a hard shift.
What I won't be is the type of MSP that is visible briefly before and after an election then dissappear.

Its time for Scotland to get off knees and be the successful Independent Sovereign Nation it can be.

Fraser Graham, a Chartered Architect, is the Scottish Liberal Democrat candidate for the Cowdenbeath and Kirkcaldy Constituency. Growing up in Fife, Fraser has deep roots in the area and a strong commitment to serving his community. His family continues to reside within the constituency, further strengthening his personal connection to the region.

Fraser's professional career as an architect is marked by a dedication to sustainable and community-focused design, reflecting his belief in the power of thoughtful, innovative solutions to improve lives.

With a passion for social justice, environmental sustainability, and economic development, Fraser brings a fresh and dynamic perspective to the political landscape. His campaign is driven by a desire to create inclusive, thriving communities where everyone can succeed.

Fraser's candidacy for the Cowdenbeath and Kirkcaldy Constituency embodies the values of the Scottish Liberal Democrats: fairness, opportunity, and a commitment to building a better future for all. He is eager to leverage his professional skills and personal dedication to effect positive change and represent the interests of all residents.

At Scottish Labour we know that we must deliver economic growth to make Scotland a success for all our citizens, and that the rewards of growth need to be tipped more in favour of our communities and workers.  Scotland's people deserve a future with opportunities for all, where kindness, hard work, and ambition lead to great outcomes. With a Labour government now in Westminster, we have a unique opportunity to energise the ambition for Scotland too.

I am a qualified accountant with over 25 years’ experience as a business leader and board director across manufacturing, oil & gas, livestock auctioning, property investment, commercial law, and financial services. These enterprises were substantial, but notably, they supported small and medium sized businesses, which account for nearly 60% of all jobs in Scotland. My experience extends to working with, and influencing, regulators, policy makers, and sector bodies. For over 24 years, my husband and I have lived in the constituency, where we raised our four children.

Fiona Law was elected as a Councillor for Clackmannanshire North in May 2022 and the Spokesperson for Environment and Net Zero. In January 2025, she became the Deputy Spokesperson for People – Health and Adult Social Care, Community and Criminal Justice. Before becoming a local Councillor Fiona worked in financial services – quality assurance and complaints. She also trained as a volunteer breastfeeding supporter, volunteering on the postnatal wards at Forth Valley Royal Hospital and established a local online support group to help families with breastfeeding support during COVID.

Do the political parties say what you think?

The Holyrood parties, from the Greens to the Conservatives, argue about independence, but they unite to endorse the dramatic cultural changes that are damaging our society.

It doesn’t matter which Holyrood Party you vote for……

Kids are taught that they can choose whether to be a boy or a girl, while a flawed philosophy of education leads to a breakdown of discipline and, inevitably, declining academic standards.  The state increasingly sees itself as the guardian of children, ensuring that parents are adhering to their narrow philosophy of child-rearing.

Differences between men and women are regarded as problems to eliminate rather than expressions of diversity to celebrate.

The state claims it wants to help families yet offers no help to couples where one parent wants to devote more of their time to their children.

Abortion continues unquestioned.  No party opposes assisted suicide.

The breakdown of family structure is not recognised as the source of countless problems.

The value of marriage in producing stable families is dismissed as archaic.

Freedom of speech is constantly being restricted, and democracy is therefore undermined.

Have the mainstream parties learnt that they can ignore our opinions because we will keep voting for them as they trample our deepest principles? It’s time to stand up for what we believe in.

I would would challenge the cosy consensus in Holyrood, and rigorous argument would replace the monochrome virtue signalling that currently passes for political debate on many key issues.

The Scottish Family Party will stand strongly against the tide that is eroding the foundations of our society, and offer in its place a fresh, bold and positive vision of a better Scotland for all.

I am standing as the Reform Scottish Parliamentary Candidate for Perthshire South and Kinross-shire.  I am also the first Candidate on the Mid Scotland and Fife Regional List.  Please vote Reform on both ballot papers.

I grew up in the far north of Scotland and, apart from a year in Kent, I have lived, worked and brought up my family in different parts of Scotland – firstly as a veterinary surgeon and latterly working for environmental charities on public affairs and media.  These varied experiences have given me a broad view of the needs of Scottish people and has led me to want to work for improved lives and communities locally and across Scotland.

I have lived in beautiful rural Perthshire for twenty-three years and I’m well aware of the challenges in rural areas. In recent years, we’ve seen the loss of post offices and police stations, poor public transport, and a drastic need for housing and jobs to help young people remain in the area. Perth city centre needs regeneration and Reform’s small business policies would encourage new entrepreneurs.  Our policy for lowering taxes will help people in work and pensioners.

Standing up for local and national concerns

I have an active and successful campaigning record, including leading a campaign to protect our wild peatlands, leading to Wild Land Areas being recognised as a national asset by the Scottish Government in Scottish Planning Policy. I have campaigned  and given evidence in Public Local Inquiries against the deluge of industrial energy projects - such as the proposed 200m turbines in Perthshire and Kinross-shire.  I have been an unpaid carer and  know that both social care and the NHS need reform.

I am an experienced public speaker and I know how the Scottish Parliament works, not just in the debating chamber but in Committees and other events, so I will be able to engage and effect change immediately.  And how needed that change is!

Reform’s policies on key issues are needed to revive our economy and restore healthy communities.  Please vote Reform on both ballot papers, and I hope that you will come and visit me in the parliament after 8th May!

Susan McGill, Health Worker

Susan has been honoured, over the last 5 years, to serve as SNP Councillor for Stirling North Ward. She has found the role of Councillor both exhilarating and challenging, and has gained a great deal of knowledge. Susan finds satisfaction in assisting constituents with issues and concerns affecting them.

Susan has worked in the NHS for 43 years, in a variety of areas and settings. She is a Registered Nurse, Midwife and Health Visitor and continues to work part-time as a Health Visitor.

Serving as the constituents of Stirling North as their Councillor has been her privilege, and Susan would be delighted to serve for a further term.

Laurie Moffat is a proud resident of Cardenden, with deep roots in the historic coalfields of the Lochgelly, Cardenden, and Benarty area. Born in Sydney, Australia, in 1981 to Scottish parents who emigrated under the £10 Pom scheme, he returned with his family to Fife at the age of three and was raised in Bowhill. His family connections extend to Ballingry (his father’s birthplace), Lumphinnans (his Di’s birthplace, known as Fife’s “Little Moscow”), Bowhill (his mother’s birthplace), and relatives still living in Cowdenbeath.

A former pupil of Lochgelly High School and a graduate of Robert Gordon University, Laurie is a former rugby player and the youngest-ever club captain of Glenrothes RFC, demonstrating proven leadership and community spirit.

With over 15 years of experience in the subsea oil and gas sector before transitioning to the biogas industry—where he managed the Anaerobic Digestion plant at Inchdairnie near Kinglassie—Laurie advocates for an all-energy future for Scotland. This includes renewables, biogas, nuclear, and sustainable hydrocarbons through a pragmatic, worker-focused transition that prioritises job protection.

As a dedicated Georgist, sovereign money advocate, and land reformer, Laurie draws inspiration from Henry George, Positive Money’s work on sovereign money systems, the Scottish Land Revenue Group’s promotion of Annual Ground Rent as a form of land value taxation, and Scotland’s historical land reform movement from the Highland Clearances to contemporary efforts for community ownership. He supports public control of money creation, the end of fractional reserve lending, and a shift in the tax burden from labour and productive capital onto unearned land values—ensuring that community-created land value benefits the public while reducing taxes on wages, earnings, investment, and business.

Inspired by Salvo and the Claim of Right—including his signing of the Edinburgh Proclamation—Laurie is committed to restoring full Scottish sovereignty, independent from both the United Kingdom and the European Union. In line with Sovereignty party policy, he supports the legitimacy of a Unilateral Declaration of Independence (UDI), conditional on a democratic majority of the Scottish electorate voting for pro-independence parties. This approach emphasises radical action by the Scottish people themselves, without seeking permission from Westminster—a path successfully pursued by other nations to achieve sovereignty.

He will stand for Sovereignty in the Cowdenbeath constituency and for Liberate Scotland on the Mid Scotland and Fife regional list in the 2026 Scottish Parliament election.

If elected, Laurie will combat de-industrialisation, oppose freeports that risk privatising public resources, and advance Georgist, sovereign money, and land reform policies to foster regeneration, create sustainable jobs, revive derelict sites for community benefit, and secure long-term prosperity and robust public services across the Cowdenbeath constituency and beyond.

Kenneth Morton is a qualified Rural Chartered Surveyor, Agricultural Valuer and Project Manager, who has worked in various positions for private estates, civil service, third sector, consultancies and energy companies for over 25 years having dealt with matters ranging from housing, forestry, agriculture, renewables, the environment, as well as energy transmission and generation.

Born and raised in rural Ayrshire and settled in rural Perthshire via Lancashire and Stirlingshire and now living in Perthshire on the edge of the Carse of Gowrie with his wife Karen and a Springer Spaniel, Kenneth has been at the forefront of witnessing the collapse of rural areas following the closure of coal mining, has witnessed the lack of joined up thinking with regards agriculture, forestry, energy policy, the damage done to the marine ecosystems due to over fishing, not to mention the degradation of the NHS, public infrastructure and transport, and our once leading education system as he has gone through life.

Kenneth was one of the first to stand for Reform UK in the 2021 Holyrood election and has been an active proponent of Reform UK since then having also stood in the 2024 Westminster election, delivering thousands of leaflets to the constituency, attending hustings and responding to government consultations both personally and on behalf of Reform UK.

As well as giving energy generation, grid planning, agricultural and environmental talks to local members branches, Kenneth wrote the 2021 energy policy paper for Reform UK and has input into Reform UK policy on a number of matters.

If elected to Holyrood, Kenneth intends to help reform Holyrood and better align its policies to support Scots in their everyday lives, reduce the burden of over regulation and put Scottish economic growth back in a positive direction.

Kenneth’s top priorities for North Perthshire are as follows: -

•	Reduce the cost of energy, and thereby lowering the cost of living for everyone and businesses
•	Reform the agricultural support system and reduce the bureaucracy on farmers
•	Reduce taxes to enable people to spend their hard earned money on what they want
•	Reform the NHS to reduce waiting lists

People often quote that the “nail that sticks up, is the first to be hammered down”, but the truth is if no one stands up, then everyone is hammered flat and nothing will change, Kenneth is standing up and ready to represent the people of North Perthshire and invites you to do so as well, and to join him in building a better Scotland by supporting and voting for Reform UK in the upcoming Holyrood election.”

I'm Calum from Kirkcaldy, I'm a freelancer in the film industry and an volunteer for 2 educational charities. I want to get into government to leave you alone. If elected I'd so what's possible to stop the government going out of control with our civil liberties. I'm also not entirely convinced that our government is as efficient with our money as it should be, and that too many resources are being diverted to unimportant yet fashionable issues. I'd do what I can to keep our government humble, and accountable.

When Mark was first in Parliament in 2003 he introduced the first-ever legislative proposal on climate change targets. This time round his ambition has only grown. Mark has got millions of pounds put into creating marine protected areas, a citizens assembly on climate change set up, and stopped the destruction of Scotland’s kelp forests. He is also a keen cyclist and advocate for road safety, and led a campaign to change the default speed limit In urban areas to 20mph. Mark has worked closely with communities securing millions of pounds to develop proposals for new rail stations, campaigning to stop developments and holding corporations such as Exxon-Mobil and Edinburgh Airport to account.

Born in Glasgow in 1973, Alyn grew up in Scotland and Saudi Arabia, returning to Scotland in 1986. Having studied Law and European Law, Alyn also gained a Masters degree in European Studies from the College of Europe in Warsaw. After a year teaching English in India and working with Scotland Europa in Brussels, Alyn qualified as a lawyer, practising commercial law in London and Edinburgh. In 2002, he started working for Richard Lochhead MSP, then the SNP Group at Holyrood as an adviser on European, Justice and Business policies. Alyn was elected to the European Parliament for the first time in 2004 and was re-elected three times in the SNP victories in the 2009, 2014 and 2019 elections - in 2019 as leading SNP candidate, winning the best ever result for the SNP in a European election. In the December 2019 Westminster election, Alyn won the Stirling seat with 51% of the vote. He pledged at the declaration to represent everyone in Stirling regardless of politics. In the Westminster Parliament, he served as the SNP Europe and EU Accession Spokesperson. Alyn maintains a keen interest in Agriculture, Food and Animal Welfare issues. He is the Honorary Vice President of the Scottish Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals, the SSPCA, Scotland’s animal welfare charity, and is an Honorary Associate of the British Veterinary Association. Both posts are voluntary and unpaid.

Darren is your Scottish Conservative and Unionist candidate for the Cowdenbeath ward and has represented the ward as a Councillor since 2017.

Darren lives in Cowdenbeath with his wife and three children and experiences the same issues and concerns that affect local residents.

As a Conservative, Darren firmly believes in localism and that local people know what is best for the area in which they live, and that decisions should be made locally and not by anonymous individuals based elsewhere.

If re-elected, Darren will continue to work tirelessly within your community and be the strong, dedicated representative you deserve.
