I’m an experienced Operations Director in manufacturing, have worked in the rural sector and am a wife and mother. Our politicians have lost your trust. That changes now.

I’ll protect women and young girls, fight for safer communities, better local services, real economic opportunity and employment. No excuses, no empty promises – just action to deliver real change. My ambition is to rebuild prosperity for everyone in Stirling, the surrounding towns and rural areas.

I am delighted to have been selected as the candidate for Dundee City East.

Too many families in Dundee are living in poverty, and child hunger is a daily reality. Young people have seen services cut and opportunities dry up, while households are struggling with rising bills and insecure jobs. That has to change.

Scottish Liberal Democrats believe in fairness for everyone, no matter who you are or where you come from. We've got a realistic plan to get things done.

We will fight child poverty and give families the support they deserve. We will create opportunities for young people through apprenticeships, skills, and safe spaces. We'll back local businesses to grow and bring investment into Dundee, creating secure, well-paid jobs. And we'll fix our NHS — cutting waiting times, supporting carers, and making sure everyone can see a GP, dentist or mental health professional when they need to.

Dundee City East deserves better. And with the Scottish Liberal Democrats, you can vote for it.
Our central focus of strengthening family life could help solve many problems in Scotland, from drug abuse to youth mental health, from crime to population decline.

It is an honour to be the candidate for West Aberdeenshire and Kincardine. The North East has been my home since 2005, my roots are here and it is where I have chosen to bring up my family.

I was elected as a local councillor in 2022 and since then have led on education for the Labour group and campaigned for children to be able to walk and cycle safely to school. I was motivated to stand as a councillor because of my concern for the world that my children and other children were growing up in. The impact of climate change is apparent in the north east as elsewhere and I want my children to grow up in a greener, fairer Britain than we have today.

I have worked in further and higher education for over twenty years. Our young people are being failed by broken promises from both governments at Westminster and Holyrood. Broken promises on class sizes, teacher numbers, attainment, nursery places, I could go on. Our FE colleges are in crisis and our universities are suffering from a completely broken funding model.

I hope to play a part in helping a Labour Government to make a difference to our communities here in West Aberdeenshire and Kincardine. If you live in the constituency and have any questions or thoughts please do get in touch.

Scotland deserves change, give me an opportunity to be that change.
I want the people of Scotland to be wealthier and healthier, this can be achieved through Independence.
Let's keep the globalists out of Scottish politics.
I never intended to get involved in politics but it's time for real people to step up.
#Independencenothingless

Maggie was our lead candidate in 2014 when we achieved the greatest number of Green votes in a European election. She has been co-convenor of the party, Rector of Aberdeen University and a director of the Scottish Independence Convention. She is former Edinburgh City councillor where she introduced participatory budgeting. She has campaigned for employment rights, gender equality, equal recognition for all LGBTQ+ people, animal welfare and peace.

Together we can elect Maggie to Holyrood to fight for climate action, and for our future as an independent country at the heart of the European Union.

I live in Aberdeenshire with my family but spent most of my life in vibrant cities. I am deeply passionate about the region’s culture, history, and natural beauty and the deep links between Aberdeen and the rest of the North East. I am committed to build a fairer Scotland.

I will prioritise education, strong ties to Europe, and a sustainable future for the North-east.

My constituents know me as their “hands-on Councillor” - just as ready to help unblock a drain as to campaign for a fairer deal from the Scottish Government for our Councils.

Working with communities has been at the heart of my decades of volunteering work and inspired my political engagement. For the last decade, I have been volunteering in Aberdeen Central and created strong links with the third sector and business community.

I was born in Taiwan and raised in Brussels, now I am working and volunteering in the City and Shire. This gives me a unique perspective blending local insight with knowledge of how things can be done differently to make the North East a more compassionate and more prosperous place to live.

For media enquiries: media@scotlibdems.org.uk

I am standing on a manifesto of change. Change that will allow people to thrive not just survive. Change that will deliver a brighter future for everyone. I'm not going to ask you to vote for me to stop someone else winning, but instead to help return a Labour Government. Only Labour can make the changes we so desperately need from Westminster. A vote for me is a vote for a strong local voice in Westminster as your Labour MP.

David grew up in Newburgh and loves the communities that make up Aberdeenshire East.

The whole region has been let down, by both Labour and Conservative Governments in Westminster and the SNP Government in Holyrood. Jobs in our energy sector are being lost, and our public services are creaking under the pressure of chronic under funding.

With a decade of experience in campaigns, David has worked to protect energy sector jobs in the North East, fought for funding for our struggling NHS, and stood to protect foundational British industries.

Speaking to people right across the constituency, David understands that you feel let down and left behind.

For media enquiries: media@scotlibdems.org.uk

I live in Aberdeenshire, am a former nurse and midwife and have dedicated much of my life to caring about others and helping with the well-being of my community. As a health and wellness coach, I continue to help individuals’ lead healthier lives.

I am a proud mother of two and my journey into politics is motivated by a deep-seated belief that the current political leaders no longer serve the true interests of the people. I had no previous inclination towards politics but decided that we can all moan about it but nothing changes if we don’t take action.  So my candidacy is a commitment to honesty, transparency, and genuine representation.

Why Am I Standing for Reform

I grew up on a farm and am passionate about UK farming and fishing, recognizing these industries are vital to the nation’s heritage and economy.  A country cannot be self-sufficient if it cannot feed itself and our farmers are struggling as the Net Zero regulations clamp down on them.  The price of red diesel has been increased, farmers are being paid to re-wild their land thus reducing the number of crop producing fields and now with the closure of Britain’s only ammonia plant, there will be a huge increase in fertiliser costs.  It is Reform UK policy to increase the farming budget to £3 Billion with a focus on smaller farms, to keep farmland in use to produce food and to bring young people into farming. WE want to help our farmers to carry on farming. It is also another Reform UK policy to renegotiate our fishing arrangements with EU and help our fishing communities build back the communities and heritage.

I want to help empower local communities and be a voice on their behalf and bring about real change, restoring trust in political leadership and fostering a brighter future for all.

I am proud to call Scotland my home, with family roots on my mother’s side going back generations, and I have lived in the Northeast for ten years. I am standing for AdvanceUK to ensure local voices are heard and communities are supported. My priorities include affordable housing, strong schools with mental health support, better NHS services close to home, proactive infrastructure improvements, and investment in local economies. I am committed to practical solutions that make a real difference in people’s lives.

I care deeply about Aberdeen. It’s where I was born, where I grew up and where I live today. I have spent my whole career in the hospitality industry. My husband Harry and I opened Pappagallos Café on Holburn Street in 1990, and we have gone on to run restaurants and holiday accommodation in Ballater and Pennan.
I have been a member of Reform UK since 2024, and I am the Area Organiser for Aberdeen City and Kincardine.
I have always been passionate about serving my community and for 26 years I was a member and Chair of the Childrens’ Panels in Aberdeen City and Aberdeenshire. I have also been a longstanding volunteer in my local churches.
I’m not a career politician. I am someone who has experience of life, knows the challenges of running a small business and has spent many years serving their community as a volunteer.
Aberdeen Donside needs and deserves stronger representation, and I can deliver that.

I believe in the value of human life and that all human life is valuable and equal. We value Family as the building block of society and would seek to promote Family and marriage through our policies such as a wedding box, tax changes to support families. Scotland faces a demographic crisis, with an ageing population and extremely low birthrate. The Family Party would seek to reverse this decline, for the future stability of Scotland.

The Scottish Family Party opposes abortion and opposes assisted suicide as a matter of principle.

Our manifesto is full of unique and well rounded policies to address many aspects of Scotlands needs. This is available at scottishfamily.org

Allan Petrie ISP Candidate for North East Scotland.

Allan Petrie is a lifelong independence supporter, trade unionist and community campaigner from Dundee who has spent more than three decades standing up for ordinary people.

With a strong trade union background and years of grassroots activism, Allan has fought for workers, defended public services and challenged decisions that harm local communities.

He is also widely known for his voluntary advocacy work supporting carers, disabled people and families with complex medical needs. As a carer himself, Allan understands first-hand how broken systems affect real lives.

In 2018 he led the campaign that stopped the closure of Glenlaw House, a respite centre for children with complex medical needs. Against the odds, the campaign forced the reversal of cuts and protected a lifeline service for vulnerable families, proof that determined communities can win when someone is prepared to fight alongside them.

For Allan, independence is not just a constitutional argument, it is the tool Scotland needs to build a country where communities matter more than party machines.

Standing for the Independence for Scotland Party in North East Scotland, Allan is clear:
Scotland deserves the power to govern itself and communities deserve representatives who actually get things done.

I am your Reform UK Candidate for Angus South, great farming country. I was born and brought
up on a farm and went to the Agricultural College.

My first job was up Glenisla. Even as a young man I could see the effects of Government interference and soon I wanted something else.

I joined the Army and trained as a surveyor, travelling the World extensively. This gave me a much broader perspective on life and experienced how other parts of the World manage their
affairs.

When I retired from the Military as a Regimental Sergeant Major I retrained to enter the London Finance Sector. After a period I set up my own business in Manchester, which I later sold. On taking early retirement I returned to Scotland and built my own home.

I have always been interested in politics and when I look at the Arbroath High St, it made me search for a political party that had the courage to do something about it rather than wringing their hands as they watch miserable decline, in health, wealth and education, all of which are
devolved to the Scottish Parliament.

That is why I chose to stand for Reform UK, they have the
courage and vision to Reform and I get things done.

My name is Brian Stewart, and I am proud to be standing as a list candidate for the Scottish Socialist Party in North East Scotland.

I’m not a career politician - I’m someone who understands the pressures facing ordinary people, because I live them too.

Across Dundee and Angus, I’ve spoken to people struggling with the cost of living, rising energy bills, and public services that are stretched to their limits. Too many feel that politics no longer works for them.

I’m standing to change that.

The Scottish Socialist Party believes in a fairer Scotland - one where wealth is shared more equally, public services are properly funded, and working people are treated with the dignity and respect they deserve.

We are campaigning for real change, including replacing the unfair Council Tax with a fair income-based system, investing in our NHS, and ensuring politicians are accountable by taking a worker’s wage.

I believe politics should be about public service, not personal gain.

This election is an opportunity to send a message - that people want something better.

If you believe in a fairer, more equal Scotland, I ask for your support on the regional list.

Together, we can build a future that works for all of us.

I was elected as an Aberdeenshire Councillor in May 2022, representing North Kincardine. I've lived in Portlethen since the last millennium – moving here just before graduating from the University of Aberdeen with a degree in Economic Science. My husband is a Scout leader here and our four children attend/ed local schools.

I believe we all share responsibility for making our communities the best place they can be. I’ve contributed my time and energy whenever I’ve been able - from pre-parenthood days in sport, through to school committees, community organisations, and now as a volunteer with the charity Home-Start Aberdeen.

I am standing to be an MSP because I am tired and frustrated with the way the SNP government prioritises 'nice to have' projects over getting the essentials right. There is only so much I, as a councillor, can do, when so much of the council's budget and activities are dictated from Holyrood. Scotland needs fresh leadership and change with fairness at its heart, from people who genuinely care about their communities.

For media enquiries: media@scotlibdems.org.uk

Michael Turvey is a father of three, an oil and gas worker with 19 years experience, a volunteer and president of a local charity. He is passionate about the energy industry, empowering young people and improving lives in the North East.

His priorities for the Scottish Parliament are:
- delivering more effective NHS and care services,
- reducing the cost of living,
- supporting our oil and gas workers,
- bringing Scottish education back to its best; and
- fairer funding for the North East.

Michael has recently helped re-write the Liberal Democrats policy on Oil and Gas Taxation to support a long term, predictable regime that supports investment.

In Spring 2026, he helped pass policy motions supporting redeployment of health resources to preventative medicine and to care services to release hospital beds.  The motion also opposed the SNP’s GP “walk-in centres” which will draw finite resource away from communities across the North East.

Michael has also lead calls and a policy change to support funding for local bridge maintenance.

Michael is the Liberal Democrat candidate for Aberdeen Donside and residents across the North East can also vote for him on their peach regional ballot.

For media enquiries: media@scotlibdems.org.uk

PRESS RELEASE – SCOTTISH BORDERS GREENS FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE	31st JANUARY 2024			 SCOTTISH GREENS ANNOUNCE CANDIDATE FOR DUMFRIESSHIRE, CLYDESDALE AND TWEEDDALE

✔	Former Holyrood and Council candidate Dominic Ashmole selected by membership. ✔	“Time to break with the past, build resilience in our communities, and face a difficult future with solidarity and compassion.” ✔	South Scotland campaign launch attended by co-leader Patrick Harvie and other Green MSPs.

The Scottish Greens have named their candidate for the constituency of Dumfriesshire, Clydesdale and Tweeddale in the upcoming UK General Election. Former healthcare technology project manager Dominic Ashmole, who stood previously in the Holyrood and Council elections, was selected following a ballot of party members throughout Dumfries & Galloway, South Lanarkshire and the Scottish Borders.

At a South Scotland campaign launch in Tweedbank last weekend, Mr Ashmole said:

“I’m grateful to party members for this new opportunity to represent the Greens. When I stood for the Council in 2022, our vote share trebled to more than 16%. I believe we can build on that momentum and send a serious message to Westminster.”

“Many voters have lost faith in the traditional parties. They don’t want a government that blames refugees and abandons climate action. Nor can they support others who continue promoting false hopes and a reckless growth agenda that has undermined the Earth’s capacity to sustain life.”

“Greens recognise that to live through these times, we must move beyond a global system that is fuelled by exploitation and consumerism, and controlled by the super-rich.”

“Last election, the Conservatives only narrowly beat the SNP here, and voters might assume it’s still a two horse race. But both of those parties have massively lost support since then.”

“So I'm asking voters to back a dark, Green horse. Let's shock the system that is failing us. Let's make this election about us setting out on a new path. Because we will need to turn to each other as we live through the difficult times the political elites have led us into.”

“A vote for the Greens is for the courage to break with the past, to build resilience in our communities, and to face a difficult future with solidarity and compassion.”

Mr Ashmole was speaking at a South Scotland campaign launch attended by Scottish Greens Co-Leader Patrick Harvie MSP, Maggie Chapman MSP (Justice, Equality, Human Rights and Economy), and Ariane Burgess MSP (Communities, Land Reform, Housing and Rural Affairs).

party_description_text: Scottish Alliance for Democracy and Freedom
statement_to_voters: For too long the establishment parties of the LibLabCon SNP and Greens and delivered their top down dictates to the people of Scotland which bear no resemblance to serving the interests of the Scottish people.  We believe that people have the right to freedom of expression, to work hard and keep the majority of their own money rather than politicians wasting it on vanity projects and globalist net zero green agendas which do nothing to help the local community. That is why I am standing for the Hamilton, Larkhall and Stonehouse constituency and the South of Scotland List with the Alliance for Democracy and Freedom. If elected I will work hard to hold elected representatives to account and deliver for the people of Scotland.

My name is Senga Beresford, a businesswoman with over 25 years of experience in small business management.

Having lived in Dumfries and Galloway all my life, I care deeply about our communities and the future of working families across Scotland.

I entered politics because I’m tired of ordinary people being ignored and let down by those in power. I want to change that. I believe in lower taxes, putting more money in the pockets of working people, and creating a system that truly supports those who contribute. I want better education for all children, stronger support for young people, and the return of meaningful apprenticeships.

I strongly support our farmers, who are vital to our rural economy and food security, and I believe they must be backed to succeed.

I stand for fairness, free speech, and opportunity for all. As a married mother of four, everything I do is driven by a determination to build a better, more secure future for the next generation.

Why I’m standing

Having campaigned across Midlothian South, Tweeddale and Lauderdale, I have spoken to many  voters who tell me the same things: difficulty seeing a GP, roads full of potholes, inadequate public transport, too few opportunities for young people, and pensioners waiting too long for the care and services they rely on.

The SNP government has failed Scotland. Economic and social decline has become normalised and our communities deserve better.

It doesn’t have to be this way.

Scotland needs a government focused on delivery and on doing the right thing for the people we seek to represent. That is why I am standing as a candidate in this election.

My ambition is to be part of the next Scottish Labour Government that restores trust in politics, invests in the NHS, improves educational standards and creates real opportunities for young people across the country.

I know first-hand the difference a Labour government can make. Labour policies gave my family security and gave me the opportunity to go to university and build a career as an economist. I want to ensure everyone has the same life chances I had.

This election is about Scotland. It is about investment, jobs and housing. It is about getting Scotland moving in the right direction. After 18 years of SNP failure, I want to play my part in turning Scotland around. In May, I want to see a positive Scotland emerge. To do that, I need your support, and I ask you to consider giving me your vote for the constituency and a Labour vote on the list.

Please get in touch or fill out the survey at survey.labour.org.uk/mstl

Born in Dailly, the son of a coal miner and the youngest of nine children, I was educated at Carrick Academy in Maybole.I joined the Metropolitan Police as a Cadet at 16 and had a successful Police career, becoming a Detective Inspector at the age of 28 serving with the force from 1972 to 1987. During that time I was a uniform response officer as a constable, sergeant and inspector. I also served as a detective in the rank of constable, sergeant and inspector, specialising in the investigation of the most serious crimes of rape, robbery and murder.Returning to Ayrshire, I studied for a professional qualification in HR and became a Personnel and Training Executive with a major local employer for over 10 years with a further career as a professional Mediator successfully building consensus and resolving conflict.I am a former SNP Councillor in South Ayrshire and, most recently, have been Member of Parliament for Ayr, Carrick and Cumnock since 2019.

My family has lived in East Lothian for generations. I was born on a hill sheep farm in the Highlands and have lived in Dunbar for nearly 6 years with my wife and our two kids, who go to local schools. I coach and referee at Dunbar mini rugby and volunteer in projects working for children in the care system. I want to be your MP because I deeply care about our community and believe in taking real action to make our lives better.

For most of the last fifteen years, I’ve been the CEO of Who Cares? Scotland. Where I fought for children in care who have been dealt the worst hand. Under my leadership we didn’t just talk about change—we made it happen. We showed that with care and action, we can transform lives. I’ve spent my life making a difference, from promoting democracy in Lithuania to tackling poverty in India and Ghana and building peace across former-Yugoslavia.

I’ve seen firsthand what happens when people fall through the cracks through no fault of their own—it’s unnecessary and unacceptable.

I want to be your MP, as I’ve seen too many politicians make empty promises, but they mostly get in the way instead of helping us thrive. The Liberal Democrats are different. We are fundamentally liberal. We respect people and will empower you to make a difference in your lives.

We have a local growing economy, but successive governments just interfere and make it harder for our businesses to thrive. We need policies that support growth without getting in the way. Small businesses are the backbone of our community, and they need fewer obstacles and more support.

Local green energy successes like the Berwick Bank wind farm should do more to benefit the local communities directly.

Schools should have the freedom to innovate and help our children succeed. Our healthcare system must be accessible and local, especially for end-of-life care. It’s unacceptable that community hospitals in Dunbar and North Berwick are closing, and this must be urgently addressed.

As your MP, I will deliver results. I’ve got a proven track record. I’ve delivered progressive solutions on budget and on time, showing that experience and passion can make a real difference.

It’s time we had quality people in the House of Commons, people who are competent and effective. I care about East Lothian and its people. I care about East Lothian’s stunning environment. I care about this constituency and will represent you faithfully. Let’s take action together.

A vote for me is a vote for the truth and for what is right. My mother always told me to think for myself and tell the truth though the skies fall. If elected, I will do everything I can to make Scotland a more free place to live. More individual liberty and personal freedoms. No more laws, no more taxes, no more expansions of state power. If you know you're right but you can't win, you should fight anyway. That's why I'm a Libertarian.

As an engineer I am trained to solve problems, not just to complain about them. So when I heard about the Family Party a few years ago, I was naturally drawn to its focus on evidence of what works best for society in general, and children in particular, rather than on fashionable but untested ideology.

My professional work entails helping diverse stakeholders from government, the private sector and civil society work together to better understand and address seemingly intractable problems related to the planning and delivery of public infrastructure.

To my mind, this approach based on transparency and participation is exactly how the Scottish Family Party is, uniquely, seeking to fix the foundations of Scottish society.

I was born and raised in Glasgow and in 2005 came to work in the Borders on a 6 month contract in IT. I liked it so much I moved here permanently in 2010.

My background is in newspaper advertising and IT. I have worked in the Borders now for almost 20 years, latterly supporting schools IT in the area and concentrating on Galashiels primaries up till 2016. I am currently still working in IT for a local company.

I am living and working in the Borders as I love the people here. They are very friendly and community minded with good family values. I particularly enjoy local traditions, including the common ridings which are a great and unique feature of this beautiful area.

Why I am standing for Reform

I joined Reform as I felt none of the current parties were working in the interest of the public and were just coming up with the same old ideas and policies. Nor were they supporting traditional British and Scottish values. I was particularly annoyed at the neglect of the farming industry by successive governments. I feel it is so important that we produce our own food. Everyone needs to eat!!

What Reform can do for the Borders

Net Zero policies and too much red tape has damaged our farming industry. Reform wants farmers with farms under 500 Ha to be able to manage their own land the way they know best. We also want to encourage more young people into farming.

There is a severe lack of affordable housing for Borderers. Local housing associations are under increasing pressure from people looking for homes. I would like to help young people remain in the rural areas in which they grew up by encouraging them to take up a trade to help build and restore homes as well as build their own future and that of the Borders. Reform UK Scotland’s small business policies will encourage new entrepreneurs to help regenerate Border Businesses and generate more jobs.

The NHS is the biggest employer in the Borders but there is a severe shortage of medical staff. Reform will bring people back into NHS frontline medicine by putting them on zero basic rate tax for three years, and writing off their student loans after 10 years of NHS service or pro rata depending on length of service.

Hello my name is Michael Gregori and I’m standing as your Scottish Liberal Democrat candidate because I believe our community deserves better than the tired politics we’ve been left with. For too long, the SNP and the Conservatives have let people down — neglecting local services, centralising decision-making, and failing to deliver the change Scotland needs.
Labour themselves has shown through their Westminster Party that they can’t be trusted by scandals and their flip flop antics on important policy.
The Liberal Democrats offer something different: a positive, practical vision for Kilmarnock and Irvine Valley. We will put fairness, opportunity, and community at the heart of everything we do. That means fighting for better healthcare close to home, supporting our schools and young people, tackling the climate crisis with real action, and ensuring local voices are heard in every decision that affects us.
I’m committed to being a strong, accessible representative who listens and acts. I will always put people before party politics, and I will work tirelessly to improve the lives of everyone in our constituency.
It’s time for fresh leadership. It’s time to move beyond SNP excuses and Tory neglect. It’s time for a fairer, greener, and more hopeful future with the Scottish Liberal Democrats.

Freedom • Family • Nation.  The Heritage Party/Scottish Heritage Party stand for

• No to mandatory vaccines  • No to ULEZ/LEZ  • No to mass illegal immigration  • No to indoctrination of children  • Yes to Keep the culture of the United Kingdom • Yes to allowing our Farmers to Farm their land • Yes to giving our Fishermen their Fishing rights back • Yes to  liveable State Pension  • Yes to a better education system

We stand for traditional Family values!

Barbra Harvie lives in Selkirk with her husband and younger son.

She is a semi-retired lecturer in ecology, countryside management and environmental science at SRUC and the University of Edinburgh.

Originally from rural Aberdeenshire, Barbra is familiar with the everyday challenges that come with living in isolated rural communities. She cares about the area where her family have chosen to live and also the issues facing the local people of Selkirkshire.

She is passionate about grass roots politics, local businesses and employment, transport and connecting communities, renewables, social justice, land reform and the day-to-day decision-making that affects everyone’s lives.

Barbra has been politically active since her teens, supporting a wide range of community campaigns. She joined the Scottish Green Party in 2014 and is now co-convenor of Scottish Borders Greens.

favourite_biscuit: Chocolate covered ginger from Border Biscuits
statement_to_voters:
INDEPENDENCE
Nothing Else! Nothing Less!
I believe in
Scotland
In the Energy and Power of its
PEOPLE
ITS
OIL, GAS, Wind & Wave Power
and the Vast Wealth of its LAND!
...BUT...
For too long
Scotland has been trapped
in a toxic and unequal union
...IT IS TIME...
To set her
Free from Westminster Rule
Free to take her rightful place on the
WORLD STAGE.
...FREE...
To be an Independent Country
And to rule Herself.
The Time is Now!

Our party of ordinary people is totally fed up with the professional politicians ignoring our needs and bending to the wishes of their own parties.

We have decided to take matters into our own hands and retrieve our own power from people who no longer represent our values.

This is my first time standing for election for any party. I have decided to do this to put more money in the pockets of hard-working Scots, to bring real change to Holyrood and to protect the Union from the SNP’s plans for another divisive referendum.

My name is Janice MacKay, I am 63 and I live in South Lanarkshire.

My career background was 30 plus years in financial services before moving to the retail industry for 7 years. Unlike some elected politicians I have first hand experience in “the real job environment” and can relate to the day to day challenges faced by people in the front line.

UKIP is the only mainstream party who can Save Britain with our pragmatic policies. UKIP offers a return to sensible low immigration levels, an end to gender confusion lessons in schools along with hate speech laws and guidelines which have eroded freedom of expression.

UKIP unequivocally supports the Union of Great Britain. Westminster is sovereign!
Abolish Devolution
I pledge to donate 40% of MSP salary to Scottish Veteran Charities if elected
Offer the people of Scotland a referendum on the death penalty
Protect free speech - speaking your mind is not a crime.

Neil MacKinnon was brought up in Peebles, he spent three years studying at the college of Textiles where I gained a chemistry degree and made many friends, moved away in 1991 but returned to Galashiels in 2001 where I have lived since. Neil worked as a research chemist for eight years after leaving Galashiels and for the last 20 years he has worked as a service engineer in the scientific sector. This background has given him a good understanding of the mechanisms behind climate change and the importance of swift action at local and national levels to address this, this led him to join the Scottish Green Party. Neil has three children who have been through the school system in Galashiels so he is aware of the need for a replacement for the Academy to be delivered as soon as possible, however he considers it important that this is done in the least environmentally impactful way, and also that the finished building is a zero emissions building or as close to it as possible. Neil plays bagpipes for Stow pipe band and is on the committee, playing at local festivals and further afield. He is very aware of pressure on local bands and organisations brought about from Covid disruption, he feels community bands require more support from local government, especially with regards to free tuition in schools, knowing bands like these give young people a sense of purpose and belonging, forging friendships and learning skills that will last them a lifetime. Neil wants to give something back to his local community, in a town and area that he loves. He has not been politically active all that long but is keen to make a difference, having seen the number of small local groups making a difference in their own ways, he is inspired by their efforts and would like to be in a position to help groups like these prosper and grow.

party_description_text: Scottish Alliance for Democracy and Freedom

statement_to_voters:
My name is Garry Campbell McClay, and I’m from Kilmarnock, Scotland.

I’ve spent my working life in the skilled trades and small business sector and currently work as a self-employed tradesman. Through my work I’ve seen first-hand the challenges faced by working people, families, and small businesses across Scotland
.
Outside of work I’ve always enjoyed being active in sport and community life, taking part in football, boxing, golf, wrestling, and the Highland Games to name a few. I’m also a father, which is one of the main reasons I care deeply about the future of our country and the opportunities the next generation will have.

My priorities are to recognise Scotland’s popular sovereignty so the people in the communities have a real voice in shaping the future of an independent Scotland. I want to protect Scotland’s identity and culture, strengthen our economy and infrastructure, and tackle the serious issues affecting healthcare, education, and the justice system. I also believe in supporting the farming and energy sector so Scotland can become more self-sufficient and reduce everyday costs without being dependent on other countries.

I’m now standing for Alliance to Liberate Scotland in the Kilmarnock and Irvine Valley constituency on May 7th 2026 to be a voice for local people and help bring the change our constituency needs to lead by example.

The party allows me to stay self funded and have my independent views with no political party whip apart from that we all unite for Scottish independence - nothing else, nothing less. This is why I decided to join ATLS as we can't make the changes we need without having full control of our own country 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿

I’m not a career politician. I spent years as a diplomat, working across four continents in three languages. I always put in the hours behind the scenes to do the job properly. That’s the same commitment I’d bring to representing this area. And then, home called. We came back. And after working for our country overseas, I want to represent our home in Holyrood.

My politics is rooted in my faith and community. I helped set up our church’s food bank, campaigned for affordable housing as a community organiser (we secured 115 co-op homes), and volunteered in a night shelter. One guest asked to be woken at 5am to get to work. That stuck with me. A job—and still homeless. That crystallised a lot.

And with Labour, the clue’s in the name. Work should be fair and dignified. That’s why I’m proud the UK Labour Government is pushing the Employment Rights Bill.

But Labour’s also the party of social justice. I don’t want to just patch things up with food banks and shelters. I want to build a society where they’re not needed in the first place. Like every member, I want to make those things unnecessary.

As a Cooperative Party member too, I believe in real community power. From energy to land, we should be expanding community ownership—and making sure community benefit is felt widely and fairly.

The Scottish Family Party is only about 3 years old and was started because of the way family and parents are being sidelined in the care and education of children.
On the Independence issue, we are neutral, with candidates and members who are for and against. Dorothy and I voted ‘Yes’ to Independence in the last referendum but we take the view that in this period of great uncertainty, now is not the time for a major constitutional restructuring and that a referendum vote should not take place in the next Parliament.

We prefer to concentrate on issues where Scotland has lost its way. Under the current administration Scotland has dropped to 35th in the World Educational rankings so we will argue in favour of exams, a greater focus on knowledge and understanding, improve discipline, and give parents the right to see what is being taught particularly in sex-education which currently contains content that most parents would consider to be grossly unacceptable.

We believe mums and dads are best placed and equipped to care for their children. State involvement should be reserved for the small minority of negligent parents and that the definition of child abuse should not be weakened (as is currently being considered) to include, for example, getting angry with your child.

We also believe that the police should not be used to enforce “Thought Control” by prosecuting people for what they say around the dinner table. Free speech in the public square, even if it is offensive to some, is necessary to preserve our democracy.
Please have a look at our website (scottishfamily.org) and our videos on Youtube which clearly describe the slippery slope we are on.

"I am proud to have been selected to stand in the upcoming by-election in Hamilton, Larkhall and Stonehouse.

“I will spend the weeks ahead speaking to as many people as possible across the constituency, to discuss their hopes for their children and their future, the strength and value of their communities and the everyday challenges that they face.

“Every vote for the Scottish Greens will be a positive vote for a fairer and greener Scotland and a brighter future for Hamilton, Larkhall and Stonehouse.

"If we are to build a truly just and green future we need to empower every town and village and ensure that no one is left behind."

I am standing as a candidate to give something back to the community which shaped my early life.

If elected my aim would be to improve local public services as an absolute priority.

Born and educated in Kilmarnock, I have always maintained strong ties to the area, where much of my family and many friends still live. Since retiring, I have spent more time in the constituency.

I joined Reform UK in December 2024 and bring a professional background in law, having graduated with honours from the University of Glasgow, qualified as a solicitor, and spent over 20 years as a principal in private practice.

Alongside my career I have volunteered internationally teaching English as a foreign language and also been a volunteer for my local community council.

Aisha was the first to attend University in her family and graduated from the University of West of Scotland in 1996.

She had a successful career in Finance and now runs her own business.

Aisha first joined the Scottish Liberal Democrats in 2013 and since then has become a passionate activist, campaigning in many elections across the UK as well as being a Parliamentary candidate.

Aisha has been a Childrens' Panel member and was an unpaid carer for her mother through a number of serious illnesses for many years. She has been active in a number of projects & campaigns supporting unpaid carers, welfare for the elderly, Human Rights, diversity and inequality.

A daughter of immigrant parents, she has a distinctive voice and set of life experiences that lend well to her brief.

My parents taught me the importance of paying attention to detail, to question everything, and to learn from mistakes and experiences. They instilled in me values of respect, fairness, politeness, tolerance and honesty.

I attended Mainholm Academy for 6 years which I enjoyed immensely.  I spent the first part of my career in the Navy serving on frigates and later submarines. I then went on to spend 20 years in the offshore oil and gas sector developing and maintaining subsea infrastructure aboard various vessels. This is one of the reasons I’m keen on promoting Reform UK policies for more technical jobs and to help businesses because they all provide valued jobs and are good for the economy, benefitting us all.

I joined Reform UK as I thought mainstream parties had gone off the rails and lost sight of what most of us are feeling and are no longer representing our views.  Reform UK will deal with mad net zero policies which are too stringent, stifling growth, making us less competitive and causing prices to rocket upwards. Immigration is out of control in an already full country with housing shortages. There are NHS concerns with over-the-top management and waste and an inability to retain staff including carers. We need more affordable housing to ease rents and waiting lists, more Police to police without political interference and fair justice, not this two-tier system which we have all seen emerging.

Help me to help you. Vote Reform UK on Thursday 16 October 2025 and let’s bring common sense back into politics.

I’m Davy Russell, and I’m proud to be standing as Scottish Labour’s candidate in the upcoming by-election for Hamilton, Stonehouse and Larkhall.
I’ve lived here my whole life. I’m a dad – and a granddad – and like many of you, I care deeply about the future of our community.

I’m not a politician. I’ve never stood for election before. But I’m standing in this by-election because this is where I was born, where I live, and where I’ve raised my family. For me, it’s the best place in the world. I want to be a champion in parliament for this place and the people I’ve grown up with and lived alongside my entire life.

I was born in Quarter, and went to school in Hamilton. I started my career as a roads trainee in 1980 and ended up as a director at a local council. I’ve worked my entire life to improve the services that we all rely on. Today, I support people with disabilities, help run a local hospice, and I’m honoured to represent His Majesty as Deputy Lord Lieutenant in Lanarkshire.

Like you, I’ve had enough of the way things are going with the SNP. I’m standing for one reason: to get things done for our community. We need to fix our NHS – too many people have had to wait too long for the care they deserve. We need to tackle the SNP’s housing crisis – so families here aren’t stuck in damp flats or waiting years for a home they can barely afford. And we need to breathe life back into our town centres – investment in the places we all use and used to be proud of. After 18 years, the SNP are out of ideas and out of touch. If they had a plan to fix things here we’d have seen it by now. This by-election is a chance for all of us to send the SNP a message: enough is enough.

I’ll always fight for our communities and put Hamilton, Larkhall and Stonehouse first.

I’m ready to work for you – and fight for you – every single day.

My name is Andrew Scott and I’m your candidate for the Scottish Parliament 2026 election for the Reform UK party.

I want to tackle issues such as high taxes, reforming the NHS and scrapping net zero.

I currently work as a postperson and I see the challenges and struggles daily going around my post duty and I would like to change and improve that.

I am standing for Reform UK party as they offer real hope and vision to transform our society through things like lower income taxes, reforming our health care, bringing our education to world class again and scrapping net zero saving you money on your energy bills.

As a resident of Stranraer and the Rhins I am determined to restore our local area to the great economic hub it once was bringing employment and services that the area is crying out for. I belong to the Scottish Heritage Party who from the outset of the pandemic rejected all notion of shutting down the economy and in effect enforcing through fear risky medical interventions that are now being proven to have caused injury and even death.

As your elected representative I will be a champion for the people of this area from the young mum looking to have her baby in a local hospital rather than giving birth in an ambulance on the way to Dumfries to the Pensioner struggling to heat the house on the State Pension alone. As a 71 year old State Pension only Pensioner, I know what it is like to try and balance the books and on council and in government I will ask passionately how a State Pensioner in Iceland receives equivalent to £2,997, and in Norway £2,447 whilst in Scotland only £782. monthly. By giving a living pension, pensioners will spend more with local businesses, for example hospitality, so giving pensioners a pittance costs society as a whole! I am determined to put this right!

I realise Iceland and Norway have invested more passionately with infrastructure, which Stranraer and the Rhins desperately need. Our road and rail services have sadly become  outdated through neglect from a far off council and government offices of unelected officials, so I will fight for our area to become an vibrant area again using our oil and natural gas resources rejecting the nonsense of un-environmentally wind turbines, the nonsense of enforced lockdowns with risky medical interventions bringing back to our area common sense initiatives that go beyond constant feasibility studies to positive action.

I will look to work with fellow councillors and government officials to reach these objectives looking to bring healing rather than divisions in society. We need to stand together in an uncertain world, my family having experienced losing all we had with the Northern Ireland troubles and it is my heart for Stranraer and the Rhins to have a community that stands together in bringing about not only the economic wealth but also the society stability and care for each other a successful nation always has at its heart.

I look forward to serving you who have elected me for many years to come.

A message from Denise Sommerville

In this election, you have a choice—not just between parties, but about the kind of representation you want.

You have two votes. One for a constituency candidate, and one for the regional list. And with both of those votes, you can choose to back someone who is not tied to party politics.

I am standing as an independent candidate for Ayr, and on the South Scotland regional list, because I believe our communities deserve a voice that answers to people—not parties.

For too long, decisions have been shaped by party lines, internal agendas, and systems that feel distant from everyday life. I’m standing to change that.

I come from this community. I understand the pressures families are under—the housing crisis, the impact of poor decisions and wasted public money, and the reality of a public health system struggling to respond to addiction.

My commitment is simple:
	•	Put Ayr, Prestwick and Troon first
	•	Challenge waste and demand accountability
	•	Push for real, joined-up solutions to the addiction crisis

If you choose to vote for me, you are choosing independent representation—someone who will speak up, question decisions, and stay accountable to you.

This election, you don’t have to follow party lines.

You can choose a representative who stands with you.

Denise Sommerville
Independent Candidate – Ayr Constituency & South Scotland Region

Candidate Statement Tracey Warman
I live in Dumfries and Galloway with my partner and can think of nowhere I would rather
be than in this corner of the world we call home.
I volunteer in our local community shop once a week, which is hugely satisfying and is a
great way of getting to know the locals as well as visitors to our area. It is really the hub of
local life . This year I was also elected to serve on the Community Council and am very
much looking forward to see what avenues this may take me down.
I worked in finance for 33 years, and took early retirement in 2019. I now spend my time
as a relief dairy worker, concentrating mainly on calf rearing at farms across the area. I en-
joy going to the agricultural shows throughout the summer.
I also run an Airbnb house share. We have been very lucky to have met some lovely peo-
ple from across the world by doing this.
I enjoy swimming in the sea and in 2023 was one of the founding members of a women’s
swimming group. We meet regularly for swims throughout the year. Our group has been a
great way of forging friendships through a shared joy of being in the water. I have also al-
ways enjoyed long distance running and have completed marathons and half marathons
across the UK, Europe and Africa
I have voted Liberal since the age of 18 and have been a member of the Liberal Democ-
rats since 2011and joined the Scottish Liberal Democrats in 2017.
My aim is to give a voice to the local communities in West Dumfriesshire and Galloway.
As a resident of Galloway, I feel I have gained a good understanding of the community in
which I live and now I want to listen to your views, concerns and needs - to learn what is
important to you and to bring your views to the attention of those that can get your voice
heard. The Liberal Democrats, in their various guises and long history have been instru-
mental in empowering local communities through a community approach, which is so of-
ten ignored by the other parties.
As a Liberal Democrat, I believe in the liberal qualities of fairness and equality

I joined the Scottish Green Party after the independence referendum, and previously took part as a candidate in the local council elections. I, like many others, have been horrified to see the lack of progress on social, environmental and climate action from the Westminster government for the past 10+ years and wanted to ensure that voters in West Dunbartonshire have the option to Vote Green. With more people than ever facing conditions of poverty and inequality, nature continuing to be depleted and undervalued, and new oil and gas licences being issued, it’s time to up the pressure. I have worked for a national conservation organisation for almost 20 years. I strongly believe in a more inclusive and equal society, where we support our communities, and want to see more urgent actions taken to prevent climate catastrophe and nature breakdown, not just in our country but globally. Vote like your future depends on it.

Ross was born in 1981 and grew up in Irvine in the Irvine east ward, He Has worked in Health and Social care for over 20 years. Along with being dyslexic this makes Him very passionate and knowledgeable about disability rights. This is the second time they are standing in Irvine east so are keen to build on last time around. Ross will work to give pupils, parents and teachers a larger say on how schools are run and how the money is spent. He will champion for better conditions for health and social care staff. Also improving financial security for unpaid carers He will put local communities at the heart of planning, prioritising their needs over those of big developers and protecting precious green and open space. Ross will ensure that Council land is managed to adapt to and mitigate climate change in a way that delivers nature-rich places, benefiting wildlife and people. Ross will also Encourage shops and post offices in local high streets, rather than chain stores or out-of-town development. He will Support grass-roots sporting activities, such as running clubs Ross encourages you to Think Global, Act local and vote Scottish Greens 1st preference this election.

I will fight to defend individual rights and liberties from the predations of government and campaign to restore those rights already eroded, such as free speech, free association and property rights.

For too long the establishment parties of the LibLabCon, SNP and Greens and dictated to the people of Scotland in a top down manner the priorities of this parties and they do not serve the best interests of the people of Scotland. We believe in freedom of expression, to have a job or a business, to work hard and earn a good wage and keep the majority of your earnings rather than handing it to government who waste it on vanity projects and globalist net zero green agendas or similar. That is why I am standing in the Scottish Parliamentary elections to fight for public services par excellence to offer people choice to gain the best education, the best health care and the best location for living and working.

As the MP for North Ayrshire and Arran since 2015, I have championed a range of important causes on behalf of constituents.From the commencement of my first term I have been assiduous in assisting constituents, community groups and local businesses as an active and accessible MP.At Westminster, I have vocally supported the right of Scotland to choose its own independent future while successfully working cross party to secure two weeks paid bereavement leave for all parents who lose a child under the age of 18 years old and those who experience a stillbirth.I continue to campaign for pension justice for WASPI women who had their state pension age raised with little or no notice and I have been extremely vocal on a wide variety of animal welfare and consumer rights issues.Locally, I work in partnership with SNP led North Ayrshire Council and promote the SNP Government as it delivers better housing, schools, and the best NHS in the UK.I have held portfolios in the SNP Westminster group such as spokesperson for Levelling Up, Department for the Environment, Farming and Rural Affairs and most recently the Department of the Attorney General.My constituency focus means that I have campaigned locally on matters such as support with dementia, loneliness, debt support, energy bills and the cost-of- living crisis.Importantly, I jointly organised with the local SNP MSP a dozen employment fayres, five funding fayres and actively encouraged a 900 job, £1.4 billion green investment into my constituency.

Adam grew up in Bishopbriggs, where he now lives with his wife and young son. This is his community – and it’s where he’s raising his family today.

He started out working in theatre and the arts before moving into the charity sector, where he campaigned for people with cystic fibrosis to access life-saving medicines. Adam has also volunteered with organisations supporting children from disadvantaged backgrounds and worked with community groups helping young people at risk of becoming involved in the criminal justice system to turn their lives around.

Adam says, _“This area is my home. It’s where I grew up, and where I’m raising my family. Too many people here feel really let down - by the state of local roads and pavements, by having to wait months for treatment on the NHS and by how much of a struggle it is to afford energy bills and food in the supermarket._

_Many of them have also totally lost faith in anyone involved in politics to make a difference or do the things they promised to do. That’s why I’m working hard to improve things locally and speak to listen to as many people on the doorsteps as possible.”_

Adam’s experience in the charity and voluntary sectors has shown him that real change takes hard work and persistence. That’s why he has already knocked on thousands of doors across Strathkelvin & Bearsden – listening to people, hearing what matters most to them, and standing up for the changes they need.

_“More than anything, being dad to a wee boy motivates me to get elected and change things for the better. For too long, the country has been going in the wrong direction – we desperately need to turn things around.”_

party_description_text: Independent Green Voice - Organic Green Scotland

statement_to_voters: David Jacobsen is the Socialist Labour Party Genera Election candidate for Edinburgh North & Leith Constituency. David has 20 years experience as a healthcare worker in the NHS and is committed to a National Health service available to all at the time of need, on demand and free of all charges - including prescriptions, dental care, and eye care. The Socialist Labour Party wants all NHS workers to receive wages and terms and conditions that reflect the social importance of their jobs. Our objectives include the provision of NHS nursing homes free of charge for people who need them and care homes owned and operated by local authorities and not by private companies chosen by a centralised power controlled by the Scottish government. On 29th February 2024 the Scottish Parliament passed the National Care Service Bill. This allows Scottish ministers to transfer social care responsibility from local authorities. This could include adult and children's services as well as areas such as justice and social work. Mr Jacobsen urges consituents to stop the transfer of healthcare functions from the NHS to the new centralised National Care Service. Vote Socialist Labour on July 4th

Paul Mack is currently an elected councillor, serving the community of Paisley Southeast (Ward 6) on Renfrewshire Council.

My family have lived in Paisley since the Pope visited Dumbarton. My father literally and metaphorically built the Hunterhill Community Centre. We Macks have many faults, but ingratitude isn't one of them.

I owe this town everything. I couldn't look my neighbour in the eye if I didn't
stretch every sinew and every fibre of my being to prevent the closure of our
Sick Children's Unit (Ward 15, Royal Alexandra Hospital). Save one child and you save Humanity.

Ms Sturgeon and friends have an absolute fidelity to referenda and the sanctity
of the 'will of the people'. Send those who wish to close our most prized possession,
something that is genuinely revered in this town and has a world renowned reputation, which is about to be stolen from us, the dictum 'Save Ward 15'. Buyer's remorse?

Mack is the new Black.

Source: https://paul-mack.com/paul-mack

I am the Reform UK candidate for North Ayrshire and Arran constituency. I was born in Glasgow to a paediatric surgeon and a mathematics teacher. I studied at the High School of Glasgow and became an accountant and a working economist, with three master’s degrees in: International Commercial Law (LLM – University of Salford)); European Business (Euro MBA – University of Maastricht)); and Digital Transformation (MBA – University of Barcelona).  I am married with 3 children.

For the last 30 years, I have worked as an international economic development consultant and policy adviser for the European Union, World Bank, IMF, British Government Foreign and Commonwealth Office and others with assignments in more than 50 countries throughout the Middle East (Egypt, Jordan, Palestine, Syria, Lebanon and Yemen), Russia, Africa and Eastern Europe.

Notable assignments include:

Shipbuilding: Project leader for the privatisation of the Western Shipbuilding and Repair Yard JSC, Klaipeda, Lithuania; Project leader for restructuring of: Egyptian Ship Building and Repair Company, Alexandria; Bijela Shipyard, Montenegro and Karachi Shipyard, Pakistan.

Trade and Investment: Lead Adviser to the Ministry of Trade and Industry Kosovo (4 years), negotiating 4 free trade agreements and advising on trade and investment strategy.

Establishment of the Investment Promotion Agency and well as SME Development. Chief Accountant to the British Government Kosovo Rehabilitation Programme, with responsibility for the allocation of £180 million in 9 months to restore power supplies, hospitals, sanitary services and some 500 small projects.

On returning to my home in Scotland, I feel the need to be politically active as a force for change. I am known within the North Ayrshire area and live in Largs.

Key Priorities for Milngavie:  •Improving Transport links across EDC and reinstating the trains to Glasgow City every 15 min  • Enough school bus provision for all in the catchments  •Green Belt Protection  •Helping the local economy grow by supporting small businesses  •Sorting out the potholes throughout Milngavie ward •Improved communication between councillors and constituents

I have lived in North Ayrshire almost my whole life. I have benefited from excellent housing and had the advantage of a first class education as I grew up. I served an engineering apprenticeship on leaving school that provided me with the platform to further develop a career in teaching. My children and grandchildren also live in our community and I hope for a similarly positive future for them and for all the people of North Ayrshire & Arran. If you agree that much needs to be done to make that a reality I ask you to consider voting for me, James McDaid, the Socialist Labour Party candidate in the forthcoming election.

I am married with two children and three grandchildren. I am a former UNISON Local Government Branch Secretary and have worked in local government for over 20 years.

We are living through an ongoing cost of living crisis wreaking misery upon millions of people throughout Britain whilst at the same time the wealth of billionaires exponentially grows and the number of food banks increase.

The Socialist Labour Party policies deal with the injustices and inequalities that people currently face in our society.  We want to secure for the people a full return of all the wealth generated by our industries and services based on common and social ownership of the means of production, distribution and exchange.

There are those who would suggest that this idea is revolutionary, and they would be right. Compromise with crooks is no solution.  It requires the removal of the small elite who control Britains economy, employment, health service, education system, pensions, and social systems.  The mad obsession with the production of nuclear and conventional weapons of war, designed to destroy life, should be replaced by a commitment to save and improve the quality of life for people everywhere.

We are witnessing in real time a genocide of Palestinians in the first instance in Gaza but also through Israeli government policies in the West Bank. The murderous assault on innocents by Israeli armed forces is an atrocity opposed by the overwhelming majority of countries throughout the world, yet the British Government continues to aid and abet this atrocity supported by the Labour Party.  The SLP supports the Palestine right to a free and sovereign state unequivocally.

The Socialist Labour Party wants to see a world that is truly at peace, with liberty, justice and prosperity for all.

My professional experience spans 20 years in social care, street fundraising and horticulture. I currently work as office manager for Irene Campbell MP.

Each role has given me insights into the challenges faced by families, workers, and small businesses.

To win this campaign, we need to engage with people directly, on the doorsteps and in our communities. We need to connect with people, listen to their concerns, and fight for them every step of the way.

It’s time to have those difficult conversations to challenge the misinformation and division spread by those who do not have our community’s best interests at heart.

After many long years of SNP incompetence, we need change. We need a new direction.

Our community faces challenges that affect us all: health and economic inequality and a lack of opportunities for young people. This is our chance to make things happen.

Together, we can send a clear message - Cunninghame North deserves better than the SNP. With Anas Sarwar as First Minister and myself in Cunninghame North we can build a fairer, stronger Scotland.

It is a privilege to be the Labour candidate for Renfrewshire North &  Cardonald, standing for election to the Scottish Parliament. As a retired consultant surgeon at the Royal Alexandra Hospital in Paisley with over 27 years of experience, I know the NHS in Scotland inside out — and I know that only Labour, the party of the NHS, can fix it.

After 19 years of SNP government, we have widespread failure in public services across the NHS, education, local government, transport and infrastructure.  The people of Scotland, and our economy, cannot flourish in such an environment. The SNP are solely responsible. We must strongly state that and make the case for change.

Gary Mulvaney was elected for his fourth term in 2017 and leads the eight member group of Conservatives on Argyll & Bute Council.

In May 2013, Conservative councillors co-founded The Argyll, Lomond, and Islands Group (TALIG) and following the 2017 elections, they entered into Administration as part of this group.

Gary Mulvaney was elected as Depute Leader of the council and is the Policy Lead for Strategic Finance and Capital Regeneration. He sits on Policy & Resources and Development & Infrastructures committees.

He is principal political lead for the Helensburgh Waterfront Development.

He is a qualified accountant, and a fellow of the Association of Chartered Certified Accountants.  His career has covered local government, marketing, and extensive experience in the motor trade. He currently is Director, and Centre Principal, of Helensburgh Toyota, having previously been the dealership's Financial Director.

Gary was born in 1968.  His home town is Helensburgh, where he lives with his wife Janet and her daughter Sammy-Jo.

Gary is a former Westminster parliamentary candidate, having contested the seat in 2017 and 2010. In 2017, the Conservatives came within 1300 votes of taking the seat from the SNP. When he stood in the 2010 General he came second, with Liberal Democrat MP Alan Reid’s majority squeezed to just over three thousand votes.

During the 2010 campaign, the local news website ForArgyll.com said that, if elected to Westminster, Gary would be "a formidable operator".  Describing him as "A natural strategist and a clear thinker...", the website said "...he has the added advantage of experience".  The writer continued "If it is possible to be open-minded and interested in change while not being troubled by uncertainties or indecision, this is who he is”.

Outside business and politics, Gary was Business Manager of the Glasgow-based Apollo Players theatre group and made his stage debut in 2010 at the Kings Theatre, Glasgow, and in 2012 he played in Annie at the Glasgow Pavilion theatre.

Malcolm Offord is the Reform UK candidate for Inverclyde, bringing experience from both business and government to his campaign. He is standing to offer a clear alternative to the policies of the established parties, with a focus on practical, common-sense solutions that put local people first.

He is campaigning for lower taxes, economic growth, and an end to policies that drive up the cost of living. Malcolm is committed to representing the people of Inverclyde, supporting local businesses, and ensuring that the area is no longer overlooked by those in power.

- Lower taxes to help working people keep more of what they earn
- End costly Net Zero measures that push up bills
- Stand up for free speech and common-sense policies
- Back local businesses and create real economic growth

I've worked in Eastwood for over a decade and live here with my young family. Like many families in East Ren, I moved here because of the opportunities it offers for my two girls.

Eastwood is an amazing place to raise children because of our beautiful green spaces, thriving communities and excellent schools, but our area cannot take another five years of cuts to local budgets. That's why I'm standing as Labour's candidate in the Scottish Parliament election next year.

The first campaign I ran in Eastwood was a decade ago, to save the local 394 bus service, which you can still see serving our communities today. I have a proven record of delivering for these communities already, and want to do more as your MSP.

party_description_text: Alliance to Liberate Scotland Scotland's Choice
statement_to_voters:
If elected I will pursue Independence & nothing less at Holyrood. By setting deadline on the SNP government, to hold the independence ballot in 2026. No excuses & no asking London for permission.Only after leaving the UK, can we build new infrastructure that invests the enormous collateral of our national energy resources.

I intend to start that transformation, by creating a National Energy Company, to provide domestic power & gas at cost price. This would create 1000s of long term careers, for our educated young generation.

I also want my Scottish Govt to invest in a National Wind/Solar/Electric civilian shipbuilding company. This would offer 1000s of new shipbuilding careers, outside of UK warship construction.

To ensure fast, efficient transport of people & goods, I support totally resurfacing every km of Scotland's road infrastructure. This would require the creation of a PERMANENT Road Replacement & Maintenance Agency, creating 1000s of lifelong careers.

Scotland also lacks both an oil refinery & a steelworks / rolling mill - both vital to our future, indigenous wind power industry. Giving yet more 1000s of skilled jobs for our new generation.

Our country is desperately in need of complete modernization & developing  Self Sufficiency;  in food, beverages, fishing, farming, tourism, railways, cycling, hill walking, water sports, historical buildings, manufacturing & natural energy resources.

Ross Stalker first joined the Liberal Democrats in 2006 while studying Biological Sciences at Edinburgh University. He made Renfrew his home in 2010 and went on to obtain a Master's Degree in Biotechnology at the University of the West of Scotland in 2014. With his background as a biologist, he understands the urgency of the climate emergency and he firmly believes in protecting and enhancing Scotland's natural heritage.

Ross has been employed for several years as a Campaign Organiser for the party, throughout three parliamentary elections, the EU referendum and the 2017 local elections, which has given him extensive experience in engaging with voters and winning political campaigns. He has also served for over a decade on the Scottish Liberal Democrat Conference Committee, which has given him experience in a broad range of policy areas.

He is particularly concerned with improving mental health services - especially outpatient services which he feels are being left behind in the new Health and Social Care Partnership model - and also wants to raise awareness and improve diagnosis of adult ADHD, having been diagnosed himself in his late 20s. He is worried about the impact of Brexit on the economy and public services, and passionate about achieving a more socially just Scotland.

Ross was the Liberal Democrat candidate in Paisley & Renfrewshire North in 2019, where he outperformed the party's national swing to more than double the local vote share.

For too long the establishment parties of the LibLabCon, SNP and Greens and dictated to the people of Scotland in a top down manner the priorities of this parties and they do not serve the best interests of the people of Scotland. We believe in freedom of expression, to have a job or a business, to work hard and earn a good wage and keep the majority of your earnings rather than handing it to government who waste it on vanity projects and globalist net zero green agendas or similar. That is why I am standing in the Scottish Parliamentary elections to fight for public services par excellence to offer people choice to gain the best education, the best health care and the best location for living and working.

party_description_text: Independence for Scotland Party, Direct Democracy.
statement_to_voters:
A Scotland Truly Free
Power Back to the People

I am standing for one reason- to return the power of Scotland into the hands of its rightful owners ….. you.

Uncompromising Independence
The time for half-measures is over. I stand to immediately  liberate  Scotland. We are a nation with the resources, the talent, and the right to self determination. I will not ask for permission to be free .
I will work to assert our sovereignty and build a modern, independent state that serves its people, not Westminster.

Equality as a Foundation
A free Scotland must be a fair Scotland. I am a fierce advocate for women’s rights and disability rights. We will dismantle the barriers that exclude and marginalize. From economic security to physical accessibility and bodily autonomy, my mission is to ensure that every citizen can live with dignity, safety, and full participation in our society.

Power Through Direct Democracy
I don’t want your vote just to represent you ,I want to empower you. I advocate for direct democracy, where the most important decisions are made by the people through citizen assemblies and national referendums. No more backroom deals , just transparent, people led governance.

Protecting Our Sovereignty: No to Freeports
I stand firmly against the implementation of Freeports. These are corporate playgrounds that threaten our workers rights, bypass environmental standards, and erode our national tax base. Scotland’s land and industry are not for sale to the highest corporate bidder.

My commitment  is a clear vision of a liberated, democratic, and inclusive Scotland.

On election day, vote for a candidate who won’t compromise. Vote for independence. Vote for rights. Vote for yourself.

Vote Colette Walker
Let’s win our future.
